How Governor Wes Moore Came Home to Baltimore We were honored to welcome Maryland Governor Wes Moore as our keynote speaker. His story of perseverance demonstrates what happens when discipline, service, and accountability compound over time. | | ---|---|--- Moore spoke about growing up between Baltimore and the Bronx and how, as an adult, he realized Maryland was the place that felt like his true home. Before he was governor and earned his many accolades, Moore faced real hardship. When he was three years old, his father died of acute epiglottitis -- a rare but treatable illness -- after being turned away from a hospital for care. That early loss shaped his understanding of how broken systems can devastate families, and why public service became a personal calling. By the age of eleven, he was drifting off course and had felt the sting of handcuffs on his wrists when he was arrested for spraying walls with graffiti. His mother made the hardest decision a parent can make: sending her son to Valley Forge Military Academy -- not as punishment, but as intervention. That decision changed the trajectory of his life. At Valley Forge, Moore learned that discipline could become dignity, and that teams -- more than individuals -- can carry people through difficult times and change the outcome of a life. Those lessons followed him to Johns Hopkins, where he played football and studied international relations and economics, and then to Oxford as a Rhodes Scholar. They also followed him into the Army. Moore is a three-time Army volunteer. He raised his hand to serve as a teenager, volunteered for airborne school at Fort Benning, completed five parachute jumps, one carrying 30 pounds of gear, and another in the dark of night, and then volunteered to deploy with the elite 82nd Airborne 'All American' Division to Afghanistan. Over a 16-year career in the Army and Army Reserve, he served as a paratrooper and civil affairs officer, leading soldiers in combat zones and working on reconciliation efforts with local communities. He earned multiple commendations, including the National Defense Service Medal, the Afghanistan Campaign Medal, and the Bronze Star. Today, at 47, Moore is one of the youngest governors in the nation, a bestselling author, and a leader focused on restoring trust in institutions and opportunity for families. Moore's military mindset became visible to the nation in March 2024, when the Francis Scott Key Bridge collapsed in Baltimore, killing six construction workers in the dark of night and disrupting one of the country's most important ports. In a moment of shock, Moore moved immediately into emergency mode, coordinating response and acting with the calm urgency of someone trained to manage an unfolding crisis. It was the same logistical, mission-driven mindset forged years earlier. That philosophy now shapes how he governs. Moore defines the most solemn duty of government simply: to keep families safe, physically and economically. It's why he's focused on building what he calls "lighthouse industries" in Maryland -- cybersecurity, technology, and advanced manufacturing -- industries that create dignity through work and offer families a stable future. subject: The media is finally noticing how bad online gambling is

The public is starting to notice just how bad companies like FanDuel and DraftKings are.

| |

# [The media is finally noticing how bad online gambling is](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=181827868&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxODI3ODY4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU5NzgxMTcsImV4cCI6MTc2ODU3MDExNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.p1VkiKigmK1p9huqZm4y5BkIJEiEusYdddljtmBzTOQ)
### The public is starting to notice just how bad companies like FanDuel and DraftKings are.
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---
| Dec 17
---
| [](https://substack.com/@bensamuels)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=181827868&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxODI3ODY4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2NTk3ODExNywiZXhwIjoxNzY4NTcwMTE3LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMjQ0MDM5OCIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.xd_AES0dd1AQg8gxqBWu3-qoWXjTa4S56Ds8ULJFGe8&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)

_This is a follow-up to what I wrote two weeks ago. And because it 's not a partisan issue, there's a chance for Democrats and Republicans to come together to address this growing crisis. In case you missed it, here's what I wrote:_
[](https://substack.com/redirect/8371ee00-fb60-4103-9062-6be138dac5a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)| | |
#### [It's (predatory) gamblin' time!](https://substack.com/redirect/8371ee00-fb60-4103-9062-6be138dac5a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
---
| | [Ben Samuels](https://substack.com/profile/12425779-ben-samuels)| | *
---|---|---
| Dec 3
| [| Read full story
---](https://substack.com/redirect/8371ee00-fb60-4103-9062-6be138dac5a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
I flew into the St. Louis airport a few nights ago and was immediately hit with more sports gambling ads.1
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4cb46f36-2538-418e-a899-bad7f29e9a68?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Because I am [extremely](https://substack.com/redirect/8371ee00-fb60-4103-9062-6be138dac5a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) [on](https://substack.com/redirect/2fc66657-5f72-4e51-91b0-d4743039dc84?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)-[record](https://substack.com/redirect/dbfe0d6d-b2f0-4ebe-9594-db3983623c8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) acknowledging that online sports gambling is bad, I have friends sending me these sorts of ads all the time now.2 They're inescapable.
But if you've been paying attention to the news lately, what you'll notice: a recent groundswell of coverage on the negative impact that sports gambling is having on communities, especially on boys and young men.
Public opinion is finally shifting, and I'm optimistic that in a few years there may be an opportunity to limit the damage that companies like FanDuel and DraftKings are causing.
Here's a roundup on what I've been reading on the topic.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTgxODI3ODY4LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzY1OTc4MTE3LCJleHAiOjIwODE1NTQxMTcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.Nb-Nd3ma7-03U_3WaGFsUZNzJBVh6hK-4d4YNW6OTGM?)
### Americans are increasingly skeptical of online gambling and sports gambling
_[The Washington Post](https://substack.com/redirect/e6132c89-5a23-417c-80bf-0dbc11c4d12c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ recently ran a poll that shows Americans' growing concerns about sports gambling. In May 2022, 23% of Americans said states allowing people to bet on sports was "a bad thing." As of earlier this month, that number has grown to 36%. (A plurality, 49%, say that it's "neither good nor bad.")
What's interesting: every group measured increasingly thinks that sports gambling is bad, relative to what they believed in 2022.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/e6132c89-5a23-417c-80bf-0dbc11c4d12c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: [Washington Post-University of Maryland poll](https://substack.com/redirect/5e06c0a4-8da7-42d1-a28b-fda656642dc5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
What I find most notable is that the people most exposed to this--the gamblers themselves and sports fans generally--are some of the people who've moved the most on this issue in the last 3.5 years. Today, relative to 2022:
* 6.7× more sports bettors agree that legal sports gambling is a bad thing.
* 2.8× more devoted sports fans agree that it's a bad thing.

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=181827868&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxODI3ODY4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU5NzgxMTcsImV4cCI6MTc2ODU3MDExNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.p1VkiKigmK1p9huqZm4y5BkIJEiEusYdddljtmBzTOQ)
Other polls find something similar. A [recent survey in Massachusetts](https://substack.com/redirect/d5a0d95d-ccc2-42b5-9cca-5dda9079fb69?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) finds that "56 percent of monthly gamblers in Massachusetts now believe that the harms of gambling outweigh the benefits."
### _[The Economist](https://substack.com/redirect/d3242d3c-963c-405c-af07-a3724e677135?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ : "College campuses have become a front line in America's sports-betting boom"
I'll start with a few quotes from _The Economist_ article:
* "The NCAA survey found that 16% of 18-to-22-year-olds engage in problematic sports gambling." ([Here's the underlying data](https://substack.com/redirect/649119a3-50fe-455a-97b1-004a02efc881?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).) For additional context, in most states, it's not even legal for 18-year-olds to gamble.3
* "At Pennsylvania State University, which has 64,000 undergraduate students, Stephanie Stama, an assistant director at the student psychological services centre, reports that 'it is increasingly common for us to hear that students have lost a significant amount of money' in sports betting and that it 'is interfering with basic needs like eating and sleeping.'"
* "Timothy Fong, a professor of psychiatry at the University of California, Los Angeles sees a similar pattern. Every one of his clients of late has been an 18-to-24-year-old man seeking help for a sports-betting or cryptocurrency addiction. The financial wreckage can be severe, too."

Finally, the chart below is instructive. I was first turned onto the issue of sports gambling because, as a young (ish) man,4 I've seen people I know end up getting themselves in way too deep--and I'm confident there are people whose problem gambling I know nothing about. So it's no surprise that young men increasingly know people who are problem gamblers:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d3242d3c-963c-405c-af07-a3724e677135?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: Siena College Research Institute, _[The Economist](https://substack.com/redirect/d3242d3c-963c-405c-af07-a3724e677135?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
### _[Axios](https://substack.com/redirect/51227f07-bd2a-4e8a-83dd-526049115df8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ : "The high cost of the U.S. sports betting boom"
Three data points taken verbatim from this article, all of which I've spoken about before:
> * In states with access to online gambling, bankruptcy rates rose 28% and debt collection amounts rose 8% -- roughly two years after legalization, [one paper found](https://substack.com/redirect/aa03f58e-5267-48e7-bb3f-cb9e6929676d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
> * [Another study](https://substack.com/redirect/db1c63c7-b7ea-4c0e-b401-5bc3adb3f5cd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found that every dollar spent on sports betting shaved 99 cents off investments -- meaning bettors are pulling from savings, not "fun money."
> * [A third study](https://substack.com/redirect/449ba70f-8053-49d9-8771-e36edbedbc50?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) tracked a 20% jump in mass-market alcohol consumption and a 75% spike in calls to helplines after legalization.
>

[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=2440398&post_id=181827868&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTgxODI3ODY4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjU5NzgxMTcsImV4cCI6MTc2ODU3MDExNywiaXNzIjoicHViLTI0NDAzOTgiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.p1VkiKigmK1p9huqZm4y5BkIJEiEusYdddljtmBzTOQ)
Because it's been a staggered legalization from one state to the next, we can more clearly see the impact in states where there's gambling vs. states where there's not.
In short: gambling addiction--because of illegal markets, [crypto](https://substack.com/redirect/4c94248d-007c-4404-9540-dad8f8aed503?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and national ads--is up everywhere. But it's _way_ up in states where online gambling has been legalized:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/51227f07-bd2a-4e8a-83dd-526049115df8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Source: National Council on Problem Gambling, _[Axios](https://substack.com/redirect/51227f07-bd2a-4e8a-83dd-526049115df8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ , and _[The Economist](https://substack.com/redirect/d3242d3c-963c-405c-af07-a3724e677135?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
### _[The New York Times](https://substack.com/redirect/9dc02bf3-7f58-40ae-9e96-7c3c1514e59b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_ : "Inside the Dark and Predatory World of Crypto Casinos"
I've focused most of my writing and research on online _sports_ gambling, but the problem is much bigger than that. Here's a video recap of this _NYT_ article that I think is really instructive:
[](https://substack.com/redirect/29b940af-4594-471e-8671-23b4d8aa0726?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
In short: a lot of casinos use cryptocurrencies to operate illegally. They lure people (usually men, usually young, often under 18) in with celebrities and the promise of wealth, but people are finding themselves with addictions and debt before they're legal adults.
_The Wall Street Journal_ [wrote something similar last week](https://substack.com/redirect/eeda539f-3cd4-4d55-b20b-c351b7b8c304?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the risks young men are taking by gambling on things like crypto and stock-market options trading.
### Lawmakers are taking notice, but they're (generally) too old to see the carnage first-hand
On both sides of the aisle, [lawmakers are taking notice](https://substack.com/redirect/60bb39a3-5821-4e68-8212-ecf9e371ec15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg): Republican Senators Ted Cruz (TX) and Ron Johnson (WI) and Democratic Senator Richard Blumenthal (CT) are all on record calling for more regulation. Right here in Missouri, Josh Hawley has expressed plenty of [skepticism on this in the past](https://substack.com/redirect/721bed68-9775-4c2c-9a2a-58b168dd8e19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).5
But [Congress is disproportionately old](https://substack.com/redirect/263eb572-cc24-48ea-86ef-ad372d9f5b32?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), which means that members of Congress are:
* [Less likely to be problem gamblers themselves](https://substack.com/redirect/62ae7cd5-cf5c-4f7b-9f2a-83e8f173d6a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
* [Less likely to know someone](https://substack.com/redirect/a48e206c-cf07-4a11-9ef1-00b707eef80d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) who's a problem gambler, online or otherwise.

What this means: it's incumbent upon those of us who are most impacted, and those of us whose friends are most impacted, to keep speaking up.
It's why I'm still writing about this, and why I will continue to do so.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9iZW5zYW11ZWxzLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9hY2NvdW50IiwicCI6MTgxODI3ODY4LCJzIjoyNDQwMzk4LCJmIjp0cnVlLCJ1Ijo5MDUwMTE5MiwiaWF0IjoxNzY1OTc4MTE3LCJleHAiOjIwODE1NTQxMTcsImlzcyI6InB1Yi0wIiwic3ViIjoibGluay1yZWRpcmVjdCJ9.Nb-Nd3ma7-03U_3WaGFsUZNzJBVh6hK-4d4YNW6OTGM?)
_**Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting.** If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, [please consider subscribing](https://substack.com/redirect/83370a1f-600b-4c8c-9648-eeae80b46f37?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)._
1
In fairness, it's better than the "Are you happy to be home?" divorce lawyer ad that greets travelers on their way down the escalator to baggage claim in the Southwest terminal:
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/4ef7fe71-8fc5-4ca8-bd58-71e95058cdfe?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
2
That's not to say that I want you to stop sending them to me, by the way. I find them interesting and motivating. Keep them coming!
3
Further context: relative to people ages 18-22, [far fewer people in the general population](https://substack.com/redirect/78ab1c12-5800-4d37-885a-1e63030054a5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are problem gamblers. [As few as 2% of people over 55](https://substack.com/redirect/62ae7cd5-cf5c-4f7b-9f2a-83e8f173d6a3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) are problem gamblers, according to the National Council on Problem Gambling.
4
I'm holding onto this designation for the last few years that I'm [younger than the median American](https://substack.com/redirect/6ae3def0-7542-4b55-907b-59e45d0e59a7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), thank you very much!
5
[The direct quote](https://substack.com/redirect/721bed68-9775-4c2c-9a2a-58b168dd8e19?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg):
> "I certainly respect the right of state voters, including ours, to make up their own minds on this. I'm a skeptic, though. I will just tell you I'm concerned about the consequences here," U.S. Sen. Josh Hawley told ABC 17 News. "I'm worried here about what the consequences are downstream on families, on the game itself, collegiate athletics, young athletes. I hope that policymakers in the state are keeping a close eye on that."
At least when it comes to the issue of abortion access, it's worth noting that Hawley's respect-the-right-of-state-voters-to-make-up-their-own-minds approach is, to state the obvious, inconsistent.

clean_body: The public is starting to notice just how bad companies like FanDuel and DraftKings are. | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more The public is starting to notice just how bad companies like FanDuel and DraftKings are. | | | Dec 17 | | | | | | | | | | | | This is a follow-up to what I wrote two weeks ago. And because it 's not a partisan issue, there's a chance for Democrats and Republicans to come together to address this growing crisis. In case you missed it, here's what I wrote: | | | | | | | ---|---|--- | Dec 3 | I flew into the St. Louis airport a few nights ago and was immediately hit with more sports gambling ads.1 | | ---|---|--- Because I am acknowledging that online sports gambling is bad, I have friends sending me these sorts of ads all the time now.2 They're inescapable. But if you've been paying attention to the news lately, what you'll notice: a recent groundswell of coverage on the negative impact that sports gambling is having on communities, especially on boys and young men. Public opinion is finally shifting, and I'm optimistic that in a few years there may be an opportunity to limit the damage that companies like FanDuel and DraftKings are causing. Here's a roundup on what I've been reading on the topic. ? Americans are increasingly skeptical of online gambling and sports gambling recently ran a poll that shows Americans' growing concerns about sports gambling. In May 2022, 23% of Americans said states allowing people to bet on sports was "a bad thing." As of earlier this month, that number has grown to 36%. (A plurality, 49%, say that it's "neither good nor bad.") What's interesting: every group measured increasingly thinks that sports gambling is bad, relative to what they believed in 2022. | | ---|---|--- Source: What I find most notable is that the people most exposed to this--the gamblers themselves and sports fans generally--are some of the people who've moved the most on this issue in the last 3.5 years. Today, relative to 2022: 6.7× more sports bettors agree that legal sports gambling is a bad thing. 2.8× more devoted sports fans agree that it's a bad thing. Other polls find something similar. A finds that "56 percent of monthly gamblers in Massachusetts now believe that the harms of gambling outweigh the benefits." : "College campuses have become a front line in America's sports-betting boom" I'll start with a few quotes from The Economist article: "The NCAA survey found that 16% of 18-to-22-year-olds engage in problematic sports gambling." (.) For additional context, in most states, it's not even legal for 18-year-olds to gamble.3 "At Pennsylvania State University, which has 64,000 undergraduate students, Stephanie Stama, an assistant director at the student psychological services centre, reports that 'it is increasingly common for us to hear that students have lost a significant amount of money' in sports betting and that it 'is interfering with basic needs like eating and sleeping.'" "Timothy Fong, a professor of psychiatry at the University of California, Los Angeles sees a similar pattern. Every one of his clients of late has been an 18-to-24-year-old man seeking help for a sports-betting or cryptocurrency addiction. The financial wreckage can be severe, too." Finally, the chart below is instructive. I was first turned onto the issue of sports gambling because, as a young (ish) man,4 I've seen people I know end up getting themselves in way too deep--and I'm confident there are people whose problem gambling I know nothing about. So it's no surprise that young men increasingly know people who are problem gamblers: | | ---|---|--- Source: Siena College Research Institute, : "The high cost of the U.S. sports betting boom" Three data points taken verbatim from this article, all of which I've spoken about before: In states with access to online gambling, bankruptcy rates rose 28% and debt collection amounts rose 8% -- roughly two years after legalization, . found that every dollar spent on sports betting shaved 99 cents off investments -- meaning bettors are pulling from savings, not "fun money." tracked a 20% jump in mass-market alcohol consumption and a 75% spike in calls to helplines after legalization. Because it's been a staggered legalization from one state to the next, we can more clearly see the impact in states where there's gambling vs. states where there's not. In short: gambling addiction--because of illegal markets, , and national ads--is up everywhere. But it's way up in states where online gambling has been legalized: | | ---|---|--- Source: National Council on Problem Gambling, , and : "Inside the Dark and Predatory World of Crypto Casinos" I've focused most of my writing and research on online sports gambling, but the problem is much bigger than that. Here's a video recap of this NYT article that I think is really instructive: In short: a lot of casinos use cryptocurrencies to operate illegally. They lure people (usually men, usually young, often under 18) in with celebrities and the promise of wealth, but people are finding themselves with addictions and debt before they're legal adults. The Wall Street Journal about the risks young men are taking by gambling on things like crypto and stock-market options trading. Lawmakers are taking notice, but they're (generally) too old to see the carnage first-hand On both sides of the aisle, : Republican Senators Ted Cruz (TX) and Ron Johnson (WI) and Democratic Senator Richard Blumenthal (CT) are all on record calling for more regulation. Right here in Missouri, Josh Hawley has expressed plenty of But , which means that members of Congress are: . who's a problem gambler, online or otherwise. What this means: it's incumbent upon those of us who are most impacted, and those of us whose friends are most impacted, to keep speaking up. It's why I'm still writing about this, and why I will continue to do so. ? Feel free to share this post with someone who will find this interesting. If you're reading this email because someone sent it to you, . 1 In fairness, it's better than the "Are you happy to be home?" divorce lawyer ad that greets travelers on their way down the escalator to baggage claim in the Southwest terminal: | | ---|---|--- 2 That's not to say that I want you to stop sending them to me, by the way. I find them interesting and motivating. Keep them coming! 3 Further context: relative to people ages 18-22, are problem gamblers. are problem gamblers, according to the National Council on Problem Gambling. 4 I'm holding onto this designation for the last few years that I'm , thank you very much! 5 : "I certainly respect the right of state voters, including ours, to make up their own minds on this. I'm a skeptic, though. I will just tell you I'm concerned about the consequences here," U.S. Sen. Josh Hawley told ABC 17 News. "I'm worried here about what the consequences are downstream on families, on the game itself, collegiate athletics, young athletes. I hope that policymakers in the state are keeping a close eye on that." At least when it comes to the issue of abortion access, it's worth noting that Hawley's respect-the-right-of-state-voters-to-make-up-their-own-minds approach is, to state the obvious, inconsistent. | | | | | | | (C) 2025 Ben Samuels 548 Market Street PMB 72296, San Francisco, CA 94104 ? ?

body:
# [](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/203?t=1&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)
Good morning!!
I just finished a run, and my team just let me know that we’re a bit short of where we need to be to hit our fundraising goal for our December 31 deadline. If you have a minute, please let me explain why this deadline is so important **[(but if you’re in a rush, you can chip in right here, right now)](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hVxyII&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251217_AM1_V1_S1&t=2&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)**.
I’m running for governor of Michigan to build the future Michiganders deserve — to raise wages, lower costs, protect our rights and freedoms from bullies and billionaires, and ensure every person has the tools they need to thrive.
Our fight to make Michigan the best place to call home requires a lot of hard work and a lot of resources. That’s why I’m hoping you’ll stand in this fight with me. Michigan is a battleground state, and we can’t afford to take any votes for granted.
I’m counting on grassroots support from folks like you to help me take our message across Michigan and reach voters in all 83 counties.
I’m running against well-funded opponents who are lagging in the polls but pulling out all the stops with wealthy, deep-pocketed special interests to get ahead. They will do everything they can to outraise us _again_ and post big numbers for our upcoming deadline. But with your help, I know we can share strong numbers that validate the momentum behind our movement.
**[That’s why I’m asking, friend: Will you join me in creating the future we deserve by pitching in a donation? Every act of support makes a difference.](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/854?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hVxyII&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251217_AM1_V1_S1&t=3&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
| [CHIP IN $10 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/604?c0=jmb-em-q42025.ecTtCp&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251217_AM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=10&t=4&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)

| [CHIP IN $50 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/604?c0=jmb-em-q42025.ecTtCp&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251217_AM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=50&t=6&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)
---
[CHIP IN $100 NOW](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/604?c0=jmb-em-q42025.ecTtCp&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251217_AM1_V1_S1&email=&amount=100&t=7&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)

[ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.jocelynbenson.com/go/239?c0=jmb-em-q42025.hprbVa&refcode=AU_JMB_FR_GEN_GEN_AC_20251217_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=9&akid=1147%2E181023%2EBTvsOt)

I truly believe that there’s not a single thing we can’t do if we do it together. Thank you for standing with me.
— Jocelyn

The December 18 live webinar and Q&A is approaching. Join Thursday at 9:30 AM PST / 12:30 PM EST for a detailed walk through of Parler’s ecosystem and an overview of the current offering. The team will give a breakdown of the company’s infrastructure, platform scale, revenue model, and long-term vision.

Parler operates an interconnected system of cloud services, global CDN, payments infrastructure, e-commerce tools, video streaming, and social applications. The leadership team will explain how these layers work together and how they support creators and merchants at scale.

The session will also review the anticipated IPO targeted for 2026 and the structure of the Regulation D 506(c) offering for accredited investors.

If you cannot attend live, please register and we will send the replay to you after the session.

clean_body: View in browser Parler   The December 18 live webinar and Q&A is approaching. Join Thursday at 9:30 AM PST / 12:30 PM EST for a detailed walk through of Parler’s ecosystem and an overview of the current offering. The team will give a breakdown of the company’s infrastructure, platform scale, revenue model, and long-term vision. Parler operates an interconnected system of cloud services, global CDN, payments infrastructure, e-commerce tools, video streaming, and social applications. The leadership team will explain how these layers work together and how they support creators and merchants at scale. The session will also review the anticipated IPO targeted for 2026 and the structure of the Regulation D 506(c) offering for accredited investors. If you cannot attend live, please register and we will send the replay to you after the session. Register Now If you would like to schedule a dedicated call with an investor relations representative, you can book a call here . Questions can also be sent to mailto:invest@parler.com invest@parler.com or call or text tel:2145602565 (214) 560-2565 with any questions. Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans _barely_ held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. **That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose.** I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026.
---

Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: **It doesn’t have to be that way.** We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a **20-point margin**. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. **How?** We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on **bold colors**. We drew sharp contrasts. We stuck it to the Left every single day, and we delivered real results. **If Republicans want to win, we need to give voters something to believe in, not just something to vote against.** **[That’s why I launched RON PAC.](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=cc5b888c69b745a29b1af7e7ec296432&_e=sbUdFjH6gbikeLakdxuGv-2MjsVBySpnLgAFtmerl4lATCCckbKj2DHeMMQFxnFHFyuQPScgEYPhNgftLdcU6jLOGNU_jy090CrmD3pbLy8LC-yk6t1pwMcBVPFAc-j3aLedEVw-MlXnIvnQggkasjz_U2TPKm_l0FwK1A3wQck7RMbBQVKVpOjXtEmY7RdxLIFvFexBt5wWSVz1nFb_It9wTCEjWgUN99GtVQO-lmWmQUBS7UNW37-mMctMRc8EZSG-6YpFFCPt7kh2iNplwf_Y1YQl7b03JBIzRFXB67L89G2rFbr0DuSH7WdKVcIozOV7mRwXBctOG7Y8lKwaC_RU7bzdhaJYCDgJynW4pq_e76TgIc1xdN6S0okc5JV0YAjIktpetH81RYBxlU0qsz_NxAK0Vnr8jpHh-WjkScYA-qS0LNPlj2VSsgo8tJHSwRe7cu5tHNY9ZZSVcXKRTkJcldvqmiOLnp7xel1Ekm4CfIKjnsa_ulCpLjSReCKM1rW-tl_SBcZo4ZduW-T7Nyt6_NrsaIAcv7sepfBwwdQElE3v5q0-Rq50D6L8g4XolSSwVHrvmnYSH3eCt0bA1kqmk7gmBE4nnVSuw3jMywfVGoTT2hZ5taqF4KsJ5g8Vr0wb7Jtk2fMy4rqDPdseHicMnaB_hEiJPBsRls7wo8mwFsUYq7whc5P_O1df_HSRawnBF9wp8TgrmmvAJHziqjb9Rv1iJLQT7hvY8355kfCjZK2BxT9aRDXravHd0jlbN5gxMFqEqywXztZ22ENLaxjsaTFLNXnNTkdNm7z82aIi35dTMHbd_dusS6QyoWQZgw4cKrKGr4E5PkUtoWzKsg%3D%3D)** We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch, don’t fold, and don’t back down. Leaders who will stand up for parents' rights, border security, economic sanity, and American strength, just like we’ve done in Florida.

Not next summer. **Right now.** **2026 is right around the corner** , and if we’re going to build a winning majority, we’ve got to get in the fight today. Don’t wait. clean_body: We need to give voters something to believe in| | | Friend, Tennessee’s special election should’ve been a slam dunk. Instead, Republicans barely held the seat, with just a 9-point win in a district President Trump carried by nearly 20. That’s a wakeup call. And if we don’t listen, we’ll lose. I went on Fox News and laid out, step-by-step, what we need to do to win in 2026. Here’s the issue we face: Some of the voters who show up for President Trump don’t show up when he’s not on the ballot. But here’s the good news: It doesn’t have to be that way. We overcame it in Florida in 2022. This state was a purple battleground. Remember, we were winning by one point just a few years ago. But in 2022, we won by 1.5 million votes...a 20-point margin. It was the biggest Republican victory in the state’s history. How? We didn’t run on pale pastels. We ran on bold colors. We drew sharp contrasts. We stuck it to the Left every single day, and we delivered real results. If Republicans want to win, we need to give voters something to believe in, not just something to vote against. We’re electing bold conservatives across the country who don’t flinch, don’t fold, and don’t back down. Leaders who will stand up for parents' rights, border security, economic sanity, and American strength, just like we’ve done in Florida. Help us take the Florida Blueprint nationwide! This moment demands action. Not next year. Not next summer. Right now. 2026 is right around the corner , and if we’re going to build a winning majority, we’ve got to get in the fight today. Don’t wait. Help us recruit, support, and elect battle-ready conservatives across the country. Onward, Onward, Gov. Ron DeSantis | | | | | | Paid for by Restore Our Nation (RON PAC). Not Authorized by any Candidate or Candidate’s Committee. www.ronpac.com | PO Box 3696 Tallahassee, FL 32315 |

Gabby has an incredible life story. She has been through more than anyone should ever have to endure – but she turned her pain into action. She’s a 3X cancer survivor who dreamed about helping people through science. Before running for office, she had a career at the St. Jude Department of Chemical Biology and Therapeutics – the same institution that saved her life as a child. Then, she made history as the first Latina Democrat and the first LGBTQ woman elected to the Tennessee House of Representatives.

Gabby’s life story has made her believe that, together, we can make the impossible possible. We couldn’t agree more.

**Plus, here is what Gabby has to say about protecting abortion rights:**

_“I am pro-choice and have served on the board of the local Planned Parenthood Affiliate and now serve on the Planned Parenthood Federation of America board. I worked against the constitutional amendment that took away abortion protections here in TN and my voting record as an elected reflects my long history of fighting for abortion rights in my state and beyond.”_

[**As a reminder, TN ranks among the bottom in the nation for maternal health outcomes, and women in TN are living under a near-total abortion ban with no exceptions for rape or incest. It’s critical that we protect pro-choice leaders in this state as we work to chip away at the anti-choice, male majority. Help us support Gabby Salinas by pitching in a split $20.26 or more contribution immediately.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121447102/589484080/-855912670?refcode=EM_F_251217salinas_N_CD_90DO_10_NG_N_L2&amount=20.26&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjBlMmNjZGU0LTU0ZGItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=vw8xdK8VQDa3Ry_w1EOhMz0PResj7Y5hyryIN3WjqmQ=)

Together,

Colleen Davis
Endorsements Manager

Peter, Reflecting on our victories last month and early last week in our special election, it’s clear: **Georgia is ready for change.** Because of you, we broke the GOP's total control of the Public Service Commission, electing Democrats to the PSC for the first time in two decades, and flipped a Trump +12 district blue in the special election for HD 121. Voters rejected extremism and sent a powerful message about the future we want to build. That success proved our movement’s strength. **Now, we must build on it, Peter.** The road to 2026 runs straight through Georgia. Defending Senator Jon Ossoff’s seat is already a national priority — and one of the toughest fights we’ll face. At the same time, we must make gains in races at every level, from local offices to the State House. **[We have a critical $10,000 goal to meet by midnight tonight. This funding is what will allow us to lay the groundwork: hire organizers, launch voter outreach, and build the infrastructure to win up and down the ballot. Will you chip in $10 right now to help us hit our goal and defend Georgia’s future?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121448481/589489397/-871074936?amount=10&source=DPG_EM_FR_121725_B1_MM3_D1_S3&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTA4MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIzYThiOTdmYy01NmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HYJLp4wbCTdfNqblszJB7nuGrYwBeoayQwxig3uR6sU=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

name: Stop MAGA
subject: Why was Boebert’s Dem opponent on Fox News???
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
We took a lot of flak for going on these conservative platforms, here’s why we did it.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NPzNq8iqxoiMUMZsLX8eC_acSHrkxMT_xFJO3oa3oo3qf3zdfR3LgoWT5C6J5yD-04jpJOLbx48r2fN3Wc37dWHgHbQQhjQCfNHf0BxQ2u6unNcUyp6KNBnnTv3KS646p9EMoCaNFHFHtnXmmqfx4LgeNoOMg9hoDuHTQ6umppQ7UsIk97_vUawoJFTpxErGexOrpN3fU1M_rvPwY4AexrieyDUdraGL&c=pa6qoywXR3ZYs7_MDjGtA8QEG-X1U_Gb-dxr189USG-vYaigGMzYlw==&ch=eY3cF-wDukkHDCkTHOT5UOtUQvF8Q-O7wrg5lP-yi-ZArNR5ZK6EAg==)
---
|

Peter,
Today Colorado's energy utility Excel energy cut power to over 100,000 Coloradans because of 60-85 mph winds. In response, Trisha Calvarese, Lauren Boebert's Democratic opponent, called out Excel on Twitter.
The Denver metro area's local Fox News affiliate, Fox 31 KDVR, saw our post and brought Trisha on to discuss it. Earlier in the week Trisha also appeared on Conservative talk radio station KHOW to talk about our race.
We took a lot of flak for going on these conservative platforms, here's why we did it.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NPzNq8iqxoiMUMZsLX8eC_acSHrkxMT_xFJO3oa3oo3qf3zdfR3LgoWT5C6J5yD-04jpJOLbx48r2fN3Wc37dWHgHbQQhjQCfNHf0BxQ2u6unNcUyp6KNBnnTv3KS646p9EMoCaNFHFHtnXmmqfx4LgeNoOMg9hoDuHTQ6umppQ7UsIk97_vUawoJFTpxErGexOrpN3fU1P4Gc2im6VrljKXyw4hFmZ-&c=pa6qoywXR3ZYs7_MDjGtA8QEG-X1U_Gb-dxr189USG-vYaigGMzYlw==&ch=eY3cF-wDukkHDCkTHOT5UOtUQvF8Q-O7wrg5lP-yi-ZArNR5ZK6EAg==)
---
Even if every registered Democrat in Colorado's 4th (Boebert's district) came out to vote in 2026 it would still not be enough to flip this seat. The path to victory here requires that we win Unaffiliated voters and even peel off some GOP voters.
We aim to persuade these voters, not by trying to out-conservative someone like Boebert. That's a losing playbook. We do it through the power of our populist policies, and with a high energy candidate voters can get excited about.
MAGA claims to be for the working class and against the elites but in reality their movement is the inverse. Our populism is real, we support the little guy and are taking on Big Tech, Big Ag, and Big Pharma, busting their monopolies, strengthening the middle class and actually ending America's blood soaked foreign policy.
To spread this unabashed left-populist message we have to meet voters where they are \- on the TV and radio platforms that they use to get their news, including Fox News and conservative talk radio stations like KHOW.
This strategy is working -- in 2024 when Boebert and Trisha went head to head, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming VP Harris by 6 pts. And just a few weeks ago, the Chair of the Colorado Libertarian party became a Democrat citing Trisha as the reason for her defection.
[This is how we splinter the MAGA base - and win. Will you rush a donation $25 to our campaign to help us keep going on conservative media, holding town halls in Republican counties, and building the coalition we need to defeat Boebert, take back this district, and flip the house in 2026?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001NPzNq8iqxoiMUMZsLX8eC_acSHrkxMT_xFJO3oa3oo3qf3zdfR3LgoWT5C6J5yD-blHbSH_vqgmcGdUu7OuZpKfMhin1TlQkwP9evwdgGm1_EbQTd2hByhWW2BZoIGwfmwuhNo4lqEK0r5opqatNL8_ueSjUq55NFn_zpYe6RL6ZHI4Cq8TDBLfVXqwf-U1Utl41PpxZqC6R1EmxPhv8sWEx1z2EjTYneS5OKGf-Vac=&c=pa6qoywXR3ZYs7_MDjGtA8QEG-X1U_Gb-dxr189USG-vYaigGMzYlw==&ch=eY3cF-wDukkHDCkTHOT5UOtUQvF8Q-O7wrg5lP-yi-ZArNR5ZK6EAg==)
---

name: Andy Kim
subject: Senate Republicans made a choice that will hurt millions of Americans
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey
body:
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121451368/589499622/41798106?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjEvMS84Nzk3MCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNmFiZGE4My01OWRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=czPIHSjkSd6CStvME5Y4Khrw9_GCrybOL2sSknQzvv0=&emci=f128f072-aada-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=16abda83-59db-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=26261389)
---

| | Last week, Senate Republicans made a choice that will hurt millions of Americans. They rejected a Democratic plan to extend the Affordable Care Act's enhanced tax credits, all but guaranteeing that these savings will expire at the end of the year. **That means starting in January, families across the country will see their health insurance premiums spike, through no fault of their own.** This is happening at a time when people are already stretched thin. As we head into the holiday season, families are making hard choices about groceries, rent, child care, and gifts for their kids. Now, millions are being told their monthly health care bill is about to go up, sometimes by hundreds of dollars a month. **Every Republican Senator knew what was at stake.** Extending these subsidies would have kept health care affordable for working families, seniors, and small business owners. Republicans chose instead to walk away, pulling the rug out from under people who depend on this coverage to stay healthy and financially stable. I want to be clear about where I stand. Health care should not be a luxury, and no one should lose coverage or face crushing costs because of political games. **I will keep fighting to lower health care costs because families deserve health care security, not uncertainty.** More soon, _Andy_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Andy Kim instantly:

clean_body: | | | | Last week, Senate Republicans made a choice that will hurt millions of Americans. They rejected a Democratic plan to extend the Affordable Care Act's enhanced tax credits, all but guaranteeing that these savings will expire at the end of the year. That means starting in January, families across the country will see their health insurance premiums spike, through no fault of their own. This is happening at a time when people are already stretched thin. As we head into the holiday season, families are making hard choices about groceries, rent, child care, and gifts for their kids. Now, millions are being told their monthly health care bill is about to go up, sometimes by hundreds of dollars a month. Every Republican Senator knew what was at stake. Extending these subsidies would have kept health care affordable for working families, seniors, and small business owners. Republicans chose instead to walk away, pulling the rug out from under people who depend on this coverage to stay healthy and financially stable. I want to be clear about where I stand. Health care should not be a luxury, and no one should lose coverage or face crushing costs because of political games. I will keep fighting to lower health care costs because families deserve health care security, not uncertainty. More soon, Andy | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Andy Kim instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for by Andy Kim for New Jersey | This email was sent by . | **_“Florida becomes the GOP’s biggest redistricting test — and a messy one” — Politico_**
---
Peter,
Florida Republicans had a hearing last week about their forced mid-decade map redraw — one that could potentially tip the scales in their favor by _three to five additional congressional seats_.
Some are calling this the “crown jewel” of the recent Republican campaign to gerrymander before the midterms. And what happens could ultimately determine which party controls Congress next year.
**[We’re rapidly mobilizing to fight back against any attempt to gerrymander Florida’s map, but we can’t let up in combating the redistricting crisis everywhere else. Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has been chomping at the bit to force through a gerrymandered map and deliver President Trump extra seats in Congress. Meanwhile, he’s looking to the U.S. Supreme Court to give him carte blanche to dilute the votes of communities of color to do it.
Just this week, Gov. DeSantis said in an interview, “ _**Yeah, yeah, so we’re going to redistrict.** The issue is that there is a Supreme Court decision that we are waiting on — the argument in October about Section 2 of the VRA [Voting Rights Act] that impacts Florida’s maps._”
Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act ensures that majority-minority districts have the opportunity to elect a representative of their choice. DeSantis and other map manipulators are hoping and praying that the Supreme Court guts the Voting Rights Act so they can reverse decades of civil rights gains.
One thing is clear: Florida Republicans are trying to do as much damage to our democracy as they can. **As lawmakers prepare to hear public comments, we need to make clear that this is a blatant attempt to suppress the will of the people.**
**[This blatant power grab in Florida is just the beginning of the next phase of this redistricting crisis — rush a donation right now, and let’s do everything we can to fight back.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121451564/589500197/-1027206931?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_FloridaConveningNor_email_20251217-NDRC-Tier1-FloridaConveningNor_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251217-NDRC-Tier1-FloridaConveningNor_&utm_campaign=FloridaConveningNor&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjA4OWNkNzE2LTU5ZGItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=8UOUj_DIVs9CQ3vBMCe7W2n_MmPyDAlp-9qYAFjWyQE=)**

clean_body: It’s the “crown jewel” in Republican attempts to gerrymander, and it must be stopped. | | “Florida becomes the GOP’s biggest redistricting test — and a messy one” — Politico Peter, Florida Republicans had a hearing last week about their forced mid-decade map redraw — one that could potentially tip the scales in their favor by three to five additional congressional seats. Some are calling this the “crown jewel” of the recent Republican campaign to gerrymander before the midterms. And what happens could ultimately determine which party controls Congress next year. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Florida Governor Ron DeSantis has been chomping at the bit to force through a gerrymandered map and deliver President Trump extra seats in Congress. Meanwhile, he’s looking to the U.S. Supreme Court to give him carte blanche to dilute the votes of communities of color to do it. Just this week, Gov. DeSantis said in an interview, “ Yeah, yeah, so we’re going to redistrict. The issue is that there is a Supreme Court decision that we are waiting on — the argument in October about Section 2 of the VRA [Voting Rights Act] that impacts Florida’s maps.” Section 2 of the Voting Rights Act ensures that majority-minority districts have the opportunity to elect a representative of their choice. DeSantis and other map manipulators are hoping and praying that the Supreme Court guts the Voting Rights Act so they can reverse decades of civil rights gains. One thing is clear: Florida Republicans are trying to do as much damage to our democracy as they can. As lawmakers prepare to hear public comments, we need to make clear that this is a blatant attempt to suppress the will of the people. = Thank you for your help in this mission-critical moment. — Team NDRC = = | | Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President. | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|---|---|--- National Democratic Redistricting Committee 712 H St NE, #25 Washington, DC 20002 United States if you'd like to receive fewer emails from the NDRC. Text MAPS to 36787, STOP to end. Msg and Data rates may apply

name: Stop MAGA
subject: Why was Boebert’s Dem opponent on Fox News???
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado
body:
We took a lot of flak for going on these conservative platforms, here’s why we did it.
| | | | | [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001m4i9dtRmG2Lerk69KwjqM7RMiUJsIWFF50LCEM3n7EHinvGi1Z4bHB_8PXIS5dNSpUuGvSYmh8UMeBjJOsJXNDx1vlcm_C8PZ793gNMn8y_DlvpQvkTuxptOTvSRThGdvpHpM5jCjXtE3IlK24j20ti2y2ONhblTiApC4evbJ6cOV7lqIezvR8cJ4BjfkZLn10iU_8B9h8gNy29lWIW3Cv4P_eZWKnL_&c=94pcTF6OM_t_CPdKE2twJtmlDceQNtZajc98zx_WsOhK2nnzo7qM1Q==&ch=O7ukQxV2gpnZjKwld-OHtQkb8A_U5E6ftXvYs5qfNdjwwHJu7emfIQ==)
---
|

Peter,
Today Colorado's energy utility Excel energy cut power to over 100,000 Coloradans because of 60-85 mph winds. In response, Trisha Calvarese, Lauren Boebert's Democratic opponent, called out Excel on Twitter.
The Denver metro area's local Fox News affiliate, Fox 31 KDVR, saw our post and brought Trisha on to discuss it. Earlier in the week Trisha also appeared on Conservative talk radio station KHOW to talk about our race.
We took a lot of flak for going on these conservative platforms, here's why we did it.
---
| [](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001m4i9dtRmG2Lerk69KwjqM7RMiUJsIWFF50LCEM3n7EHinvGi1Z4bHB_8PXIS5dNSpUuGvSYmh8UMeBjJOsJXNDx1vlcm_C8PZ793gNMn8y_DlvpQvkTuxptOTvSRThGdvpHpM5jCjXtE3IlK24j20ti2y2ONhblTiApC4evbJ6cOV7lqIezvR8cJ4BjfkZLn10iU_8B9h8goz5qjef1t43RVIX6LoA-K&c=94pcTF6OM_t_CPdKE2twJtmlDceQNtZajc98zx_WsOhK2nnzo7qM1Q==&ch=O7ukQxV2gpnZjKwld-OHtQkb8A_U5E6ftXvYs5qfNdjwwHJu7emfIQ==)
---
Even if every registered Democrat in Colorado's 4th (Boebert's district) came out to vote in 2026 it would still not be enough to flip this seat. The path to victory here requires that we win Unaffiliated voters and even peel off some GOP voters.
We aim to persuade these voters, not by trying to out-conservative someone like Boebert. That's a losing playbook. We do it through the power of our populist policies, and with a high energy candidate voters can get excited about.
MAGA claims to be for the working class and against the elites but in reality their movement is the inverse. Our populism is real, we support the little guy and are taking on Big Tech, Big Ag, and Big Pharma, busting their monopolies, strengthening the middle class and actually ending America's blood soaked foreign policy.
To spread this unabashed left-populist message we have to meet voters where they are \- on the TV and radio platforms that they use to get their news, including Fox News and conservative talk radio stations like KHOW.
This strategy is working -- in 2024 when Boebert and Trisha went head to head, Trisha had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming VP Harris by 6 pts. And just a few weeks ago, the Chair of the Colorado Libertarian party became a Democrat citing Trisha as the reason for her defection.
[This is how we splinter the MAGA base - and win. Will you rush a donation $25 to our campaign to help us keep going on conservative media, holding town halls in Republican counties, and building the coalition we need to defeat Boebert, take back this district, and flip the house in 2026?](https://t7pkg9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001m4i9dtRmG2Lerk69KwjqM7RMiUJsIWFF50LCEM3n7EHinvGi1Z4bHB_8PXIS5dNS2aOxufX3aQJaMzLmzV1dkBcHQjuUWyBV8NwaR-v4lVgtNkHxJHY0YGkqnvYC50-XzpVpLrBhJW2q7wBfrCqJJxNV0-ribOlL7SnEI2vxH77dYSQR9NqmrdwQDMaHZBYPZfe6N5Da1dWiiT18fZKtWnEv4e1yqaRcO-05Ghop-4c=&c=94pcTF6OM_t_CPdKE2twJtmlDceQNtZajc98zx_WsOhK2nnzo7qM1Q==&ch=O7ukQxV2gpnZjKwld-OHtQkb8A_U5E6ftXvYs5qfNdjwwHJu7emfIQ==)
---

name: Catherine Cortez Masto
subject: ¡La lucha sigue!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS
body:
We are the last line of defense for Nevada families and for our freedoms. | | | |
---
Friend,
When I was first elected in 2016, at the same time as Donald Trump, I told all of our grassroots supporters “ _¡La lucha sigue!_ The fight continues!”
Next year’s election night will be the 10th anniversary of that moment, and we have a choice to make:
* We can make real progress for working families by winning back the House, flipping the Governorship, and enshrining abortion rights in our state constitution.
* Or, we can leave things as they are as Trump and his extreme Republican allies continue to put billionaires ahead of the best interests and wellbeing of Nevada’s families.

**I refuse to believe that the current state of affairs is how things should be. Nevadans deserve better.** We’ve proven time and again that when we put in the work, when everyday folks chip in $3 or $10 or whatever they can towards our shared goal, we can win. I won my race in 2022 by just 7,290 votes, and I couldn’t have done it without grassroots supporters like you. So believe me when I tell you that every contribution makes a real difference.
**[Before year’s end, I need you with us. Please, friend, any amount you give right now to NV Dems will power our grassroots operation to reclaim our democracy and set things right.](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqu4ksFa5SF7IXH6Sr7Lz9yT1IBVfzOrqxt30R4iT-GDafBJDvuHpAgsW6tC74mUNP4fHIds6-o58to1oh6NvVw5aq-Fr8wcJ9XpS4iUf3jeMjwlLI_c6VucMNIaNzExo6_0SJ_TQdCgwa6Fw1wka9YCMeAcCjH71VgLyG5hjAms3kyS_VYcb64iran-XUirKyw9nFqldfhb4nyAtmtfnI6rEWaK_9l4TCzAOZ1ofqHrY0DrkPnfcg6LWv_y7d4FpCr25is-It2tLVTCnMQt6vzYAEDcUWIY3fAyJH4_WU07HkWCQYx4RdHjMTBtJj2IwmxIqwnb1YGZtjtnZI3K7i4skKpkHvGZikZCEgjjnWKc2A/4mi/gFY8EAV6QyKEhiUSlSi21g/h0/h001.iSuFCAmGo-qG4nF40RMgJQBNjg2VqfWamTENGREfN-k)**

clean_body: We are the last line of defense for Nevada families and for our freedoms. | | | | Friend, When I was first elected in 2016, at the same time as Donald Trump, I told all of our grassroots supporters “ ¡La lucha sigue! The fight continues!” Next year’s election night will be the 10th anniversary of that moment, and we have a choice to make: We can make real progress for working families by winning back the House, flipping the Governorship, and enshrining abortion rights in our state constitution. Or, we can leave things as they are as Trump and his extreme Republican allies continue to put billionaires ahead of the best interests and wellbeing of Nevada’s families. I refuse to believe that the current state of affairs is how things should be. Nevadans deserve better. We’ve proven time and again that when we put in the work, when everyday folks chip in $3 or $10 or whatever they can towards our shared goal, we can win. I won my race in 2022 by just 7,290 votes, and I couldn’t have done it without grassroots supporters like you. So believe me when I tell you that every contribution makes a real difference. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | | ¡La lucha sigue! The fight continues! Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto The Nevada State Democratic Party fights for progress in the Silver State. We believe that we are stronger together than on our own, and that America succeeds when everyone gets a fair shot, everyone does their fair share, and everyone plays by the same rules. | | Paid for by the Nevada State Democratic Party, NVDEMS.com, not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. name: So it begins
subject: Trump super donor pledges $250 million for third term run
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
What happened at the White House yesterday made it clear that the battle against a Trump third term is far from over.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TYoJ0TBcr47M8eBjCX3Kjb-14QulLEsfr_w4eHxn-EwkLlScHBzOmRFNN1XvzY9DjrvP9BbZc1lhrlCmLDGpFP53Fspbnw9CAbUs0e_i2VNU1n6zO6lJiVux0g7fIO4_AD1BX_1R7LRYA_zH0TGgbdWN4x3iiNySH_2CkLzB3kY9fM_XHK5LzVleu1UNwESCe3-S2dSbyzZbViQpHjaz3IivhghKA-i9CqS6AyzDwww=&c=M8Ro1YcMVFuK3vpI06bSQlKt_oLcCukaS1a4CTtYFCaK2Z4USxDbQw==&ch=7eKwfgNY73K_SCxVVNmBQJLlG5DZYdxxNocAcwEZEPRNQUZx36hE0g==)
---
| Peter,
A quarter-billion dollars.
That's how much MAGA billionaire Mariam Adelson pledged to bankroll a third term for Trump. Inside the White House. In front of cameras for all to see.
---
| [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TYoJ0TBcr47M8eBjCX3Kjb-14QulLEsfr_w4eHxn-EwkLlScHBzOmRFNN1XvzY9DMCT7RpcsdkXZ5G9Ra85xqJ-Zrgv051tc1ryolx5goPfWMMAwmxPdu79OocBYMml3GSPwWnMB21yIwKnrw1viulrXFyrzpBfFTAtjKIuPnMaszrUgM2_0dx5Bp4CTG5tQ9JJfaoWSPcIBzGy24Lj482u7vaNjaNXxCs4NhEkfTuI=&c=M8Ro1YcMVFuK3vpI06bSQlKt_oLcCukaS1a4CTtYFCaK2Z4USxDbQw==&ch=7eKwfgNY73K_SCxVVNmBQJLlG5DZYdxxNocAcwEZEPRNQUZx36hE0g==)
---

Trump's plot for a third term just won't die.
Last month, Defend Our Courts helped lead the defense of Pennsylvania's Supreme Court. We stopped MAGA from seizing control of the court that oversees how elections are run in the nation's biggest swing state. It was a blowout victory and a crucial win for democracy.
But what happened at the White House yesterday made it clear that the battle against a Trump third term is far from over. Trump has a fallback plan, and it runs straight through the courts in Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan.
In 2026, the Supreme Courts of these three critical states are on the ballot. If MAGA can buy these seats, they won't need to win an election in 2028, they'll just have their hand-picked judges throw out results they don't like.
The threat didn't go away after PA. It just tripled.
Defend Our Courts is once again leading the vanguard, rallying our over 100,000 grassroots supporters from every corner of this country to defend these three crucial courts.
But we cannot fight three fires at once without your immediate help. The stakes are higher than they have ever been.
[Will you rush a donation of $50, $25, or whatever you can spare to help us hold the line in AZ, GA, and MI?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001TYoJ0TBcr47M8eBjCX3Kjb-14QulLEsfr_w4eHxn-EwkLlScHBzOmRFNN1XvzY9DRCCbrEeFY97NRK3M9sG9K0_8GAEAtpsRsyc98Ob7JjzCIMQZtZ_eWxBq7WzFvZ4r9rmqL-lk8jpbdCn8yXNtN9wsRbA25v7F3bMk_k8kGUm3t9rXR7YpBaGc7OuoDV5D3hQFkyv-yno8LEvXySeMqiieNTb7AgR2FgxxfGW4R8qwlZZ1ggXGPg==&c=M8Ro1YcMVFuK3vpI06bSQlKt_oLcCukaS1a4CTtYFCaK2Z4USxDbQw==&ch=7eKwfgNY73K_SCxVVNmBQJLlG5DZYdxxNocAcwEZEPRNQUZx36hE0g==)
---

clean_body: What happened at the White House yesterday made it clear that the battle against a Trump third term is far from over. | | | | | == | Peter, A quarter-billion dollars. That's how much MAGA billionaire Mariam Adelson pledged to bankroll a third term for Trump. Inside the White House. In front of cameras for all to see. | == Trump's plot for a third term just won't die. Last month, Defend Our Courts helped lead the defense of Pennsylvania's Supreme Court. We stopped MAGA from seizing control of the court that oversees how elections are run in the nation's biggest swing state. It was a blowout victory and a crucial win for democracy. But what happened at the White House yesterday made it clear that the battle against a Trump third term is far from over. Trump has a fallback plan, and it runs straight through the courts in Arizona, Georgia, and Michigan. In 2026, the Supreme Courts of these three critical states are on the ballot. If MAGA can buy these seats, they won't need to win an election in 2028, they'll just have their hand-picked judges throw out results they don't like. The threat didn't go away after PA. It just tripled. Defend Our Courts is once again leading the vanguard, rallying our over 100,000 grassroots supporters from every corner of this country to defend these three crucial courts. But we cannot fight three fires at once without your immediate help. The stakes are higher than they have ever been. == | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | | | | == | They have billionaires. We have the people. We've beat them once before, but now we have to do it again on a much grander scale. We can't afford to let up, democracy is on the line. -- Defend our Courts | | | | | | | | | | == | | | | | == | | | | Mail checks to: | | | | Defend Our CourtsP.O. name: Official Democratic Headquarters
subject: What just a few dollars can do
body:
---
Derek, We’re reaching out today to ask you to [**chip in to the DNC**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?refcode=em_20251217_5573_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5573_22930333_nmX0fX&akid=5573%2E22930333%2EnmX0fX) — and we hope you’ll give us a minute of your time to tell you how we’ll put your contribution to work. In 2025, Democrats organized everywhere — in all 50 states, up and down the ballot — to deliver record-breaking victories. We also hosted town halls in every state, ensuring that Americans in red districts could have their voices heard, even when their own elected officials refused to be held accountable to their constituents. And now, we’re making a six-figure investment to recruit and train staff in key states, to ensure we’re placing experienced operators in critical battleground states as early as possible. This is the type of work your contribution funds. We know that to take back the House and Senate in 2026, we need to scale up the infrastructure we built this year exponentially, so that we can organize and compete in every district in America. Because when we organize everywhere, we can win anywhere. **Derek, for the cost of a monthly streaming service, you can help us build the resources we need to fund all of this work.** [**If electing Democrats in 2025, 2026, and beyond is worth a few dollars to you, we’re asking you to contribute today. Any amount that feels right to you makes a big difference:**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?refcode=em_20251217_5573_fr&amount=7&refcode2=5573_22930333_nmX0fX&akid=5573%2E22930333%2EnmX0fX) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $7](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-december-2025?express_lane=true&amount=7&refcode=em_20251217_5573_fr&refcode2=5573_22930333_nmX0fX&akid=5573%2E22930333%2EnmX0fX)**

Our communities are under siege, and felon Trump’s mass deportation agenda is fueling this crisis. To put more "thugs on the ground" fast, ICE has removed the age requirement for new hires, skipping background checks, and lowering standards so much that truckloads of unqualified agents may be the ones with criminal records. This is not public safety; it's a terrifying failure of government. _ICE is proving to be the worst of the worst._ **The Local Democracy Project is fighting back, but we need your help to support local candidates who will PROTECT our neighbors and RESIST the terror and abuse ICE is inflicting.** [**Will you chip in to help us TAKE BACK our government at every level and restore safety and sanity?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqsCgbBF5rm9TF3OCm2PlAzc830mH0SaEU79yenHkofnN_uWYPpvMkxHbaNmJMl7Mjq50-rCekK79UYNFuPRuPwkz3iH1xqXfMEee0S0B6XyvjAGT7U8ozrf8mhrczL3Dm32yMNjXyIVE2tSBapdcH8TWoBuJdnz8K18kJ51bxIcIniAQtJ5Dv6HlLCZ-kldF6Lu7kpUg2zSS_mycpyInG2-mZG6g7GaIrDg8EEyyp2qnhMeMofDhFQlrcEm8KzJmgLo0oyuzl5xRBuWW_J7kR9Png6Un74As2vrYgWDptZozflkLOG1MrnNovaD20xk6DXa2x11KED-kjgN1CSS7nfsHKz_gMeeRO8OKkktrn_XFPkElYOHG1nWpz48CqsgTaU/4mi/BNjf1BOmQTOUSqT07x4Lwg/h2/h001.gCepYo4TPMHqvSZA0z4yfcEjlkLs_t5V1ywezw0Z4JI) ****_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately._ [Donate $10

Our communities are under siege, and felon Trump’s mass deportation agenda is fueling this crisis. To put more "thugs on the ground" fast, ICE has removed the age requirement for new hires, skipping background checks, and lowering standards so much that truckloads of unqualified agents may be the ones with criminal records. This is not public safety; it's a terrifying failure of government. ICE is proving to be the worst of the worst. The Local Democracy Project is fighting back, but we need your help to support local candidates who will PROTECT our neighbors and RESIST the terror and abuse ICE is inflicting. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Crucially, the Trump regime is well behind its inhumane and illegal mass deportation goals, thanks in large part to courageous state and local efforts to protect immigrant communities and limit cooperation with federal agencies. This proves that local action works! We are the frontline defense. States like Florida and Texas mandate local law enforcement assist ICE, leading to high arrest numbers. But in states like Illinois and New York, local policies limiting collaboration have suppressed arrests, saving thousands from the horrors of federal custody. The regime relies heavily on local collaboration, and local elections are where we can stop it. Your donation powers our efforts to elect progressive candidates up and down the ballot who will enact strong, protective policies to STOP Trump's assault on our towns and STOP ICE.** Thanks for your support. -Local Democracy Project supports down-ballot races and empowering communities across the country. name: Voter Action Project
subject: SCOTUS and the VRA
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www
body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Mother Jones: Republican Justices Appear Poised to Destroy What’s Left of the Voting Rights Act [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121725_1&amount=25]
We'll hand this straight to Mother Jones, Team: " The Supreme Court, with its six-to-three Republican-appointed majority, appears ready to kneecap what remains of the Voting Rights Act’s protections for minority political representation. "
The descriptive language seems harsh, but the objective of these justices seems clear -- a complete dismantling of our seminal civil rights law . We've said it before and will say it again, but it's been clear long before the MAGA movement that Chief Justice Roberts has seen the removal of the VRA as one of his top goals for his time on the Court. It appears he may be on the cusp of succeeding.
During recent oral arguments in Louisiana v. Callais , at least five conservative justices seemed ready to handicap the VRA so that it will no longer stop white majorities from locking racial minorities out of elected office through racial gerrymandering.
The consequences are simple to understand and grave for voter participation. The harder we make it to vote, the more politicians make voter participation feel worthless, the less people actually take part. Needless to say, the less people who participate, the less our democracy and our government looks like the people it's supposed to represent.
Team: We have to spread the word about this case and the consequences of dismantling the Voting Rights Act. Fuel our work with $25 or whatever you can today and help us defend the right to vote >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121725_1&amount=25]
GIVE $15 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121725_1&amount=15] GIVE $25 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121725_1&amount=25]
GIVE $50 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121725_1&amount=50] GIVE $100 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121725_1&amount=100]
GIVE $250 NOW ➜ [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?express_lane=true&refcode=121725_1&amount=250] Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121725_1&amount=35]
At issue in the case is Section 2 of the VRA, which requires that racial minorities have an equal opportunity to meaningfully participate in the electoral process. This provision has been used to strike down districting schemes and maps that prevented Black voters and other racial minorities from electing their preferred representatives. Since 1965, Section 2 has given people of color a seat at the table, from school boards to the halls of Congress. It appears this 60-year era is coming to an end.
“ Race is a part of redistricting always ,” Justice Sonia Sotomayor said, addressing the solicitor general from Louisiana, who was arguing against an existing map that had enabled the election of two Black members of Congress in his state. “ What you’re saying to us [is]...‘You can use [race] to help yourself achieve goals that reduce particular groups’ electoral participation, but you can’t use it to remedy that situation .’”
Striking down the last remnants of the Voting Rights Act is exactly why the Supreme Court held this case over from last year. Gutting Section 2 would take us backwards in time and allow racial gerrymandering to be a standard practice, silencing more voters. Just like the redistricting fights across the country, this is all about whether the party in power can choose their voters in order to stay in power in perpetuity.
We simply can't afford to go backwards like this. Will you chip in with $25 or more to help us fight back against these partisan efforts to silence voters? >> [http://secure.actblue.com/donate/ifvap-power?refcode=121725_1&amount=25]
https://voteractionproject.org/ [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121725_1&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121725_1&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax-deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.

Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States

| Every Georgia Democrat should be cheering: Eric Gisler -- a Democrat who runs a local olive oil shop -- won a special election and flipped a Georgia state House seat that Donald Trump carried by 12 points.
Republicans want you to believe districts like Marjorie Taylor Greene's aren't winnable. They tell us her district is too red, too far gone, too hopeless to contest.
But they said the same thing about districts just like the one Eric Gisler now represents.
[Will you rush a donation to my campaign right now to help me build on this momentum and win MTG's district for Democrats?](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pHSawSE8oXi-FeClJ3QswKGLl5mbiuBI6yfmq3F9whvxuNYzrR4ggbYKFm1OPpQxaar29RfYMjTiUZazDciyFHuCFHw9V6z8gIMXtx7OoteCMLxJarVAfwpXcYZaAWXjdxviHvLVFOQ0XETe3OAXt-plT1zOdYyeVFfGWf6uuvLOTa2MnFwzWbSSn-3iJz7jTI8CW2QBpokgXd8blxpeEOsTxDP2E5KL&c=NlfeArJBdda2GZs9jOU0Em7GnDs-8NTJBTfr7_sfvRW3nAFmtc9yCQ==&ch=iqTtWXwCym80mTX1mw95xpnrefhiZR1560Nu6QH3dYSVqk5ntXOu_Q==)

| Here's what this win tells us:

* Voters in so-called "deep red" areas are tired of being taken for granted.
* When Democrats actually show up and give people something to vote for, they respond.
* The map is changing -- and our imagination needs to change with it.

I'm in this race because I believe Georgia deserves more than chaos, conspiracy theories, and cruelty. We deserve representatives who care about lowering costs, protecting our freedoms, and actually solving problems -- not chasing cable news hits.
This election result isn't just a feel-good headline. It's a blueprint.
We can organize in places Republicans have written off. We can build real coalitions in communities MTG has ignored. And yes -- we can flip her seat.
[But that only happens if we invest early and build strong. So if you're as energized by this news as I am, I'm asking you to step up: Please rush a donation of any amount to my campaign right now.](https://zzejj9hbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001pHSawSE8oXi-FeClJ3QswKGLl5mbiuBI6yfmq3F9whvxuNYzrR4ggbYKFm1OPpQxaar29RfYMjTiUZazDciyFHuCFHw9V6z8gIMXtx7OoteCMLxJarVAfwpXcYZaAWXjdxviHvLVFOQ0XETe3OAXt-plT1zOdYyeVFfGWf6uuvLOTa2MnFwzWbSSn-3iJz7jTI8CW2QBpokbjb1rw38T120ZbUx1ofeq&c=NlfeArJBdda2GZs9jOU0Em7GnDs-8NTJBTfr7_sfvRW3nAFmtc9yCQ==&ch=iqTtWXwCym80mTX1mw95xpnrefhiZR1560Nu6QH3dYSVqk5ntXOu_Q==)

Republicans want you to believe districts like Marjorie Taylor Greene's aren't winnable. They tell us her district is too red, too far gone, too hopeless to contest. But they said the same thing about districts just like the one Eric Gisler now represents. == | Here's what this win tells us: Voters in so-called "deep red" areas are tired of being taken for granted. When Democrats actually show up and give people something to vote for, they respond. * The map is changing -- and our imagination needs to change with it. I'm in this race because I believe Georgia deserves more than chaos, conspiracy theories, and cruelty. We deserve representatives who care about lowering costs, protecting our freedoms, and actually solving problems -- not chasing cable news hits. This election result isn't just a feel-good headline. It's a blueprint. We can organize in places Republicans have written off. We can build real coalitions in communities MTG has ignored. And yes -- we can flip her seat. == | | | | | == | == | == | == Thank you. -- Shawn Harris | | == | SHAWN FOR GEORGIA | P.O. Box 1688 | Cedartown, GA 30125 US | | | | | |
