# Conservatives pissed at Left-wing media's latest hit piece
The Trump administration faced harsh criticism from conservatives following **[Vanity Fair's](https://substack.com/redirect/9ac6e6f6-32e2-43f2-af30-97994fc1b4be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/9ac6e6f6-32e2-43f2-af30-97994fc1b4be?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)bombshell profile of White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, with critics questioning why the White House granted access to the liberal publication.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/87bae54a-cc98-4fc4-ae2a-18483d32283f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Extensive cooperation questioned:** Wiles participated in 11 interviews over the past year with Vanity Fair writer Chris Whipple, making extraordinary comments including describing Trump as having an "alcoholic's personality," calling Vice President JD Vance a "conspiracy theorist for a decade," and saying Attorney General Pam Bondi "completely whiffed" on Jeffrey Epstein files, prompting conservative backlash over why Republicans cooperate with left-leaning outlets.
* **Conservative media erupts:** The Federalist's Mollie Hemingway declared herself "sick to death of people on the right who seek the approval of left-wing media," while radio host Dana Loesch warned "the legacy press isn't your friend," and New York Post columnist Karol Markowicz criticized the White House for allowing unflattering photo shoots, with Outkick's Clay Travis advising Republicans to only conduct live interviews preventing selective editing.
* **Damage control launched:** Wiles called the piece a "disingenuously framed hit piece" that omitted positive comments about Trump and the team, while Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt accused Vanity Fair of "bias of omission" and taking words out of context.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/5801d775-47e8-48b2-8dbd-724b3108fddf?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---

Vice President Vance joked the lesson should be "we should be giving fewer interviews to mainstream media outlets" as administration officials rallied behind Wiles on social media.
* * *
# **Trump administration adds 5 more countries to travel ban**
The Trump administration announced Tuesday it is adding **[five nations to its travel ban](https://substack.com/redirect/d61c338d-b06a-431e-b3c6-1453df321a5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**[ ](https://substack.com/redirect/d61c338d-b06a-431e-b3c6-1453df321a5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)list and imposing further restrictions on 15 others, citing security concerns following the Thanksgiving weekend shooting of two National Guard members by an Afghan national.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d61c338d-b06a-431e-b3c6-1453df321a5d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
* **Ban expansion details:** Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, South Sudan, and Syria join the existing 12-country full ban list, with complete travel restrictions also imposed on Palestinian-Authority-issued travel documents, while 15 additional nations--including Nigeria, Tanzania, and Zimbabwe--face partial restrictions, bringing total affected countries to approximately 40.
* **Security justifications cited:** The White House argues restrictions are necessary because many targeted nations have "widespread corruption, fraudulent or unreliable civil documents and criminal records" hindering U.S. vetting capabilities, plus high visa overstay rates, deportation cooperation refusals, or "general lack of stability and government control."

The administration claims the measures advance "important foreign policy, national security, and counterterrorism objectives" by preventing entry of foreign nationals about whom the U.S. "lacks sufficient information to assess the risks they pose."
* * *
**[Together with Health Science Institute](https://substack.com/redirect/fee029f0-f08c-4858-837a-3598f83bd1a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
# **[MAHA M.D. proves the"PLANDEMIC" is REAL](https://substack.com/redirect/fee029f0-f08c-4858-837a-3598f83bd1a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/78fa2d18-cfb5-424b-9031-5ca6f427a913?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Dear truth-seeking American,
This MAHA doctor's **[viral video](https://substack.com/redirect/fee029f0-f08c-4858-837a-3598f83bd1a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** not only confirms the COVID pandemic was built on lies...
But he's holding undeniable proof the next "PLANDEMIC" is starting now.
Before it comes for your family **[SEE HIS MESSAGE HERE.](https://substack.com/redirect/fee029f0-f08c-4858-837a-3598f83bd1a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
P.S. The new "Plandemic" will kill 600,000 mostly older Americans in 2026. **[View the 2 signs of disease- and how to protect yourself here.](https://substack.com/redirect/fee029f0-f08c-4858-837a-3598f83bd1a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)**
[Learn More](https://substack.com/redirect/fee029f0-f08c-4858-837a-3598f83bd1a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* * *
# **Candace Owens describes four-hour meeting with Erika Kirk**
Podcaster Candace Owens shared **[details Tuesday evening](https://substack.com/redirect/2190bb30-7e9e-447d-b06b-5dcb03adace6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** about her lengthy Monday meeting with Turning Point USA CEO Erika Kirk, revealing she remains unconvinced of the accused shooter's guilt despite the organization's efforts to address her concerns.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/8c2738a5-3ba4-4084-8059-885668794f15?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
* **Meeting purpose and opening:** The four-hour sit-down began with TPUSA COO Justin Streiff asking what they hoped to accomplish, with Kirk and Streiff expressing upset over Owens' tweet calling TPUSA a "god-forsaken company" people shouldn't fund--comments Owens acknowledged she must "own."
* **Evidence questions central:** Owens explained she hoped for gained confidence to tell her audience "Tyler Robinson shot Charlie Kirk" and pressed leadership to explain their certainty about the suspect's guilt, prompting them to call their lawyer who revealed TPUSA has seen no evidence beyond what's publicly available.
* **Doubts persist:** Following the meeting, Owens declared "I am very confident stating...I don't believe that Tyler Robinson pulled the trigger" after learning investigators won't reveal additional evidence until the upcoming hearing when they must present concrete proof beyond the affidavit to move the case forward.
* **Comments:** Many commetns beneath the Youtube video regarded how vague the conservation rundown seemed, and the ignorance the greater public is forced to have regarding the entire situation.

MUST WATCH!: TDS protesters LOSE THEIR MINDS when reporter ask them to say something nice about President Trump Support our protest coverage by subscribing to our PolitiBrawl News YouTube channel: | | ---|---|--- Conservatives pissed at Left-wing media's latest hit piece The Trump administration faced harsh criticism from conservatives following profile of White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, with critics questioning why the White House granted access to the liberal publication. | | ---|---|--- Extensive cooperation questioned: Wiles participated in 11 interviews over the past year with Vanity Fair writer Chris Whipple, making extraordinary comments including describing Trump as having an "alcoholic's personality," calling Vice President JD Vance a "conspiracy theorist for a decade," and saying Attorney General Pam Bondi "completely whiffed" on Jeffrey Epstein files, prompting conservative backlash over why Republicans cooperate with left-leaning outlets. Conservative media erupts: The Federalist's Mollie Hemingway declared herself "sick to death of people on the right who seek the approval of left-wing media," while radio host Dana Loesch warned "the legacy press isn't your friend," and New York Post columnist Karol Markowicz criticized the White House for allowing unflattering photo shoots, with Outkick's Clay Travis advising Republicans to only conduct live interviews preventing selective editing. Damage control launched: Wiles called the piece a "disingenuously framed hit piece" that omitted positive comments about Trump and the team, while Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt accused Vanity Fair of "bias of omission" and taking words out of context. | | ---|---|--- Vice President Vance joked the lesson should be "we should be giving fewer interviews to mainstream media outlets" as administration officials rallied behind Wiles on social media. Trump administration adds 5 more countries to travel ban The Trump administration announced Tuesday it is adding and imposing further restrictions on 15 others, citing security concerns following the Thanksgiving weekend shooting of two National Guard members by an Afghan national. | | ---|---|--- Ban expansion details: Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, South Sudan, and Syria join the existing 12-country full ban list, with complete travel restrictions also imposed on Palestinian-Authority-issued travel documents, while 15 additional nations--including Nigeria, Tanzania, and Zimbabwe--face partial restrictions, bringing total affected countries to approximately 40. Security justifications cited: The White House argues restrictions are necessary because many targeted nations have "widespread corruption, fraudulent or unreliable civil documents and criminal records" hindering U.S. vetting capabilities, plus high visa overstay rates, deportation cooperation refusals, or "general lack of stability and government control." The administration claims the measures advance "important foreign policy, national security, and counterterrorism objectives" by preventing entry of foreign nationals about whom the U.S. "lacks sufficient information to assess the risks they pose." | | ---|---|--- Dear truth-seeking American, This MAHA doctor's not only confirms the COVID pandemic was built on lies... But he's holding undeniable proof the next "PLANDEMIC" is starting now. Before it comes for your family P.S. The new "Plandemic" will kill 600,000 mostly older Americans in 2026. Candace Owens describes four-hour meeting with Erika Kirk Podcaster Candace Owens shared about her lengthy Monday meeting with Turning Point USA CEO Erika Kirk, revealing she remains unconvinced of the accused shooter's guilt despite the organization's efforts to address her concerns. Meeting purpose and opening: The four-hour sit-down began with TPUSA COO Justin Streiff asking what they hoped to accomplish, with Kirk and Streiff expressing upset over Owens' tweet calling TPUSA a "god-forsaken company" people shouldn't fund--comments Owens acknowledged she must "own." Evidence questions central: Owens explained she hoped for gained confidence to tell her audience "Tyler Robinson shot Charlie Kirk" and pressed leadership to explain their certainty about the suspect's guilt, prompting them to call their lawyer who revealed TPUSA has seen no evidence beyond what's publicly available. Doubts persist: Following the meeting, Owens declared "I am very confident stating...I don't believe that Tyler Robinson pulled the trigger" after learning investigators won't reveal additional evidence until the upcoming hearing when they must present concrete proof beyond the affidavit to move the case forward. Comments: Many commetns beneath the Youtube video regarded how vague the conservation rundown seemed, and the ignorance the greater public is forced to have regarding the entire situation. * You're currently a free subscriber to . name: Bo French
subject: DEI has overtaken the Railroad Commission
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Bo French Campaign
body:
Texas energy, at the hands of the Texas Railroad Commission (RRC), is overrun with woke DEI spending.
| | | | [](https://link.emails.bofrench.com/click/e596597/VaHR0cHM6Ly9ib2ZyZW5jaC5jb20/SZW1haWxzX2JvZnJlbmNoX2NvbTI1MTIxNw/LbmNsQXJwSDM/gaULadQ/ses6961ee0c)
---
|
---
| | Dear friend,
Let me say something most politicians in Austin can’t say ---
I actually know this business, and I didn’t hear it from big-wig Austin lobbyists in fancy suits.
I was born into it.
My family has been in the oil business for nearly 80 years. Both of my grandfathers were in the oil business, and my father was in the oil business as a wildcatter. He also owned a successful oil service and oilfield equipment manufacturing business.
And in Midland, my brothers and I run our family office, still fully engaged in the Texas energy industry every single day.
But that’s not all.
_While the political class was busy giving speeches, I was building businesses overseas that protected the world’s largest oil and gas companies from some of the most dangerous threats on the planet - - radical Islamists and Somali pirates._
So when people ask why I’m running for Railroad Commissioner, the answer is simple:
_**Texas deserves someone who knows the oil business inside and out**._
I’m that man for the job. After all, it's in my blood.
---
| | [Donate](https://link.emails.bofrench.com/click/e596597/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9iby1mcmVuY2gtY2FtcGFpZ24vcnJj/SZW1haWxzX2JvZnJlbmNoX2NvbTI1MTIxNw/LbmNsQXJwSDM/gaULadQ/sch2695e8a1)
---
| | For years, the Railroad Commission has been held back by career politicians who would rather cater to foreign governments than their own constituents.
And Texans are paying the price.
As Railroad Commissioner, I will use my experience to:
* Lower costs for operators
* Increase efficiency
* Streamline permitting
* Pressure the Legislature to reduce taxes on the industry that powers our state and our economy

Because if we want to secure Texas’s future, we must keep Texas energy dominant.
That’s why I’m asking for your support today.
Together, we can launch the next great Texas energy boom.
---
| | | [](https://link.emails.bofrench.com/click/e596597/VaHR0cHM6Ly9ib2ZyZW5jaC5jb20/SZW1haWxzX2JvZnJlbmNoX2NvbTI1MTIxNw/LbmNsQXJwSDM/gaULadQ/ses6961ee0c)
---
| | **For Texas,**
**Bo French** **Candidate for TX Railroad Commissioner**

---
| | P.S. I’m the only candidate in this race with the experience and backbone necessary to make Texas the energy capital of the country.
**[Support my campaign for Railroad Commissioner.](https://link.emails.bofrench.com/click/e596597/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9iby1mcmVuY2gtY2FtcGFpZ24vcnJj/SZW1haWxzX2JvZnJlbmNoX2NvbTI1MTIxNw/LbmNsQXJwSDM/gaULadQ/sch2695e8a1)**

body:

|

clean_body: I’ll admit, I’m a little bit humbled | | | = Yesterday, you heard from my longtime partner in serving Virginia, Senator Tim Kaine. He told me he’d reach out to you and help me get back on track for my important end-of-year fundraising benchmark. He also told me he thinks he can recruit more grassroots donors to my campaign than I can. And I’ll admit it: I’m pretty impressed by – and grateful to – the 232 supporters who answered Kaine’s request with a donation. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = Whatever the outcome of our friendly competition, I’m proud to work alongside Kaine every day to continue delivering for Virginia. And I’m incredibly grateful for every single person who has stepped up to support my reelection campaign and to every American who has stood up and spoken out against this administration and their reckless agenda. We’ve got a long road towards the midterm elections, thank you for sticking with me. = Thank you, Warner = * | | Friends of Mark Warner 1490-5A Quarterpath Road #213 Williamsburg, VA 23185 United States | Click here to . | Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner

clean_body: | | | If the Pentagon won’t hold Hegseth accountable – Congress must. Pentagon didnot conduct a routine investigation into whether Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth damaged national security by sharing strike plans on a privately owned messaging app, Signal... because Hegseth didn’t order one. Hegseth decided not to take accountability and instead investigated his own staff, even threatening to polygraph defense officials he believed were disclosing embarrassing details about him. Our brave men and women are now terrified of doing anything that could be perceived by Hegseth as an attempt to undercut him. We** can nothave a paranoid, egotistical maniac as the head of our Defense Department – we must impeach Hegseth. Cedar Key Progress has expanded our billboard program demanding Hegseth’s impeachment. Congress is the only power that can hold Hegseth accountable because we certainly know Trump won’t. name: Roy Cooper
subject: Please allow me to explain why I am asking for your support today.
body:
I can’t do it alone.
|
# [](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2365?source=&t=1&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
Peter, I am running in the most competitive United States Senate race of 2026, and while I’m confident that we can win, I _know_ I can’t do it alone. I’ve won tight races before, like when we unseated the incumbent Republican governor — the first time anyone had defeated a sitting governor in North Carolina. **[But I only won that race, like every election in which I’ve ever run, because I had the help and support of people who believed in me. I’m asking you to believe in me now, and more importantly, I’m asking you to donate.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2899?t=7&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_ | [DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2900?t=8&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $10](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2901?t=9&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $25](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2902?t=10&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $50](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2903?t=11&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $100](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2904?t=12&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2905?t=13&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
As North Carolina’s next Senator, I will do everything in my power to make our state and country better. I’ll advocate for health care accessibility, abortion rights, a livable planet, and access to the ballot box. This is the message my team and I are taking to every corner of this state.
Connecting with people where they are is how we win. I should know. _But it doesn’t come cheap._
**Meanwhile, my far-right opponent and his extreme Republican allies are already attacking me — and we need to direct resources into countering them.**
That means we can’t afford to fall short. We can’t afford to cede an inch of ground to the GOP.
**[Peter, if you’re with me, don’t wait to pitch in. Please make a contribution tonight.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2899?t=14&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)**
_If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_
[DONATE $5](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2900?t=15&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $10](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2901?t=16&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $25](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2902?t=17&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $50](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2903?t=18&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE $100](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2904?t=19&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE ANOTHER AMOUNT](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2905?t=20&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
I appreciate all your help,
Roy Cooper
[](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2899?t=21&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
* * *
Democrat Roy Cooper is running for U.S. Senate to help take back the Majority for Democrats. North Carolina is Democrats’ best chance to flip a seat from red to blue — the path to stopping Republicans in Washington starts here.

National Republicans will target North Carolina as a key battleground and invest heavily in defeating us.

Cooper is a former state legislator, Attorney General, and most recently Governor of North Carolina. North Carolina will be the most competitive Senate election of 2026, and Cooper enters the race as the most formidable candidate of either party.

Cooper’s signature achievements include helping to expand Medicaid and helping make NC the top state for business by creating thousands of jobs and growing the clean energy economy.

**[Donate today to help win this seat and flip the Senate blue.](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=2&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)**
[DONATE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1914?source=&t=3&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
---
[DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.roycooper.com/go/1915?source=&t=4&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
[SHOP OUR MERCH STORE](https://act.roycooper.com/go/2133?t=5&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
[info@roycooper.com](mailto:info@roycooper.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.roycooper.com/go/825?t=6&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
Cooper Victory Fund
PO Box 1190
Raleigh, NC 27602
Paid for by Cooper Victory Fund, a joint fundraising committee authorized by Cooper for North Carolina, DSCC, and North Carolina Democratic Party — Federal.
---
Contributions or gifts to Cooper Victory Fund are not tax deductible.
The first $7,000 of each contribution from a person and the first $10,000 from a multicandidate committee (“PAC”) will be allocated to Cooper for North Carolina. The first $3,500 of a person’s contribution and the first $5,000 of a PAC’s contribution will be designated for the primary election; the next $3,500/$5,000 will be designated for the general election. The next $10,000 from a person and $5,000 from a PAC will be allocated to North Carolina Democratic Party – Federal. The next $44,300 of a contribution from a person and the next $15,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC General Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Headquarters Fund. The next $132,900 from a person and the next $45,000 from a PAC will be allocated to the DSCC Legal Fund. A contributor may designate his or her contribution for a particular participant. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution.
Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email address, with a check, or with a spouse — thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.
By clicking the links above, you certify that you are responsible for paying all charges incurred in using the debit or credit card to be charged, and that your personal funds will be the true source of the contribution.

[You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time.](http://act.roycooper.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)
If you wish to receive fewer messages from us, [submit this form to let us know.](https://act.roycooper.com/signup/i-want-less-emails-cooper-north-carolina/?t=22&akid=139%2E124072%2EqVkBK9)

name: =?utf-8?q?Trump=E2=80=99s_Hit_List_via_Our_Revolution_?=
subject: There's a target on our back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Our Revolution click here to unsubscribe
body:
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1011&refcode=email-20251217_v&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]Our Revolution logo

Peter Willis,

Let's be blunt: the Trump administration is preparing lists. They're
vowing to investigate and prosecute the left, labeling any group demanding
justice as “extremist.”

It isn't hypothetical – it's happening now. And Our Revolution could be
among the targets.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34052?source=email-20251217&t=1061&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]Headline: Bondi outlines prosecution pathways after Trump call to go
after antifa, left-leaning groups

How do we know? The far-right has made it clear who they want to silence:
Fox News Radio just smeared Our Revolution as “Bernie Sanders' Gestapo,”
and MAGA propaganda outlets like Breitbart are zeroing in on us with
growing intensity. This is not random – it's coordinated intimidation.

Our team is working round the clock to protect our volunteers, keep
activists safe at rallies, and secure the tools we need to organize –
encrypted communication, rapid response systems, legal support, and
personal security trainings. But our resources are spread thin. Every time
we think we're covered, another attack pops up.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1057&amount=22&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&refcode=email-20251217nd&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]We can't keep up this pace without your support, and donations are falling
behind this month. That's why we're asking: Can you donate $10, $25, or
whatever you can so we can stand strong together and meet this $10,000
emergency defense fund goal before midnight tonight?

If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your
donation to Our Revolution will process immediately.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1042&express_lane=true&amount=9&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]CHIP IN $9 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1043&express_lane=true&amount=22&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]CHIP IN $22
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1045&express_lane=true&amount=27&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]CHIP IN $27 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1046&express_lane=true&amount=46&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]CHIP IN $46
[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1047&express_lane=true&amount=66&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]CHIP IN $66 [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1048&amount=9&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT

It's not hard to see what Trump and his cronies are doing. They're openly
laying out a plan to weaponize our government's most powerful
investigative agencies – FBI, Homeland Security, DOJ, and even the IRS –
against left-wing groups like ours. Then, they've told law enforcement to
use Trump's sweeping National Security directive (NSPM-7) to “root out”
not just activists, but organizers and funders as well.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34052?source=email-20251217&t=1060&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]Headline: Trump's war on the left: Inside the plan to investigate
liberal groups

We refuse to be silenced, criminalized, or bullied – because our work
matters, now more than ever. As Trump's poll numbers plummet and even his
MAGA backers start to see that he's only out to help his oligarch friends,
we have the chance to make real wins to stop Trump's authoritarianism and
win progressive victories up and down the ballot.

But the scary truth is that if the far-right's campaign works, it's not
just Our Revolution at risk – it's every single activist who's dared to
speak out for a fairer, more just America.

We're already expanding encrypted communications, legal protections,
emergency security training, and rapid-response systems to keep our staff
and volunteers safe. The problem is simple: these urgent steps cost money,
and our fundraising this month is falling short.

We have always known that organizing for justice would make us enemies of
the powerful. And right now, we need to know: are you with us?

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/34051?t=1054&amount=22&refcode=email-20251217nd&amounts=9%2C22%2C27%2C46%2C66&firstname=Peter%20Willis&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&akid=9981%2E5885482%2E9Nv-bK ]When a president labels political opposition as “enemies,” defending our
movement isn't optional – it's essential. Can you chip in today to help us
raise $10,000 for our emergency defense fund and meet this moment head-on?

If we don’t act now, we risk losing the movement we’ve built together
since Bernie founded Our Revolution in 2016 — to fear, intimidation, and
silencing by Trump. Please, rush a donation now so we can keep organizing,
keep safe, and keep fighting for our democracy.

When we organize, we win.

clean_body: [ ]Our Revolution logo Peter Willis, Let's be blunt: the Trump administration is preparing lists. They're vowing to investigate and prosecute the left, labeling any group demanding justice as “extremist.” It isn't hypothetical – it's happening now. And Our Revolution could be among the targets. [ ]Headline: Bondi outlines prosecution pathways after Trump call to go after antifa, left-leaning groups How do we know? The far-right has made it clear who they want to silence: Fox News Radio just smeared Our Revolution as “Bernie Sanders' Gestapo,” and MAGA propaganda outlets like Breitbart are zeroing in on us with growing intensity. This is not random – it's coordinated intimidation. Our team is working round the clock to protect our volunteers, keep activists safe at rallies, and secure the tools we need to organize – encrypted communication, rapid response systems, legal support, and personal security trainings. But our resources are spread thin. Every time we think we're covered, another attack pops up. [ ]We can't keep up this pace without your support, and donations are falling behind this month. That's why we're asking: Can you donate $10, $25, or whatever you can so we can stand strong together and meet this $10,000 emergency defense fund goal before midnight tonight? If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Our Revolution will process immediately. [ ]CHIP IN $9 [ ]CHIP IN $22 [ ]CHIP IN $27 [ ]CHIP IN $46 [ ]CHIP IN $66 [ ]CHIP IN OTHER AMOUNT It's not hard to see what Trump and his cronies are doing. They're openly laying out a plan to weaponize our government's most powerful investigative agencies – FBI, Homeland Security, DOJ, and even the IRS – against left-wing groups like ours. Then, they've told law enforcement to use Trump's sweeping National Security directive (NSPM-7) to “root out” not just activists, but organizers and funders as well. [ ]Headline: Trump's war on the left: Inside the plan to investigate liberal groups We refuse to be silenced, criminalized, or bullied – because our work matters, now more than ever. As Trump's poll numbers plummet and even his MAGA backers start to see that he's only out to help his oligarch friends, we have the chance to make real wins to stop Trump's authoritarianism and win progressive victories up and down the ballot. But the scary truth is that if the far-right's campaign works, it's not just Our Revolution at risk – it's every single activist who's dared to speak out for a fairer, more just America. We're already expanding encrypted communications, legal protections, emergency security training, and rapid-response systems to keep our staff and volunteers safe. The problem is simple: these urgent steps cost money, and our fundraising this month is falling short. We have always known that organizing for justice would make us enemies of the powerful. And right now, we need to know: are you with us? [ ]When a president labels political opposition as “enemies,” defending our movement isn't optional – it's essential. Can you chip in today to help us raise $10,000 for our emergency defense fund and meet this moment head-on? If we don’t act now, we risk losing the movement we’ve built together since Bernie founded Our Revolution in 2016 — to fear, intimidation, and silencing by Trump. Please, rush a donation now so we can keep organizing, keep safe, and keep fighting for our democracy. When we organize, we win. name: Mark Kelly
subject: BREAKING: Escalation from Pete Hegseth
body:
The Department of Defense is upgrading its preliminary review of Mark Kelly to an official investigation.
Peter, Mark Kelly here.
They are escalating.
[](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251217-1.rHYoQV&refcode=em-20251217-1&t=1&akid=1075%2E1086043%2E8FQIRt)
We hadn't heard anything significant since December 10th, and then they dropped this.
Honestly, we learned about this the same as everyone else. We found out that Trump called for my arrest and execution by tweet. We heard about this investigation for the first time by tweet. And now that they're escalating, we hear it by tweet — again.
Trump and Hegseth won't let this go — but I refuse to back down. And I need you with me to have any chance against them.
**[Please. If you’ve got my back: Split a donation of $5 between me and Wes Moore.](https://act.wesmoore.com/go/417?c0=wes-em-20251217-1.rHYoQV&refcode=em-20251217-1&t=2&akid=1075%2E1086043%2E8FQIRt)**

Thank you in advance for the help.
Mark

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: THIS RFK Jr shocker is so outrageous... it could get him impeached:| | | | December 17 | | | | | RFK Jr’s stand against Big Pharma just uncovered this | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. [Click here now to see the full story.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=aeb2708dddc84d949523730340c6c2fb&_e=98q-hr_uuosImw_TSdPSmhE2ZpiGWuJhzEPwYzv76Kfku9PkbAx5MPnfN6_vsdW8ZnYA4DFZEkq4czKuNLOo-9SCODZCj4UIBrnQgmlVbCiQFVv41JYh6bCGNmyIUD4Lx6I1GUYyr2-Lb7qiuj8iE4tmzWfXz0chVV0V2d2It1YVG5XmjTev2xgF0HjoF6R6Xtc04uqu_oWcFl-SDwd0Wb6OcgcAe9-Nes3lJqLIi03tNbdBWYxZ2A-Ksp0phrYSayS0N-vUxPMJbDulrx8W_DSncL-oJ6PDPTM0UUkgUVNTP6jahnJf-0dxSMJkdfna7_FyFBRoXlfVJ8vue-AmNsqJsY_Y7ZXLk448hOAdh8wavaHRFWOBnYNUDlGGeecpWEtdqzhQ62EwaAD4EQQHIuSZZRy9juW9Pbep1xndHOl_hBoefzLVDipQFRpXYQAfPCdRVVpFSR-VdZHtCeh5Kp4FNJ8XI6v6EGYDalZC0YWYXt8urle9X9eRRybXWLDW6DJ5Hc0COlue_5-gyBjlNRWATZ9s9W6tbcA9oHxGS2h64aOcfmoFyaO0CF82_zV1)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. name: Mark Kelly
subject: Escalation from Pete Hegseth
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
The Department of Defense is upgrading its preliminary review of Mark Kelly to an official investigation.
#

Folks, Mark Kelly here.
They are escalating.
[](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=liftoff-pac-em-25/.wDfCNk&refcode=em251217-1-lp&t=1&akid=6300%2E6688874%2ENTX0bw)
We hadn't heard anything significant since Decembver 10th, and then they dropped this.
Honestly, we learned about this at the same time everyone else did. We found out that Trump called for my arrest and execution by tweet. We heard about this investigation for the first time by tweet. And now that they're escalating, we hear it by tweet — again.

Just look at their new pick to help lead the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). He was a trailblazer for pro-Trump election deniers, helping spread a baseless claim about noncitizens voting in the 2016 election — a myth that _Democracy Docket_ says became “a precedent for the right to reject official results and sow distrust in the voting process.” Now he’s getting rewarded with a powerful position. But wait, there’s more. Trump has already appointed election conspiracy theorists to key roles at the Departments of Justice and Homeland Security — giving anti-voter operatives a chance to attack the independence of elections from the inside. **_The Guardian:_ “The people who tried to overturn the 2020 election have more power than ever — and they plan to use it.”** And now, the Department of Justice is working hand-in-hand with outside conspiracy theorists to advance false claims that Trump’s 2020 loss was somehow illegitimate. Investigators are reaching out to discredited activists and partisan operatives to advance bogus claims of fraud. **False allegations could be used to manufacture a reason to restrict ballot access and change how elections are run — interfering with the 2026 elections in a blatant power grab.** Just look at the ways myths about noncitizen voting have been weaponized to push for mass voter suppression bills like the “SAVE” Act. **Election deniers have been put in key positions of power, and we can’t let them spread false claims that could do real harm.** **At Fair Fight Action, we push back against election disinformation, work to prevent or mitigate voter suppression, and make sure voters have the information they need to cast their ballots.** [**But we count on grassroots support to power this work, which only grows more vital by the day. name: Kirsten Gillibrand
subject: Can I get your input on this? >>
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Gillibrand for Senate
body:
Friend, My job has always been to fight for the people – to make government fairer, more accountable and more compassionate – _but that fight only means something when I’m listening to you._ Every conversation, every survey and every bit of feedback you share helps me understand what’s most important to Democrats across the country. Whether it’s protecting reproductive rights, making health care affordable or standing up for our democracy, your perspective drives our mission forward. **[**_So before we begin reviewing responses tomorrow morning, I’d love to know what you think. Please take a moment to complete our December Priorities Survey and tell me what issues you want to see front and center:_**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121467258/589550385/1913517005?refcode=LOC_EM_SURV_2025.12.17_PRIORITIES-SURVEY_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NS_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZjExMGVmNi02YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N9M1_woOaaoygoEzr62Kyn0bV3wW2PR3LHjh2biidWw=&emci=bac4132e-b0da-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=2f110ef6-6bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7659029)** | | [December Priorities Survey](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121467262/589550389/1913517005?refcode=LOC_EM_SURV_2025.12.17_PRIORITIES-SURVEY_B1_F1_S1_C1_90AC-NS_RS-T0&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTkvMS83MjcyMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIyZjExMGVmNi02YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=N9M1_woOaaoygoEzr62Kyn0bV3wW2PR3LHjh2biidWw=&emci=bac4132e-b0da-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=2f110ef6-6bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7659029) Conducted by Kirsten Gillibrand for U.S. Senate | SUPPORTER ID: 108267856

Republican extremists continue to push a dangerous agenda — threatening our reproductive rights, our access to affordable health care, our democracy and everything we care about.
That means it’s more important than ever to defend our Democrats, elect candidates in key battleground states and flip the Senate blue.
**To do that, we need all the help we can get, and that includes leaning on supporters like you. Your input informs our strategy, and it could be the difference between winning and losing next year.**

clean_body: Friend, My job has always been to fight for the people – to make government fairer, more accountable and more compassionate – but that fight only means something when I’m listening to you. Every conversation, every survey and every bit of feedback you share helps me understand what’s most important to Democrats across the country. Whether it’s protecting reproductive rights, making health care affordable or standing up for our democracy, your perspective drives our mission forward. | | Conducted by Kirsten Gillibrand for U.S. Senate | SUPPORTER ID: 108267856 STATUS: PENDING | Republican extremists continue to push a dangerous agenda — threatening our reproductive rights, our access to affordable health care, our democracy and everything we care about. That means it’s more important than ever to defend our Democrats, elect candidates in key battleground states and flip the Senate blue. To do that, we need all the help we can get, and that includes leaning on supporters like you. body:
I’d be so grateful if you could take my Priorities Survey and let me know how I can best serve this community

George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, [click here.](https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/-Phg2RhDIEGJVPbb5vK87g2?unsubscribedata=2aTePqOLPp27sOUGSwzVGTkQvgMndJJSwm%2BEaywSJluAzsY2WHjmzW2jQTVwjXpJSUkJwxarl1vxjiCC9%2BuV2ECIiyvu0EwPPWnRiTLI8clNCdMXW721cnnl4rBZK%2F33uykIyGC9mS0Z%2FGLOQSSsHFlMnbk2GMx%2Fy2rOdLdVdeNVn3RztqNhLjhRqg7JTgIv6OJ3USmKFhWMhbjXRghO5xNoVYmhWWazviGvvLronQYHxQdY0GckxYDLaF8%2FW5gEquob5LTNCZYjGMcBxHMX0AdWqtQxWwoZjmF%2FojjlOcPM2o6kUZA6gcVDFQNLGq%2BfUIzkP1eHYjzACu5tHMpynBH%2FhwMJQmf%2FMdqywpvA62Xa5AANd7haEpX2EFa221G9bIVrdE9hZ1FucqhmFmKzEl5dpZINX4kYc1bSXb5VBeETg3TM6bFBU5X7kFd4PEdn)

Peter, Since being sworn into Congress almost one year ago, I’ve met with families, small business owners, and community leaders across NY-16 — and I’m reminded every day that the most important part of my job is listening to folks like you. Washington may be noisy, but our work is about real people — keeping healthcare affordable, protecting our democracy, strengthening schools, and making our communities safer and more affordable. [That’s why I’m reaching out today: I want to know what’s on your mind and how I can best serve you. Will you take a moment to share your priorities with me?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121467497/589550874/859997585?source=SBD_GL_EM_SURV_121725_Priorities_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjM1NjA2M2YtNmNkYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=a1T086FOBzdJqMkB3lmnEuYnCQfB1JHi6oq-w8BSAhQ=&emci=27ecc338-6cdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6356063f-6cdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=21388742) | [TAKE THE SURVEY →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121467501/589550880/859997585?source=SBD_GL_EM_SURV_121725_Priorities_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjM1NjA2M2YtNmNkYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=a1T086FOBzdJqMkB3lmnEuYnCQfB1JHi6oq-w8BSAhQ=&emci=27ecc338-6cdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6356063f-6cdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=21388742)

Through the highs and lows, hearing directly from New Yorkers about what matters most to them is what renews my commitment — and reminds me why I got into public service in the first place: to fight for our community and build a better future together.
[So if you have a few minutes to spare, I’d be so grateful if you could take my end-of-year Priorities Survey and let me know how I can best serve this community.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121467502/589550883/859997585?source=SBD_GL_EM_SURV_121725_Priorities_180DO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTAvMS8xMDQwMzgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNjM1NjA2M2YtNmNkYi1mMDExLTgxOTUtMDAwZDNhMWQ1OGFhIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=a1T086FOBzdJqMkB3lmnEuYnCQfB1JHi6oq-w8BSAhQ=&emci=27ecc338-6cdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=6356063f-6cdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=21388742)

George Latimer

For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality.

clean_body: I’d be so grateful if you could take my Priorities Survey and let me know how I can best serve this community George Latimer is a lifelong Westchester resident who has built a career in public service by delivering unparalleled progressive results for local residents. Now, he's taking that work to the next level by serving as your Congressman, representing New York's 16th District. If you’d like to unsubscribe, | | Peter, Since being sworn into Congress almost one year ago, I’ve met with families, small business owners, and community leaders across NY-16 — and I’m reminded every day that the most important part of my job is listening to folks like you. Washington may be noisy, but our work is about real people — keeping healthcare affordable, protecting our democracy, strengthening schools, and making our communities safer and more affordable. | Through the highs and lows, hearing directly from New Yorkers about what matters most to them is what renews my commitment — and reminds me why I got into public service in the first place: to fight for our community and build a better future together. Thank you for sharing your thoughts with me, Peter. George Latimer * | | ---|--- = For over three decades, George Latimer has been a champion for progressive policies and leaders, helping lead the effort to make Westchester, a formerly solid Republican County, into a blue firewall that has withstood multiple red waves in recent years. George’s unparalleled track record in advancing Democratic goals includes successfully cutting regressive taxes, fighting for civil rights and racial equality, defending a woman’s right to choose, standing up to the NRA to keep guns off our streets, and voting for marriage equality. | = = Paid for by George Latimer for Congress George Latimer for Congress PO Box 789 White Plains, NY 10602 United States If you would like to unsubscribe, click here: You can also =.

It’s amazing how much of the disastrous Biden administration has been undone in such a short time.

Border security is up, the flow of deadly drugs is down, and Americans are safer. Our economy is recovering, the American worker is being prioritized, and the dangers of AI are being exposed.

But it wasn’t all good. clean_body: | | | | | | | We’re closing up the books on 2025, and what a year it’s been! It’s amazing how much of the disastrous Biden administration has been undone in such a short time. Border security is up, the flow of deadly drugs is down, and Americans are safer. Our economy is recovering, the American worker is being prioritized, and the dangers of AI are being exposed. But it wasn’t all good. This year we got confirmation of what was long suspected—Biden’s DOJ abused their power and spied on law-abiding citizens, including me! So as we look to 2026, I can promise you two things—I will keep fighting for the American worker and against the tyrannical overreach America has suffered for too long. I can’t fight this battle alone. If you believe in me and my cause, please consider rushing an end-of-year contribution to help me stay in the fight. Time is running out! | | Josh Hawley | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|---|---|--- | | | Paid for by Josh Hawley Victory Committee | | | | |

name: Ted Cruz
subject: The Tennessee wake-up call
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:
We barely held on in Tennessee– and the warning signs are flashing for 2026

| | | [](https://trk.email.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9ERUNFTUJFUi1QUkVTU1VSRS1WMl8yMDI1MTIxNyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPUVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PURFQ0VNQkVSLVBSRVNTVVJFLVYyJnV0bV9hZ2VuY3k9UEcmYW1vdW50PTc1In0/HBRwyaoQfeZfP5oSR8IIZA)
---
| | Fellow Conservative, I hope you're seeing these emails - but honestly, with contributions slowing down, it's hard to tell if our top supporters are still fired up. Recently, Republicans held a key House seat in Tennessee... but the margin plummeted to single digits in a district President Trump carried by 22 points just a year ago. **_That's a drop of more than half - and a stark reminder that without Trump on the ballot in 2026, our voters could stay home while the radical Left turns out in force._** **Is enthusiasm fading? Are we taking our wins for granted after an incredible year?** I'm worried. Next year's midterms will be all about turnout, and we can't afford complacency. That's why I'm asking personally: [**_Can you reaffirm your commitment to our team and our party by chipping in $10 or more today?_**](https://trk.email.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9ERUNFTUJFUi1QUkVTU1VSRS1WMl8yMDI1MTIxNyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPUVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PURFQ0VNQkVSLVBSRVNTVVJFLVYyJnV0bV9hZ2VuY3k9UEcmYW1vdW50PTc1In0/D9o6tQrYdtE1GWow8vCxug) This isn't just a donation - it's your signal to my team that our movement is alive and ready to fight. We'll gauge our grassroots strength by the response to this email.
---
[ **Chip in $10 or more to show you're still in** ](https://trk.email.tedcruz._dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1FLUhvdXNlRmlsZSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249Q3J1el9ERUNFTUJFUi1QUkVTU1VSRS1WMl8yMDI1MTIxNyZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPUVtYWlsJnV0bV9jb250ZW50PURFQ0VNQkVSLVBSRVNTVVJFLVYyJnV0bV9hZ2VuY3k9UEcmYW1vdW50PTc1In0/yzlcV4z8Y57wQDjp2HoSIQ)
---
Together, we'll prove the doubters wrong - again. Thanks,
Sen. Ted Cruz

name: Tim Walz
subject: Commonsense gun violence prevention
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
# [](https://act.timwalz.com/go/195?refcode=em20251217-305&t=1&akid=305%2E34582%2Ed3G9oY)
Peter,
Republican legislators refused to hold a vote on gun safety measures supported by nearly 70% of Minnesotans — **so I’m taking action on the weapons that have caused terror and heartbreak for our state.**
Yesterday, I signed the first executive actions taken by a Minnesota Governor focused on common-sense gun violence prevention.
We’re laying the groundwork to hold gun manufacturers accountable, expanding education, training, and best practices on extreme risk protection orders, and partnering with the Minnesota Department of Education and local schools to promote safe storage.
**Let me be clear: Minnesotans are demanding change — and these executive actions are just the first step. Republicans in the Legislature need to show courage and hold a vote on banning assault weapons and high-capacity magazines.**
**[Peter, if you’re with me and the majority of Minnesotans, will you chip in $5 today and help my reelection campaign so we can continue this critical fight?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251217.nRktuR&amount=5&refcode=em20251217-305&t=2&akid=305%2E34582%2Ed3G9oY)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251217.nRktuR&amount=5&refcode=20251217-305&t=21&akid=305%2E34582%2Ed3G9oY)**
I’m grateful,
Tim Walz

**[Donate ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251217-305&t=22&refcode2=305_34582_d3G9oY&akid=305%2E34582%2Ed3G9oY)**

name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Re: Helping the most vulnerable neighbors
body:
During this time of increased need in our communities, your support will lift up families. | | |
---
Friend, It’s the holiday season and our team is out in the community, ensuring no child and no family has to go without. We joined the Westland Goodfellows in packing gift bags to fulfill holiday wish lists for kids and families.**And here are our staff and volunteers wrapping gifts for families we’re sponsoring this holiday season:** [****](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbttxGUujCfoZbY4IsntvOG6LRQnKog6uOL7AMducsfZtCK4G-5oYESrwpMBSaiyGBV1-EyWmyLy9y7wXj7LB7fn4I-oosM-TL10U89PZXxv4yz7tT8Bh671gAGVSuiAVWQHrKiQwsLPcKe-HDnm0aPJbzYJ-TsZ7m5knBEf_v4slN7jhV4r2gGc9LuSa4ef1xvO6Z2T2Gzg-fvNXtWFOb-uMLyK6VJTVhRLqRIUypH5binOXoByoxUoV7fkm34tau0X7jhLJLBRl_P1I-BPmt7OEvQNVLY_CbT0-Ry-yZiXKKi58QHt79lNDpXjVTDSeG_yDW4_W9psmQuZRK_j1sMzcOmvu7yERfex5YkGAx_ChWWyt2Nc8UTl86K2puFSLvi/4mi/rqaTbbaxQTis_zYIykbfFQ/h0/h001.BYw-xmD044zMStgebSsmZ5tgPe_spNSrJ54T3aaJ3Hk) Each month, our Rooted in Community Leadership PAC is supporting impactful nonprofits, community organizations, and campaigns through volunteerism and direct financial support—donating $100,000 this past year. \---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: **Rashida Tlaib******
Date: Tue, Dec 16, 2025
Subject: Helping the most vulnerable neighbors
To: dpwillis67@gmail.com
Friend,
Each month, our team partners with local nonprofits and organizations to invest in our communities’ needs. Here are a few examples from this past year of our service work:
* **Packing hundreds of food boxes and hot meals** with various partners including the People’s Pantry; Yes Sir, Yes Ma’am; and City Covenant Baptist Church

name: Dr. Ami Bera
subject: Republicans refuse to act to keep healthcare affordable
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bera for Congress
body:
Our nation is in the middle of a healthcare crisis — and it is set to get even worse.

| Hi Peter,

Our nation is in the middle of a healthcare crisis — and it is set to get even worse.

For months, Republicans have refused to take meaningful action to protect America’s healthcare system or provide certainty to families bracing for steep price increases.

Starting on January 1, millions of Americans will face unaffordable premiums. In California alone, an estimated 2.3 million people will see their health care costs soar, forcing far too many to forgo coverage entirely.

Trump and Congressional Republicans are asleep at the wheel as this affordability crisis grows. Families cannot wait. We need to extend these subsidies now and work toward durable reforms of our health care system to make sure every American has access to affordable, quality health care.
