name: Jasmine Crockett
subject: It’s official: I’m running for Senate.
body:
Y’all, it’s Jasmine.
I’m reaching out with big news, and I wanted you to hear it from me first: I’m officially running for the United States Senate here in Texas.
Let me be clear: With our current wannabe dictator in the White House, I’m not launching this campaign to play nice. I’m doing it to fight — because Texas deserves someone who doesn’t back down when the bullies come for her. It deserves someone who fights back.
But to win this race, we’ll have to take on Donald Trump, the Texas Republican political machine, and all of their shady special interest donors. And to be blunt, that won’t come cheap.
That’s why I’m asking you to become one of my founding donors as we launch this historic grassroots campaign. I’m counting on your support now more than ever before so that we can hit the ground running and finally flip Texas blue. Will you answer the call by splitting a donation between my campaign and Real Texas? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jasmine Crockett and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1&amount=25]
Let me be clear: When there’s a fight worth having, I don’t flinch. I don’t shrink. I don’t back down.
Just ask the communities I’ve fought for as a civil-rights attorney, the defendants I’ve represented as a public defender, and the people of Dallas that I serve in Congress. I’m a lifelong fighter for justice.
So Donald Trump can run his mouth all he wants. All his attacks show is that he’s terrified of what I represent — and what I’m bringing to the Senate when we win this race: truth, accountability, and a future where power answers to the people.
If you believe that insults won’t silence us — if you believe in a Texas that stands for justice, equity, and real representation — I need you. We’re launching a campaign to change the game. But real campaigns need real support. That’s why I’d be so grateful if you could pitch in $10, $25, or more to help us hit the ground running. Can I count on you to split a donation between my campaign and Real Texas ? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Jasmine Crockett and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
DONATE $5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=5]
DONATE $10 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=10]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=100]
DONATE $250 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?express_lane=true&refcode=120825_1&amount=250]
Other Amount [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-jasmine?refcode=120825_1&amount=25]
Thank you,
Jasmine

Only U.S. citizens or legal permanent residents are eligible to contribute. An Individual can contribute up to $5,000 to The Real Texas PAC per calendar year. Married couples may together give a total of $10,000 per calendar year. Federal political action committees (PACs) can contribute up to $5,000 per calendar year. The first $5,000 contributed in a calendar year from federally permissible sources will be deposited in The Real Texas PAC's contribution account. Once a federally permissible source has contributed $5,000 in a calendar year, The Real Texas PAC will transfer any amounts in excess of $5,000 to its non-contribution account. All contributions from corporations and labor unions will be deposited in the non-contribution account. Please contact us at realtexas@mbacg.com if you would like a different allocation.

name: From Trump
subject: Friend, are you STILL with me?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc
body:
Before I address the nation tonight, just say YES!
| | | | [](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
---
In just a short time, I will address the nation.

The cameras will be rolling. The media will be watching. And what I’m about to say will not be business as usual.
[GET BEHIND TRUMP!](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
I can’t tell you everything yet — but I can tell you this:

**A major truth is about to come out.** **Something they never wanted exposed.** **Something that could change history.**
[GET BEHIND TRUMP!](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
But here’s the truth they don’t want you to hear:

The pressure on me right now is enormous. The attacks are relentless.

**And I cannot move forward unless I know the American people are standing with me.[Friend, are you STILL with me?>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)**
[STAND WITH TRUMP](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
Before I step up and speak - before this truth bomb drops - I need to hear from YOU.

**Not later.** **Not after the headlines.** **NOW.**

[STAND WITH TRUMP](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
As 2025 comes to an end, one thing is crystal clear:

America is coming back. The momentum is real.
And Make America is no longer a promise – it’s happening.

**[Friend, before all eyes are on me, I need to know — are you still with me? Make it crystal clear here.>](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)**
Thank you,
President Donald J. Trump
[](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
[STAND WITH TRUMP](https://link.win.donaldjtrump.)
|

clean_body: Before I address the nation tonight, just say YES! | | | | In just a short time, I will address the nation. The cameras will be rolling. The media will be watching. And what I’m about to say will not be business as usual. I can’t tell you everything yet — but I can tell you this: A major truth is about to come out. Something they never wanted exposed. Something that could change history. But here’s the truth they don’t want you to hear: The pressure on me right now is enormous. The attacks are relentless. And I cannot move forward unless I know the American people are standing with me. Before I step up and speak - before this truth bomb drops - I need to hear from YOU. Not later. Not after the headlines. NOW. As 2025 comes to an end, one thing is crystal clear: America is coming back. The momentum is real. And Make America is no longer a promise – it’s happening. Thank you, President Donald J. Trump | | | | | | | | | | Paid for by Never Surrender, Inc. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate authorized’s committee. . | | P.O. BOX 13570 ARLINGTON, VA 22219 Click here to change your subscription status.

body:
We’re working to raise $50,000 before December 31 to recruit and train the next class of Emerge women who will defend our rights, flip the seats Democrats must win, and carry this Blue Stampede straight into the midterms.

| Peter,

This year, something powerful took shape across the country: a Blue Stampede.

Nearly 300 Emerge women were on the ballot in 20 states. 223 won.

This wasn’t luck. This was a trained, prepared, community-rooted pipeline of women stepping up in force — flipping seats, holding the line, and changing who holds power in America.

_**And in 2026, that Blue Stampede can grow even bigger.**_

subject: Thank our postal workers this holiday season

body:
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
Folks,
Postal workers have continued to deliver for us through a particularly brutal year.
Their official motto is “neither snow nor rain nor heat nor gloom of night” will stop the Postal Service, but they also have delivered through aggressive political attacks, job cuts, service slowdowns, and nonstop pressure from Donald Trump, who calls the USPS a “tremendous loser” and tried to shove it under the Commerce Department and fire the board to pave the way for privatization.
Yet they deliver for all of us. Let’s deliver messages of gratitude directly to postal workers across the country, on their routes, in sorting centers, at distribution hubs, and in local post offices.
Sign the card and add your words of support to thank our postal workers across the country today >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
During the holiday season, their busiest time of the year, USPS workers move over 15 billion pieces of mail and packages, including more than 400 million packages in a single week, to every single ZIP code in the country.
Today, we can boost their spirits, and show them that the country values their service. When you sign, you can also write a personal note to share with workers. Our coalition partners at the American Postal Workers Union will deliver our messages of thanks directly to postal workers across the country.
Add your name and a personal message of thanks by clicking here >> [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
SIGN HERE NOW ⇨ [https://actionnetwork.org/forms/thank-postal-workers/?source=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&referrer=group-integrity-first-voter-action-project&redirect=https%3A%2F%2Fsecure.actblue.com%2Fdonate%2Fifvap-actions]
Voter Action Project [https://voteractionproject.org/]
https://www.facebook.com/voteraction [https://www.facebook.com/voteraction] https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org [https://bsky.app/profile/voteractionproject.org] https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/ [https://www.instagram.com/voteractionproj/] https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/ [https://www.linkedin.com/company/voter-action-project/]
Integrity First Voter Action Project is dedicated to expanding ballot access and increasing voter participation across the country.
Since the Supreme Court gutted key provisions in the Voting Rights Act, extreme politicians have taken advantage to pass harsh laws that restrict access to the ballot box. These restrictions particularly affect disadvantaged communities and people of color. It’s our goal to bring awareness to these laws wherever they may crop up and organize voters to take action to ensure the right to vote is available to everyone in their states and communities.
Your support is crucial to our efforts. Will you donate today to help us protect voting rights? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121725_2&amount=20]
Donate Now [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/integrity-first-voter-action-project-1?refcode=121725_2&amount=20]
Contributions or gifts to Integrity First Voter Action Project are not tax deductible as charitable contributions or as business expenses under IRC Section 162(3). Integrity First Voter Action Project is organized and operates under section 501(c)(4) of the Internal Revenue Code.
A copy of Integrity First Voter Action Project's latest financial report may be obtained by mail at Integrity First Voter Action Project, c/o Mele Brengarth & Assoc, PO Box 15845, Washington, DC 20003.
Paid for by Integrity First Voter Action Project, www.voteractionproject.org, and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.
Integrity First Voter Action Project
P.O. Box 19294
Portland, OR 97280
United States
This email was sent to: dpwillis67@gmail.com. Emails are a crucial way to keep supporters up to date on the latest attacks on voting rights and our efforts to expand ballot access for all Americans. If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.everyaction.com/p/FQb7Rbo110qLPmSvMAn63Q2?unsubscribedata=eBtWozm8m8GwOpyJOSw7zJYfWrs%2BGZa7SwjbegCwrmk0UsJS3XxGzi0RIbxU8Cz57r6iTGc7Kkfh8MZGU6y4bAR4AP2LaAV4ZZBKA8Fk6%2BrFdGmzT5DZmxw1O%2FkTBdtnwr2SDZ9jSVGvZAIYK03uHf%2BrN3AXDVxaOKw2b%2F8D8TFdxGP0YkOZY%2F8uS795B2Wi7WYiawhr%2FcXD4Do7pt%2FioIevl52cGcmdlTiq%2Fj6TomeWcjEM85GZAt1yaN4Qp2cjsHvb4f%2FRKAe54CUutMjieC9v3ZK%2BK8l2zBafoqIXp2CEqmO2cM2Qqhk9ZRtFaGSexBllIlKUtr9AUdiAMtLk0%2B9umSyL%2B8oXWuUjum25cba0v5oKQaUjzgqtnhRpc4ZukKxeTVo0gxyom1OTEX0A%2BE6biJHIO4CivL2TRLyp3JM%3D .

name: Team Mandela Barnes
subject: ‘Indications’ Tim Michels will run
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mandela for Wisconsin
body:
Yes, MULTI-MILLIONAIRE Tim Michels
| Hi, Republicans are lining up to compete for this Governor's seat — **this time it’s rumored that MAGA favorite and multi-millionaire Tim Michels might join in.** **[If that’s enough to convince you to contribute today, you can click here to help Mandela fight back.](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_121725_B1_Michels&t=2&akid=5721%2E196590%2E_pJCGQ)** The last time he ran for governor, Tim Michels had the full backing of Donald Trump. He also spent almost _$12 million_ of his own money for his campaign. Michels thought he could buy his way into the governor’s mansion — Wisconsinites proved him wrong. Because power shouldn’t just belong to the people who have the biggest bank accounts — it belongs to the people. Our campaign is about rejecting the Washington Way and uniting people around the Wisconsin Way: protecting healthcare, higher wages, and lower costs. We stopped Tim Michels once before, and we’ll do it again if we have the resources to compete with his bank account. Early polling shows that Mandela would be favored to win against Michels, but if he spent nearly $12 million of his own money on his last run for governor, we can’t take anything for granted. **[Will you chip in $12 to show our Republican opponents the power of grassroots support from across Wisconsin?](https://act.mandelabarnes.com/go/1212?c0=mb-em-fr-dec25.SSbw4W&amount=15&refcode=MB_EM_FR_121725_B1_Michels&t=3&akid=5721%2E196590%2E_pJCGQ)** Thank you,
Team Mandela

name: Troy Downing
subject: I'm staring defeat in the face.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Troy Downing for Congress
body:
I need help TONIGHT

[](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)I'm staring defeat in the face.
As a combat veteran, Christian, husband, and father, I thought I could count on fellow Patriots like you to stand with me against the radical Left. But it hasn’t panned out like I hoped.
**I'm Troy Downing, a freshman GOP Congressman fighting to put America First.**
Our House Majority hangs by a thread – just a few seats separate us from the Democrats' socialist agenda.
I'm facing a brutal truth: the November deadline is looming, and my campaign could be over if we fail to meet our goal. Democrats are waiting to pounce with their massive cash advantage.
_We can't let them see weakness now._
I'm not asking for a bailout or a massive donation. **[I just need a small contribution from patriots like you to keep my campaign alive:](https://trk.emails.troydowning._cmVjdXJyaW5nPXRydWUmYW1vdW50PTM1JmV4X3RpZD0yMDI1MTIxN19CWC00Ti4xMTAxMTdfdDE3NzU4NzQtMjg5OSZ1dG1fY2FtcGFpZ249MjAyNTEyMTdfQlgtNE4uMTEwMTE3X3QxNzc1ODc0LTI4OTkifQ/hqss7EIA8wXA1kdK11CE2A)**[ SEND A CARE PACKAGE NOW
](https://trk.emails.troydowning.)Without your help, I'll be forced to surrender this critical House seat to the radical Left. But if you chip in right now, you’ll help deliver a victory over the Democrats' agenda.
We’re not just aiming to win an election– We’re in this for our country's future.
[So please, will you make a small contribution to help a combat veteran take down the Democrat machine?](https://trk.emails.troydowning._-m0zlpKDQqufbr_AiBZQ)
Your support is my last line of defense.
God Bless You and God Bless America,
Troy Downing | |

name: Jon Ossoff Campaign HQ
subject: Big deadline coming up and we could use your help
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jon Ossoff for Senate
body:
#

We wish we didn’t have to send these pesky emails asking for money.
We’d much rather be telling you about all the work Jon is doing to stand up to the Trump Administration and push back against the corruption in Washington. But the truth is, these emails are absolutely critical to our campaign.
Thanks to _Citizens United_ opening the floodgates for dark money in our elections, the reality is that winning a competitive Senate race like Georgia’s is going to take hundreds of millions of dollars.
And Jon won’t beg federal lobbyists or corporate PACs to cut him a check. Instead, our campaign is powered by folks like you giving $5, $10, $25 at a time to requests like this one.
So even though we wish we didn’t have to ask, here it is:
**[Can you please donate $5 to Jon’s reelection campaign today? We’ve got a big FEC fundraising deadline coming up at the end of the month, and Jon is counting on us to step up before it’s too late.**](https://act.electjon.com/go/32?c0=ossoff-em-dec25.lXrKfY&refcode=em251217-1-f-b&t=1&akid=1293%2E5691347%2ECw2V2B)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

body:

|
Peter,
They say power reveals a man.
Right now, it's revealing a man who is going completely batshit insane.
We are watching the mental unraveling of Donald Trump in real-time. It's not embarrassing anymore, it's frightening and dangerous.
In the past 48 hours, Trump danced on the grave of director Rob Reiner, who was tragically murdered alongside his wife by their own son, implying he died because of his "Trump Derangement Syndrome." Then, he turned the White House into a wall of petty grievances, installing plaques under portraits of Joe Biden and Barack Obama filled with unhinged insults.

Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important?

If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are _**desperate**_ to hear the unfiltered truth.

**This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for:**

* Free market capitalism
* Respect for achievement and success
* Free speech
* The future of young people and our great country

Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles.

clean_body: I’m sending you an urgent message about an organization I’m personally supporting, The Atlas Society. I’m personally supporting The Atlas Society—please read and consider donating. | This email is unlike anything I've ever sent you before. No, this isn't about a campaign or an election. I'm sending you an urgent message about an organization I'm personally supporting, The Atlas Society. Their CEO, Jennifer Grossman, has been my friend for thirty years -- we got our start in cable news together in the mid-1990s, and she later hired my firm to do polling when she worked at Dole Food Company. Through ups and downs, we've remained friends. Today, she's the CEO of The Atlas Society, a non-profit organization whose mission is to spread Ayn Rand's message of capitalism, personal responsibility, reason, and entrepreneurship to young people. Why is The Atlas Society's mission so important? If my career in polling and serving as President Trump's 2016 Campaign Manager taught me anything, it's this: Americans are desperate to hear the unfiltered truth. This is why we need more young people to hear The Atlas Society 's message. Here is what Jennifer and her organization are fighting for: Free market capitalism Respect for achievement and success Free speech The future of young people and our great country Our culture is pushing a twisted message of victimization, envy, woke nonsense, socialism, class warfare, and hatred for our country and its founding principles. Young people need an alternative. The Atlas Society spreads Ayn Rand's message to millions of young people via social media, video content, graphic novels, conferences, and so much more. I promised Jennifer I would help her raise $25,000 for The Atlas Society, which is why I am reaching out to you. Please consider making a donation by using one of the links I've copied below: You may be surprised, but when young people hear Ayn Rand's message of entrepreneurship, refusing to be a victim, and personal achievement, they are inspired and want to learn more. Yes, politics are important. But politics are downstream from culture. And culture is downstream from philosophy. The Atlas Society is on the frontlines of fighting in the philosophical culture war, which may determine the fate of the country's future. We CANNOT give up the culture or give up young people. This is more important than a campaign or a single election. You and I must invest in reaching young people with this message. Please don't wait to take action. My goal is to raise this $25,000 ASAP within a 48-hour period. Can I count on you to make a donation? Kellyanne Conway, Former Senior Counselor to President Trump The Atlas Society is a 501(c)(3) Not For Profit Organization. All donations are tax-deductible. 1038 Miwok Dr Lodi, CA 95240

name: gop-alert@cadem.org
subject: Republicans aren’t surrendering — neither can we.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the California Democratic Party | www
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-footer?refcode=header&amount=5]
Hi there — We need to talk about something Darrell Issa just admitted publicly, because it reveals exactly what we’re up against in 2026 .
After reports that he considered moving to Texas to run in one of their rigged GOP-drawn districts, Issa instead declared:
“I’m not giving up on California, and hopefully none of my colleagues that have been drawn into tough districts are going to give up. I’m hoping they all run.” CHIP IN $ >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-dec25?&refcode=&amount=]Make no mistake: Republicans aren’t surrendering. In fact, leaders like Rep. Issa are doubling down.
And that means if we want to flip the U.S. House in 2026, California Democrats must fight harder than ever.
Will you step up today with a donation of $ to build the organizing power we’ll need to defeat Issa and his MAGA allies? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-dec25?&refcode=&amount=]
Passing Prop 50 ensured that we still can win the House majority.
But Texas’ rigged maps still exist. The Supreme Court just upheld them. Donald Trump and his MAGA cronies have used every dirty trick to engineer this national advantage.
So what’s their next move? Hold every California district possible — even the competitive ones where Democrats might be favored.
Issa’s decision wasn’t offhand. It was a rallying cry to vulnerable Republican incumbents:
➡️ “Stay in the game.”
➡️ “Hold your ground.”
➡️ “California is too important for Republicans to lose.”
Which is exactly why we must make California the center of the national fight for the House. Even if we have to fight for every single seat tooth and nail .
Will you power the fight to flip California districts and overcome Texas’ rigged map advantage? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/cdp-dec25?&refcode=&amount=]

name: Tim Kaine
subject: Something to celebrate
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Derek, it may feel like there’s not as much to celebrate this year — but I want to take a moment to talk about a really meaningful win.
I’m proud to announce that the final version of this year’s defense bill — the National Defense Authorization Act — includes a provision I’ve worked on for years to repeal the Iraq war authorizations from 1991 and 2002.
Congress sometimes starts wars and then just leaves the authorizations on the books forever — zombie authorizations .
These zombie authorizations are dangerous, because any president who wants to bypass Congress and start a war can point back to them to justify their actions.
We saw President Trump do that during his first term and President Biden do it during his presidency, too. And the fact is, no matter the political party, it’s critical that any president come to Congress before starting a war — the Constitution is clear.
Repealing these zombie authorizations has been a goal of mine for many years now. Once signed by the President, these will be the first war authorizations that have been repealed since the Gulf of Tonkin Resolution that deepened American involvement in the Vietnam War — which was repealed in the early 1970s.
Congress shouldn’t be about forever wars, and presidents shouldn’t be about unilateral wars.
I’ll continue to do all I can to make sure Congress continues to reclaim our Constitutional powers — both authorizing war, and ending war.
If you want to keep seeing wins in Congress — holding the president accountable, reclaiming congressional power, and fighting for the American people — please consider pitching in to my Common Ground PAC today to help elect leaders who get things done. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
If you’ve saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:
DONATE $25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=25]
DONATE $50 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=50]
DONATE $100 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025?express_lane=true&amount=100]
OTHER [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-cgp-2025]
Thanks for standing by me in this fight — I’m proud of this result, and I hope you are too!
—Tim Kaine

name: Mark Lamb
subject: Something bigger is broken.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS
body:
When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. |
---

[](http://links.g4yamerica.com/u/click?_t=cc7dda5c2cc64061900678c88bd3a18a&_m=202fa498f03749ef95ea123b40984fc8&_e=k4WUXHdiUy0C0byNxLoHJjZbyPO8otGL6ppYciaxin7mqYF60W7ipLK7k3S9ZB4XAIQRkee30a671V3-Lnqp9aO0Ggut-eBMkJ6jZH6_REQORm7XxINnY-uu8C2BT4X-EhB8ThXigYwCFTA6sB34L0_CzC5PxrPkhagiy-Eg6vsW8YVRIGPyP2mhrPryCNczy-lNXOb5h_jjlBYyz5oANeVNGliqHubxBgxBHXPem3j-eesTl7oqWLGkw2iwJCzD6K2RhdoE_QiTWbsVqIOzin4OR2jXn1P3LcO8WKWJii8iVenPrmj8jJUbkRr_PkUBtjBSdhiMI3Fsup6x4ZH_OeVl_iZpMgQA6ZCPR1IIlHypNXdE8bfTR2GkiT5jV-rfAQVHU_t4w4BIHqSzMZDA3-FDcO1RGrB_yihqiGWo0ufk_XV_QjcIMWb0yip3Tkimf5-wqHZOQg6X1GnHVhSFJIbtqQtA8AQY_hOaEI8VeTD4bpdDGRqo8rPArQj6mbbb6B_8AFDFx0PtE_EouSmug8PcznhZxroYHn6Mqm5lXnTKDDfCdpqZYnjjIdrKt0Mt7_hGIihk2GBzmISNYgx3fx9_bHqwobjr4UKqmhBcYeGUrBx5WYI-G0IziOCveL0tMADhoM58y_9HAx4wvZGBa7bDkxQigPrgVHnGLYKTUYA%3D)

Fellow Patriot,

Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself:

When something is broken, you don't complain - _you fix it._

That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient.

But today, something bigger is broken.

Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. _The people_.

The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom.

They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough.

**That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it.** To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone.

clean_body: When something is broken, you don't complain — you fix it. | Fellow Patriot, Long before I ever put on a badge, I made a simple promise to myself: When something is broken, you don't complain - you fix it. That philosophy guided me through eight years as Sheriff. It's why I stood up to cartels, why I defended our border communities, and why I backed our deputies even when it wasn't politically convenient. But today, something bigger is broken. Washington has stopped listening to the people who make this country work. Not the lobbyists. Not the bureaucrats. The people. The ranchers who wake up before dawn. The small business owners who sign both sides of the paycheck. The parents who are fighting to protect their kids' future. The families who still believe in God, Family, and Freedom. They deserve a voice that won't fold when things get tough. That's why I'm running for Congress - not to join the political class, but to challenge it. To restore the kind of leadership that answers to the people, not to the unelected and unaccountable. And I'm going to fight for that every single day. But I can't do it alone. If you believe America needs leaders who show up, tell the truth, and stand firm - Thank you for being in this fight, Mark Lamb Republican for Congress, AZ-5 Join Mark Lamb on social media! - | | PAID FOR BY MARK LAMB FOR CONGRESS Don't want to receive any more of our emails? We hate to see you go: .

[****With less than two weeks left in the year, we can’t afford to fall short on any goals – especially as we head into an election year.** **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121477916/589586414/318555295?amount=6&refcode=email_121725_missedgoal&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWU1NWY4YS04NWRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vg0h_hMdcNmsTklS07v_RYRybncw7AZVKgGLTmkteNo=)****[Can you chip in $6 to help us finish the month strong?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121477917/589586415/318555295?amount=6&refcode=email_121725_missedgoal&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWU1NWY4YS04NWRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vg0h_hMdcNmsTklS07v_RYRybncw7AZVKgGLTmkteNo=)**** **[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121477918/589586416/318555295?amount=6&refcode=email_121725_missedgoal&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlOWU1NWY4YS04NWRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=Vg0h_hMdcNmsTklS07v_RYRybncw7AZVKgGLTmkteNo=)** We’re sorry to be so direct, but hitting goals like this is critical. Here’s why: National Republicans have been targeting Andrea’s seat for YEARS, and have spent MILLIONS doing so. And, with just three seats standing between us and a Democratic House majority, best believe, they’re gearing up to do so again in 2026. name: Mark Kelly
subject: Really?
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
I asked Pete Hegseth specific things, and instead of answering he started lecturing me about lawful and unlawful orders. Yeah, really.
#

It is my job, as a United States Senator, to conduct oversight on the executive branch.
As part of that oversight, I attended a classified briefing with Pete Hegseth and others on the U.S. military strikes in the Caribbean.
I won’t get into the details, but I came prepared with substantial and serious questions. I asked him specific things about this military operation, and instead of answering he started lecturing me about lawful and unlawful orders.
Yeah, really.
Pete didn’t want to answer my questions because he doesn’t have good answers. He’s unqualified and out of his depth. What he’d rather do is try and remind me that he’s directed the Pentagon to investigate me for repeating something every servicemember learns in training: you have to refuse illegal orders.
And it’s worth noting he’s on tape publicly saying the same thing. On Fox News. About Donald Trump. Just 9 years ago.
It would be laughable if this wasn’t so serious. He was trying to intimidate me into silence — even in a classified briefing, with no cameras to perform for.
Not gonna work.
I was elected to this job twice, first in 2020 and again in 2022. The people of Arizona sent me to Washington to represent them, to be their voice in government.
Nobody is going to stop me from doing that job, especially not somebody like Pete Hegseth.
Thanks,
Mark
**[DONATE](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-em-dec25/.s2Rl9G&refcode=em251217-2-1mil&t=1&akid=6301%2E6688874%2EBLfVo4)**

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=2&akid=6301%2E6688874%2EBLfVo4).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

A Tesla Explosion That Changes Everything

Near Trump Tower in Las Vegas, a Tesla Cybertruck exploded—and it’s raising serious questions.
| [ ](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=2a274baa874746bcbb8be7ad449ef50a&_e=aorLObbMQ7VGf2tmSMyzunIDiQFPCJ5rQsRoEGYs4bNlw_7MvXWSiUu0oE04GEkX-SM6r-8hUvpiBKh7CQb0LqYGDPicLroitC95UoW7qxybp2jgTkuA9Nyni5PTd5SkaoKNo85BOE1sRiL-PNhDUhJtYVf61sO4OeLF-9c2kxoe-2eqmlMEw06uZogHnEO0otRT76E0IgxUS6SLGEsQAhW0_YiN18oHLrnCrDmJHyo7xyN12Y13CVg3qCX8C8SoRHPMZLn9R4oOA84D_DIw9g0gRhT8J_8EB8RpScM9TCpvrigAp-KVYNOp1WTYotpDm_NWfo2MSuMP6It4K_oBkYASMH84hc8SKSkr27l-cyfbOUu7scxwDJbUytF255JTYzNWyOqZZWwFsnx9HM95ryUH-Z267lCpOz6EDIxD6yvrTt9UV1UhPnK4yxDRXiWDAK-GcLRNJ3SO3Qbzr6mvRy2YRtBJURdnEoFBi41_0TXJjwuSpGB1NZmZArctAdVyQu4GQ9n8wPzhdLcX7SCx5AyC1IgRuzRoDlz7uTEUWbmANdfbrqf3dBdgvwdtYZthcrbyQSeMYcgJcAR45G5lXw%3D%3D)

The details are _shocking_. Gas canisters. Fireworks. A planned detonation.

But here’s the twist...

There’s something bigger behind this story—something most people haven’t noticed.

Do you see it?

[CLICK HERE to uncover the truth.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=2a274baa874746bcbb8be7ad449ef50a&_e=aorLObbMQ7VGf2tmSMyzunIDiQFPCJ5rQsRoEGYs4bNlw_7MvXWSiUu0oE04GEkX-SM6r-8hUvpiBKh7CQb0LqYGDPicLroitC95UoW7qxybp2jgTkuA9Nyni5PTd5SkaoKNo85BOE1sRiL-PNhDUhJtYVf61sO4OeLF-9c2kxoe-2eqmlMEw06uZogHnEO0O69f72SvH-hK2rXT4Q9OXd5YbE7M6mOk8pZpWQg-ZoDjbB2eyplB38YKk7NqnOVnFzkwOM_U-dT88eCSVM-9bCn6fYCKEay-YqMeaq430FfT2cBHF3giz0NDNbP_sD0BWPwdnI1E_cf_5sVo_vwbx37r8FXRm_cNOQQLrf9DtykjS9cveexK5nYVomeBP0ceyW9zbayxfJoSg0KQBBKgRxnnflDnWjnjY511ZREkSaDs5_dqHJVIY-sMiWYuOYSjdHpM6X7seFY6TxIViKFmoa2mvykjdyJaD-XezLF0PO_KOiZLwjDDZKDc3hhyB_pUcC-037eFH3LyijjsXvO_KBrM2-CgPzmJOjhGCXD3Pb7xWrX9gJmoQutq2OhnHOQe0RmptxMcemQX4FUnCks8zQ%3D%3D)

You won’t believe what’s really going on.
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. name: Cori Bush
subject: My message to AIPAC:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori For Us
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251217_AIPACmessage_eoy1_actrs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251217_AIPACmessage_eoy1_actrs]
AIPAC poured $9 million into St. Louis last cycle to lie about me and silence our movement.
But they made a mistake. They thought giving me time off would slow me down. They were wrong.
Now they’ve got a fight on their hands. I’m not just running for me, I’m running for all of us. For progressives, for the people, for everyone who stands up against the forces trying to crush progress. I’m running for YOU.
Last time I was in Congress, I flipped a few tables and ruffled some feathers in the name of good trouble on behalf of the people of St. Louis.
Now, we’re getting back up, stronger, louder, and ready to finish the work we started: lowering costs, protecting our neighbors, and standing up for St. Louis.
Our primary election is August 4, 2026 . It sounds far away, but I promise it’s closer than you think. Especially when billionaires are pouring money into my opponent’s campaign, hoping to drown out my voice again.
The reality is, it’s big money versus a movement they could never buy. We’re ready to win, and we need you on the ground, knocking doors, making calls, and connecting with voters.
And with our ONLY end-of-year deadline of the cycle in just TWO WEEKS, we can't afford to slow down now.
Can I count on you to join the movement today by donating $25, or whatever you can afford, to be part of the fight to bring hope and dignity to families all across St. Louis? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/upl_cab_email-25q4?refcode=x_251217_AIPACmessage_eoy1_actrs&amount=25]

In solidarity,
— Cori

name: Josh Gottheimer
subject: Happy holiday season!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JOSH GOTTHEIMER FOR CONGRESS
body:
# [#] Team,

As we gather with family and friends to celebrate the holidays, I wanted to wish you and your family a happy holiday season and a hopeful new year.

The holiday season is all about community and spending time with the people you love. And this year, I am especially grateful for the people here in New Jersey and for my supporters, like you, who have helped me truly make a meaningful difference in Congress.

Thank you for being a part of my team and helping me to continue fighting for hardworking families, bipartisan progress, and more. I am confident we will continue this impactful work in 2026 and deliver real results to the American people.

I hope you get the love, comfort, and rest you deserve after all your hard work this year. Enjoy the holidays, and here’s to a bright 2026 filled with progress and possibility.

Yours,
Josh
Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. clean_body: [#] Team, As we gather with family and friends to celebrate the holidays, I wanted to wish you and your family a happy holiday season and a hopeful new year. The holiday season is all about community and spending time with the people you love. And this year, I am especially grateful for the people here in New Jersey and for my supporters, like you, who have helped me truly make a meaningful difference in Congress. Thank you for being a part of my team and helping me to continue fighting for hardworking families, bipartisan progress, and more. I am confident we will continue this impactful work in 2026 and deliver real results to the American people. I hope you get the love, comfort, and rest you deserve after all your hard work this year. Enjoy the holidays, and here’s to a bright 2026 filled with progress and possibility. Yours, Josh Josh Gottheimer represents New Jersey’s Fifth Congressional District. Josh is in Congress to fight for lower taxes and Jersey Values. He’s working across the aisle to find real solutions for New Jersey families and is actively fighting against the ideological gridlock in Washington. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

This is a paid advertisement for AMASS’s Regulation CF offering. Please read the offering circular at [https://invest.amassbrands.com](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=d761b3a762cc40e7915ce69aa1a70931&_e=V9HnVNAepkFEXjRoKSsfeVlFpCRPtOv9lJMsP3EyTYbPl3wscuFb_lwIsgmdQMvSTbHPYBhSn19JUdOIPt_2mlG939IAWDQZ6YeyNBBubDJpLiWVIRINC-NACZBKpE---_j4glOZNR8ndRBexawaErTNfsDUC0HK0YcoM3XYIW6Of9qodTDP4BEOFnJaJ-i-o99hi0JnN8g3lX7JWAtAeUFNEVin0tCnxIj2aTz4z0XqkTuOZTOKDIENxkwhF0wZ3hFD-QpqvJvmc0jQF9JrqIS1sYAn3sb4PAPhEBl8w44Sesg45ng61ZZOfRoAp781H0Dyr8VI7iaKyk5E8tcGPQ%3D%3D)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

Guess which. | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Please read the offering circular at Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Chris Pappas
subject: This is a longer email
body:
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1001&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]Chris Pappas for Senate[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/126?t=1002&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]Chris Pappas for Senate

I have a lot
to tell you in this email.

If you don’t have the time to read my story but want to show your support,
[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251217_2_bio.vt-EZk&refcode=em20251217-2&t=1003&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]please chip in $5 today.

I’ve lived in Manchester my whole life. My great-grandfather started the
Puritan, a landmark establishment that’s a significant part of the
Manchester community today.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251217_2_bio.vt-EZk&refcode=em20251217-2&t=1004&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]Chris at the Puritan

The Puritan is where I got my start — working there taught me what service
means, and I carried that into my career as a public servant.

Now I want to bring that same work ethic to the Senate and fix this system
that is too broken to serve the people.

But I’m facing one of the most competitive races in 2026. [ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251217_2_bio.vt-EZk&refcode=em20251217-2&t=1005&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]Will you rush
a donation of $5 today to help me win this close election
and stop the GOP from expanding its majority?

It doesn’t matter what your politics are: The system is not working.

Trump and national Republicans are too busy fighting to line the pockets
of their billionaire friends instead of helping our neighbors who need it.

They’re giving tax cuts to the ultra-wealthy while slashing health care
benefits. They’re building a gilded ballroom in the White House while
Granite Staters continue worrying about housing security.

It’s time to change that. That’s why I’m running for Senate.

Together we can take on the biggest challenges. We can fight for working
families, lower costs, defend reproductive freedom, and stand up to MAGA
extremism — and win.

But I’m counting on your support to make that happen.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251217_2_bio.vt-EZk&refcode=em20251217-2&t=1006&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]Please, pitch in $5 to support my
campaign today. We can’t keep NH blue without your help.

[ https://act.chrispappas.org/go/181?c0=em_ms_cp_20251217_2_bio.vt-EZk&amount=5&refcode=em20251217-2&t=1025&akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw ]Donate $5 »

Thank you for all that you do.

Let’s do this,

Chris Pappas

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://act.chrispappas.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=4071%2E582294%2E1cSWgw

clean_body: If Boebert truly supported the Epstein Files release, why would she get so upset when new information is made public? | | | | | == | Peter, We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island. Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media. Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't): | == Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person. Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter. A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings. Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county. == If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | == | == | == | | | == | == | == | == | Thank you, Team Trisha Calvarese | | | | == | Mail checks to: Trisha 4 CO 9609 S University Blvd., PO Box 631322 Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 | Paid for by Trisha 4 Colorado Trisha Calvarese for Congress | 9609 S University Blvd. PO Box 631322 | Highlands Ranch, CO 80163 US | |

Peter,
We have long called for all of the Epstein Files to be released to the public. That should include any and all photo evidence, written communications, and especially the client list that Epstein and Ghislaine Maxwell kept to track what big name celebrities (and politicians) "partied" on the island.
Strangely, since Donald Trump begrudgingly signed the Epstein discharge petition last month, Lauren Boebert doesn't seem terribly interested in transparency, according to her own social media.
Boebert got oddly defensive after House Oversight Democrats released new photos showing private areas of Epstein's island mansion, melting down about the release and falsely claiming they were old photos (they aren't):

Lauren Boebert can't be trusted to do the right thing. She is a very sick person.
Carpetbagging Boebert has switched back to her default MAGA Mode to protect Trump--because without Trump's support, she stands NO chance against her hometown, Democratic opponent, Trisha Calvarese, who has consistently outraised Boebert each fundraising quarter.
A category 5 storm is brewing for Republicans like Lauren Boebert who keep following Trump off a cliff like loyal mindless lemmings.
Boebert only survived re-election by 546 votes. In 2024, her Democratic opponent Trisha Calvarese had the best performance a Democrat has ever had in this district, overperforming Kamala Harris by 6 pts while Boebert embarrassingly underperformed Trump in every single county.
[The only way we will ever get accountability and transparency over the Trump Administration is by defeating Lauren Boebert and flipping the House. name: Rep. John Ray Clemmons
subject: Join the TN House Democrats on Jan. 7th!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_
body:
This is the best chance to support the Caucus in the new year.
| | | | Can't see this email? [View in Your Browser](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCQ0RCTJ1YB4CVPT2DAEF0N2_0)
---
| | | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCQ0RCTJ1YB4CVPT2DAEF0N2_1)
---
| | | | Hi Peter, The Tennessee House Democratic Caucus Pre-Session Fundraiser is just weeks away! [We invite you to join us on Wednesday, January 7th, from 5:30 to 7:00 PM at Germantown Cafe to kick off the 2026 election cycle by showing your strong support for our Democratic leaders and candidates.](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCQ0RCTJ1YB4CVPT2DAEF0N2_2) Your continued commitment to our mutual cause is vital. See you there!
---
| | [ ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCQ0RCTJ1YB4CVPT2DAEF0N2_3)
---
| | [ RSVP HERE ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCQ0RCTJ1YB4CVPT2DAEF0N2_4)
---
| **As of January 13th at noon, our Caucus and members are prohibited from fundraising until session adjourns months later. This is why our pre-session fundraiser is so important. It is absolutely critical for us to be able to build upon our current momentum throughout session.**

2026 looks to be a strong year for Democrats, and we can make real headway towards breaking the Republican supermajority with your support.

Donate today!
---
| | [ RSVP HERE ](https://ctrk.klclick.com/l/01KCQ0RCTJ1YB4CVPT2DAEF0N2_5)
---
| Questions? Contact Abby at **abby@triumphstrategies.com** or **317-319-0015**.

Thank you very much,
Rep. John Ray Clemmons
Chair, TN House Democratic Caucus
---
| | | |
---
| _Paid for by the Tennessee House Democratic Caucus, Ronnie Glynn, Treasurer_

body:

We know what you’re thinking: _does $1 really make all that much of a difference?_
**The answer is YES. Without a doubt.** Here’s why:
Your $1 will help us outwork, outhustle, and outsmart Kansas Republicans in the final days leading up to their legislative session.
Your $1 will help us spread the word all across Kansas about this DC-backed scheme — and build grassroots opposition from Democrats, Republicans, and unaffiliated voters alike.
Your $1 will help us keep a barrier-breaking representative in Congress — not another wealthy, cookie cutter, establishment-backed National Republican.
**_[One month may seem like a long time, but it’s going to fly by fast — so we need to be prepared. name: Brown, Sherrod
subject: I never planned this
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Heartland Patriots (LucasKunce
body:
I need you with me, Peter.
Peter,
I never planned on running for office again.
But I see what’s happening in Washington – and I can’t stay on the sidelines.
**I’m jumping in this fight: I’m running for Senate in 2026.**
_Why?_ Because Ohioans are working harder than ever, and they’re still falling behind.
_Why?_ Because wages are flat.
_Why?_ Because costs are rising.
_Why?_ Because the people in power are only looking out for their rich friends and corporate interests.
No one is fighting for Ohio workers and families in the Senate. But with your support, we can change that.
We can build a country where workers share in the wealth they create, where hard work pays off, and where everyone who works hard can afford a decent life.
This won’t be easy – and with Ohio being one of the most competitive Senate races in the country, we know there are many fights ahead. But if we stand together, we will win.
**[I need you with me, Peter: Will you chip in $10 split with Heartland Patriots right now to help me flip Ohio’s Senate seat and stand up for workers across the country?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98575?refcode=em20251217-9840&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amount=10&t=1&akid=9840%2E614464%2EVJrU4Q)**
**[DONATE](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/98575?refcode=em20251217-9840&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9840%2E614464%2EVJrU4Q)**
In solidarity,
Sherrod

Grateful that my friend and former colleague Susan Wild joined us today to talk about the breakthrough on Capitol Hill when four battleground district Republicans -- three from Pennsylvania -- decided to join the Democrats to try to extend the ACA subsidies by three years. Their discharge petition will force a vote on the House floor early next year. Many obstacles remain, but, like in _Dumb and Dumber_ , we're telling you there's a chance!

name: Team Pou
subject: Cold weather ❄️ can’t mean cold fundraising
body:
Not in an EOQ month!

| |
---
Team, It’s getting cold in New Jersey... but our fundraising cannot freeze over. Not in December. Not in an end-of-quarter month. And definitely not in a race that will determine control of the House. Here’s the truth, friend: **our performance this month matters more than ever.** This district is one of the seats Democrats must hold to take back the House majority. If we want to stop the MAGA agenda, lower costs for working families, and deliver real results, it starts right here. And that means we cannot afford a single “slow day,” especially during one of the toughest fundraising periods of the year. So we’re keeping the energy high, the heaters on, and our spirits warm.
