clean_body: Not in an EOQ month! | | Team, It’s getting cold in New Jersey... but our fundraising cannot freeze over. Not in December. Not in an end-of-quarter month. And definitely not in a race that will determine control of the House. Here’s the truth, friend: our performance this month matters more than ever. This district is one of the seats Democrats must hold to take back the House majority. If we want to stop the MAGA agenda, lower costs for working families, and deliver real results, it starts right here. And that means we cannot afford a single “slow day,” especially during one of the toughest fundraising periods of the year. So we’re keeping the energy high, the heaters on, and our spirits warm. But now we need your help to keep our fundraising strong. = | = | = ---|--- = | = = | = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately. subject: He came for you. Let this devotional help you prepare your heart.
body:
Christmas is not just a time to celebrate, it’s a time to remember why we needed saving. | | | _Please see a special message from our sponsor. If you would like to unsubscribe, visit this[link](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/u/wiTXN4SqdhVCnz_BokyavJXRLHx-7j0sBFdWIMFrF2MByayVaQ1fGh1BnwKOrM2iteFc7AbAnIp7Ro7T2CK4nkr9DmDZ6H7LrPjEC1Fn_uWPKxe_MXVJ7dbkvbsBVkDBF3SXNZYf5LYgXnUnNDYgPIdUwt1_EcalAfmrQEVRim6G6Bp_8odfyVUE/jSx4t2ijPYWNS5rSzPH1Jr6m2wFnJwAG/15)_
[](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/w6IKIZAIeMiHkVRd6n1wZez7jj-EAfZtNuYdkxC_Itr0oX79XSlWOuoegy-V1-j6yIufBhABiPm6hA8kL6-Imz5bm7zmH73AxoQhXy5mVhDPM6S9F6HRZ3siSu0jJ_-2MG2YgJRRl-YcZNlY5i_bXPxvzQH7Auk09w1n2LC8GqargtPeCpzd9CW1Egul-x0D0fciMcYybpYF6LxEPAZfT3YNlqSraddZysuSA3nSVjQK8ElgcRdUHcfzbKtiY5d8jX-BeUMZZrO0e4Y99eyDJMPK6otyxaytkWXaS2gZy_G6CXBLWpuOs9LxhNudY1pIeNbOFHVJ4g30pe9_WL9XHdCZTmkR6aJVvqxTP2aUImvk1GwVVNS1K4xy_T6tySGWk5-V7b3VGiMAYWZV-Q0l5YSzUg/Mdg0NRbjDeCJyMBP3pJ93tHwvpBMH-Wv/15)
Hi Friend, Christmas is not just a time to celebrate, it's a time to remember why we needed saving. Jesus didn't come to start a holiday. He came to rescue us from sin and bring us back to God. That's what this season is really about, a divine rescue mission. And at PreBorn!, we've created a simple devotional to help you prepare your heart with that truth at the center. [**It 's called ****_Unto Us a Child Is Born: 12 Scriptures to Prepare Your Heart for Christmas._**](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/KOufO8NH3x1Rcq6JjnbnmECTzM0Wit_jXaLKtOgrItPW_I6PjNJXWeI7upDku_pjHdn4BQtqW5-6cKnec3vaovsyZNNLCrik9ouAqSyCRjSZ22c_9PcazH6BcOa-wsCvmbP6pL7Gpe07b2AMV0R29WUb0U_m2TpXalvAbsd7nZUB2aHMz8yHLpUdkn8t2nI5OZliPl5bz3uIfR2MicJniKQHLGNOLbLwRwRd9-24kCuxyXsoZuJDD9vxa7IjoM-DDM8AMY7Di8VcD3UcWkBdkbla6VzUVU7YUVMSlQW99bRRGTGD96wkOmpITVenE-f4fWuQNDowyccas89NLqtQ-lXli7B4qP9Uirf-4F4DralE_dR3rSlkTqW6LrBC1R_qltqquJWvamijVDR45k86NmXV9Q/D6yFVwQeROgNsnChZmIGx822uIlrGUgW/15) This FREE 12-day devotional will walk you through:
---
* Full Scripture passages that tell the Christmas story
* Short reflections from Dan Steiner to help you respond in faith
* Simple, heartfelt prayers to center your heart on Christ

---
[](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/ojVmSc_eYTjh3lUDde0rjjjETxzTsUzDPp7gPtZZCyxTojZ-mntC_FrYTYQcBbXGVdvUS6z8n2PN2mrE5jMBqNiHtGGjCpbAbWiap7FUvDJ8Ve2Eng9KIyk4OBbeXf-pvvt6oAnp8tet-Cje9jSHHJ0TlYkL4WLDVBX9HMtXvg53yDkcc9a_ZLgBGgLFyOwy9xO0C4KCHux76VnFVwaPeAx-XxmPsiF916lVL9LZFQ7X6JGK1LEPKZ5nxgZRj8X1VbFV0XuwtBHLAl5oGdj1CZbdJVlqxaZuDtf7fSrSb-yd1zRggBJMZAwE9TvDz_0LiLPpS8MHKsA4mdcd1211Rb7dnn-uyyhXUjkV5BonpraWJ8gBXWCg4Wti3-LtBXpwZsf-V2D5-39diX6PTIREZfHKVg/4qgoTZ_trz3OFcK9vcDr7PR6B8sbknMy/15)
---
Whether your December is busy or quiet, we hope this helps you focus on what matters most: the miracle of Christ's coming for you. You can get your copy here: **[https://resources.preborn.com/unto-us-a-child-is-born](http://links.newsletter.conservativedirect.com/s/c/fN5Htku1v8qt3ywOI3fXbtyj39zoJOyaLbSZ1g2FUgf3kio1t9CiEChLhQCfwM8t3pgqeplN2ucV7HxntPISkr_7bGuUBgjm_N2x48dXBLn4_uK1jgy9tlwVfeu9YRnMoAfnGsy46q9hsCN-AYi1hNMwUvRIFCzSflXBjCYl-6nzocC9SL0bS_hwk8Q1J1N4H37pm34hs8GZg2hsGGaNZkEdzA61JD3FVWV_UfyvXjJ89K2LFeBLBBhtAzL5JpShry4ycYG99R1bo4ls3FMsfOkKlpVVIvoXVGcTtGAEGTUdDKA37tEzx9Pq1ykvKYxFSbgJAaSnseF1VCgXlfyaG4tfWZecWnVoZ23TADHJVO-_y8wDS8tQmvRvYpqxgKf1RAZ8apoTFhK3jpvjTf9XX4u8Cg/YSY6rnC0vDVUi-4jHAMy3NnP7jGuokEA/15)**

We pray this resource will help focus your mind and renew your joy in Christ this Christmas season! Merry Christmas,

The PreBorn! Team
---

name: Joe Jaworski
subject: BREAKING: Poll Shows Jaworski Ahead
disclaimer_text: paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign
body:
A new poll from the Barbara Jordan Public Policy Research & Survey Center at Texas Southern University shows our campaign leading the race for TXAG | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/jaworskifortexas.com/last-night-14191663?e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | [ ](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=1b4e64bae1&e=5d8d80652b)
---
| | , I want you to hear this directly from me. **A new poll from the Barbara Jordan Public Policy Research and Survey Center at Texas Southern University shows our campaign leading the race for Texas Attorney General.** That’s real momentum. And it’s no accident. It’s the result of showing up across Texas, telling the truth, and building a campaign rooted in integrity instead of fear. Texans are responding because they’re ready to move away from MAGA cruelty and toward serious, honest leadership. [](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=8bf08db826&e=5d8d80652b) But, I also want to be just as clear about what this moment means. This race is close. A two-point lead is not a victory lap, it’s a signal that what we do next matters enormously. Momentum only lasts if we fuel it, and the next phase of this campaign will determine whether we can turn this lead into a decisive win on March 3. That means expanding our outreach, staying visible statewide, and competing every single day. And that takes resources. [**If you believe in what we’re building, I’m asking you to help right now. Please make a contribution today to help us protect this lead and keep moving forward.**](https://jaworskifortexas.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=2e1f50319ad85f5f9e5c11ac9&id=bcff2596e0&e=5d8d80652b) We’re proving that Texas Democrats are ready for change. Now we have to finish the job, together. Onward!

**
**

clean_body: A new poll from the Barbara Jordan Public Policy Research & Survey Center at Texas Southern University shows our campaign leading the race for TXAG | | | | | | | , I want you to hear this directly from me. A new poll from the Barbara Jordan Public Policy Research and Survey Center at Texas Southern University shows our campaign leading the race for Texas Attorney General. That’s real momentum. And it’s no accident. It’s the result of showing up across Texas, telling the truth, and building a campaign rooted in integrity instead of fear. Texans are responding because they’re ready to move away from MAGA cruelty and toward serious, honest leadership. But, I also want to be just as clear about what this moment means. This race is close. A two-point lead is not a victory lap, it’s a signal that what we do next matters enormously. Momentum only lasts if we fuel it, and the next phase of this campaign will determine whether we can turn this lead into a decisive win on March 3. That means expanding our outreach, staying visible statewide, and competing every single day. And that takes resources. We’re proving that Texas Democrats are ready for change. Now we have to finish the job, together. Onward! | "DONATE" | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | mailto:info@jaworskifortexas.com | | Pol adv. paid for by Joe Jaworski Campaign Our mailing address is: Jaworski for Texas 2228 Mechanic St Ste 307 Galveston, TX 77550-1881 Want to change how you receive these emails? 🥐 🍭🍗🍬🍾🥧
Sour cream vs. applesauce with latkes.
Latkes vs. hamantaschen. _(This one is actually Phil’s family’s biggest friendly debate!)_
Mashed potatoes vs. homemade pumpkin pie.
No matter your background, faith, or practices, this time of year is full of tasty seasonal treats. Our campaign HQ team always looks forward to a whole cornucopia of delicious food as the days get shorter and colder.
For some of us, the end of the year is a time of plenty. For too many, that’s not the case. No Coloradan should ever go hungry, regardless of the season, but especially as fall turns to winter, no one in our state should be left struggling to meet this basic need. **That’s why Phil’s office has been fighting for months — and is continuing to fight — to protect SNAP benefits for the 600,000 Coloradans (half of whom are children) who depend on these programs to meet their basic needs.**
**[Friend, as your governor, Phil will never stop fighting for a Colorado where everyone can get their basic needs met. With so many holidays just around the corner, and so much to enjoy, we’re curious: is your favorite holiday food tradition sweet or savory?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121479432/589591918/1108432072?refcode=ea.a34cad64-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ms=ea.a34cad64-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS84MTM2MiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMzRjYWQ2NC04YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=S7I6S5mzgM42vh6vjENmiip9jplmNE-8GXoWDfbd4hw=&emci=5d0277a9-79db-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=a34cad64-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=51844520)**

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: Senate Republicans rejected another plan to extend the expiring ACA subsidies
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
Republicans have failed the American people again.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121479601/589592448/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNDRjYWQ2NC04YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BoHFWROWXGBDmERjqcLKhEAiVWq1Q9sEvXV5dbDuqvE=&emci=bacb832a-7fdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=a44cad64-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7731215)
---

| | Republicans have once again failed the American people by refusing to protect affordable health care. **Last week, Senate Republicans voted down a plan to extend ACA subsidies, even as premiums are set to spike in just days.** All Democrats voted for the bill to extend the credits that help more than 20 million Americans afford health insurance. But almost all Republicans voted against it. Health care in America is already far too expensive, and Republicans are making the problem even worse. Despite Trump claiming for years that he was working on one, he still doesn't have a plan — not to lower premiums, not to protect coverage, not to make care affordable. **The Trump and Republican agenda is to make families sicker and poorer.** I'm fighting to bring down the cost of health care for families in Oregon and across the country. Health care is a fundamental right — and for it to be a right, it has to be high quality and affordable. [**Add your name to support affordable health care for every American.**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121479602/589592449/-1447538199?contactdata=xiRw9lYmgZkJLxRpzlhN8MZcNxfCYwtF1QH5N1X0W3TivPcq1j0M7fL3LB78r13DGsak3cE4vOA5U8lpkwVJr5pPDw3Mgs4ixFP58aHiH5f60Rer/QLls0REVVSRBrXTMqUoEjrKGZgYINK1qF9WDA==&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNDRjYWQ2NC04YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BoHFWROWXGBDmERjqcLKhEAiVWq1Q9sEvXV5dbDuqvE=&emci=bacb832a-7fdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=a44cad64-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7731215) [Sign the Petition](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121479603/589592450/-1447538199?contactdata=xiRw9lYmgZkJLxRpzlhN8MZcNxfCYwtF1QH5N1X0W3TivPcq1j0M7fL3LB78r13DGsak3cE4vOA5U8lpkwVJr5pPDw3Mgs4ixFP58aHiH5f60Rer/QLls0REVVSRBrXTMqUoEjrKGZgYINK1qF9WDA==&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhNDRjYWQ2NC04YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=BoHFWROWXGBDmERjqcLKhEAiVWq1Q9sEvXV5dbDuqvE=&emci=bacb832a-7fdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=a44cad64-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=7731215) Onward! _Jeff_ | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution to Jeff Merkley instantly:

clean_body: Republicans have failed the American people again. | | | | Republicans have once again failed the American people by refusing to protect affordable health care. Last week, Senate Republicans voted down a plan to extend ACA subsidies, even as premiums are set to spike in just days. All Democrats voted for the bill to extend the credits that help more than 20 million Americans afford health insurance. But almost all Republicans voted against it. Health care in America is already far too expensive, and Republicans are making the problem even worse. Despite Trump claiming for years that he was working on one, he still doesn't have a plan — not to lower premiums, not to protect coverage, not to make care affordable. The Trump and Republican agenda is to make families sicker and poorer. I'm fighting to bring down the cost of health care for families in Oregon and across the country. Health care is a fundamental right — and for it to be a right, it has to be high quality and affordable. Onward! null: RFK Jr. has absolutely no business anywhere near our public health system. Here’s why:
**👉 **He _fired_ the CDC director and replaced key health leaders with anti-vaccine extremists.
💉 He _canceled_ vaccine campaigns and downplayed deadly measles outbreaks as cases surged across the country.
🧬 He _shut_ down critical mRNA vaccine research, halting work on pediatric cancer, HIV, and public health innovation.
😷 He’s _blamed childhood vaccines and prenatal Tylenol for autism_ – even as real experts call his claims junk science.
📉 And his agency laid off hundreds of CDC staffers, crippling our response to the respiratory illness season.
Enough is enough, null.
**This isn’t just incompetence. It’s an attack on our health, our safety, and our future. It’s why Haley Stevens introduced articles of impeachment against him.[_Now, we're calling on 100 respondents from 18326 to let us know what you think. Team, Why does Trump love Pete Hegseth so much? Because Hegseth lets Trump get away with everything. Hegseth is pretty much the parent who lets him eat ice cream for dinner every night. Hegseth injects politics into the Department of Defense, a place where loyalty to our country, not to one man or political party, should be the priority. Hegseth’s also a fellow perpetrator of sexual assault. He has no moral compass, no guiding principles, no loyalty to the Constitution, only to whoever hands him a paycheck or pats him on the back. [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121479789/589592882/-445823664?hl=en&refcode=251217_tm_trumpheg&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS83NDc1OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5NDY2MmRhYS04YmRiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=FNMzQ0eXcELnnjwYmLtwzHmBuN32xcBYMw3gmy-Br7c=&emci=822b56d6-52db-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&emdi=94662daa-8bdb-f011-8195-000d3a1d58aa&ceid=194769) Trump rewards that kind of corruption. Because when you have no values, loyalty becomes a transaction. This is what happens when the people in power stop serving the American people and start serving each other. name: Gretchen Whitmer
subject: Seat 218
body:
|
#
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121479842/589593082/-185804085?refcode=em-12.17.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NjAyNCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJiYjZiNDgxMi04Y2RiLWYwMTEtODE5NS0wMDBkM2ExZDU4YWEiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=aZudjAKjUEjsYM1gAL_OsWbdKmOgOETqkGfX5tZecTU=) Hi friend, It’s Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, reaching out on behalf of my friend Kristen McDonald Rivet. Kristen is Michigan’s only Frontline Democratic Member of Congress, and she represents _Cook Political_ ’s “Seat 218,” meaning this district is the tipping point for control of the U.S. House. Simply put, we need your help to get her re-elected. National Republican super PACs have already poured nearly **$1 million** into misleading, dishonest attack ads against Kristen. They’re doubling down because if they flip MI-08, they’ll surely win the House in 2026. **They’re going after Kristen because she’s effective.** Kristen has spent her entire career fighting for kids and working families – from expanding early childhood education to making health care more affordable and cutting taxes for the middle class. Now, in Congress, she’s one of the loudest voices pushing back against Republican handouts for billionaires over our health care and food for hungry families. But the more effective Kristen is, the harder the GOP works to defeat her. They know MI-08 could decide the fate of the US House. That’s how high the stakes are. [**So friend, if you’re able, I hope you’ll pitch in to help Kristen fight back and keep MI-08 blue. name: Jazz Lewis
subject: Thank You for the Honor of a Lifetime — And What Comes Next
body:
https://www.jazzlewis.com/ [https://www.jazzlewis.com/]
Dear Friends, Neighbors, and Supporters,
I write today with deep gratitude and reflection, and with an excitement about the future that I can feel in my bones. After nearly a decade of serving you in the Maryland House of Delegates, effective January 5, 2026, I will be transitioning out of the legislature to begin a new role in the senior leadership at the University of Maryland, College Park.
This was not a decision I made lightly. Serving my home, the people, and communities of Legislative District 24 and Prince George’s County has been the honor of my life. Being a young man whose family wasn’t from Maryland, but chose this community, meant that our neighborhood, recreational teams, our church, and our public schools were the anchor for me and my family. I have so much to be thankful for, and I led with a mindset of trying to be a “hometown hero” for those who never saw themselves in some of the rooms God has graced me with the opportunity to enter. Together, we have built a record of service, legislative achievement, and community investment that will stand the test of time. I am profoundly grateful for every resident who trusted me with their hopes, their challenges, and their belief that public service can still make a difference.
As I step into this next chapter, still rooted here in Prince George’s County, still committed to the public good, I want to reflect on what we built together, what this moment means, and why I believe with absolute confidence that the best is yet to come .
A Record Built Together
When I first entered the General Assembly, I often said my values were shaped by growing up in a working-class family right here in Prince George’s County, a family that taught me to leave people, places, and institutions better than I found them. That commitment guided me in Annapolis, and together we delivered real progress.
Transformational Legislation
Across the years, we confronted some of the toughest issues facing our state with courage, creativity, and an unwavering belief that Maryland can lead the nation when we choose compassion and common sense.
From the early days, we fought for justice. In 2021, we passed the Juvenile Restoration Act , aligning Maryland with constitutional standards and giving people who made bad decisions early in life a second chance at life after demonstrating rehabilitation, delivering a landmark victory for fairness and humanity.
We also established Health Equity Resource Communities , securing $22 million annually so that underserved neighborhoods across the state could finally receive the healthcare providers and care they deserve.
As the years progressed, our agenda expanded, from workforce protections and transparency reforms to technology governance and children’s online safety. In 2024, we passed major legislation like the Artificial Intelligence Governance Act to set guardrails for the emerging technologies reshaping our daily lives, ensuring that Maryland protects civil rights while embracing innovation.
We advanced economic development, public safety reform, consumer protection, and higher-education fairness. In 2023, HB 4 banned legacy and donor preference in college admissions, leveling the playing field for every Maryland student, no matter their family wealth or connections. We made historic investments in our schools and students. Through the Blueprint for Maryland’s Future we continue to make great strides in improving the education our children receive in every jurisdiction of this great state.
We were able to make union dues tax-deductible after the Trump Administration took that ability away from our hard-working families at the federal level. By acting, we put more money into the pockets of those who built our state and make our economy run. Union jobs are some of the best that our state has, offering the chance at the American dream and I was honored to continue to stand alongside working Marylanders once again.
In 2023, we passed the Public Employee Relations Act , which streamlined how our state government settles disputes with state employees ensuring that problems are settled fairly and quickly and that our state workforce can focus on providing the services that Marylanders depend on every day.
We tackled criminal justice reform by passing numerous bills to ensure that equity is at the center of our legal system and that every Marylander is able to entrust that our law enforcement and court system is fair and equitable and that everyone is held accountable fully and fairly. From ensuring that all of our officers have body-worn cameras to making sure we follow best practices, we have made Maryland safer and fairer for all its citizens. We also made our legislative branch more equitable by introducing racial impact analysis so that we can study the impacts that different pieces of legislation will have on Marylanders of color and across all communities.
And just last year, we passed the Protect Our Federal Workers Act, which declared that Maryland was not going to stand by while the second Trump Administration illegally fired thousands of civil servants in Maryland and beyond. It offered loans to those impacted workers, allowed our state attorney general to sue the federal government when it fired workers illegally, and ensured we fought back when workers needed us most.
From reentry support for citizens leaving incarceration and small-business growth to modernizing our transportation systems and uplifting library workers, nearly every major issue facing Maryland families found voice and momentum through our work together.
Leading Through Historic Times
When I was appointed House Majority Whip , the youngest to hold the position, I took on the responsibility of ensuring our Caucus could advance bold legislation, from reproductive rights to vocational service-year programs, from cannabis reform to education funding, from anti-poverty tax credits to public safety. Each session, we moved together with discipline and purpose, delivering results for families who needed a government that worked for them.
I am grateful to my colleagues who believed in me and to every Marylander who deserved our very best, every single day.
A Legacy of Investment in Prince George’s County
Service is not only measured in the bills we pass, but in the resources we bring home to strengthen our communities. Here again, WE delivered.
Over the years, Prince George’s County secured hundreds of millions, indeed billions, of dollars in state capital investments, touching virtually every corner of the County and District 24.
From 2019 onward, we brought home funding for:
* Central Avenue and Blue Line Corridor revitalization including more than $400 million for infrastructure, placemaking, economic development, and an entertainment district that will anchor the County for a generation.
* Transit-oriented development at Capitol Heights, New Carrollton, and Largo metro stations.
* Healthcare expansions , including urgent care, oncology, and hospital modernization across the region at University of Maryland Capital Region Hospital, Doctors Community Hospital, MedStar Southern Maryland, and Adventist HealthCare Fort Washington Medical Center.
* Parks, playgrounds, and community centers from Hill Road to Highland Gardens, Lake Arbor to Glenn Dale, ensuring our families have safe, vibrant places to gather.
* Housing and neighborhood stabilization , including multifamily developments, youth career centers, and shelter investments.
Support for Bowie State University, the University of Maryland, College Park, the University of Maryland Global Campus, and Prince George’s Community College , almost every year of my service through new academic and research buildings, innovation hubs such as supporting Ion Q and the quantum initiatives, to student housing and much more.
We helped bring home everything from bike lanes and business districts to mental health facilities and funding to incentivize grocery stores locating in the inner beltway. Some appropriations were large, $1.6 billion for our school system in 2024 alone. Others were small but indispensable: the American Legion post renovations, community boxing gyms, arts programs, job training expansions, and more.
These were investments in our future .
A Commitment to Service That Continues
Although my chapter in the legislature is ending, my service to the people of Maryland, and especially to Prince George’s County, continues.
At the University of Maryland, I will serve an institution that shaped me into the leader I am today. This campus changed my trajectory, just as it continues to transform the lives of tens of thousands of Marylanders each year. In this role, I will help secure resources, partnerships, and policy support to keep our flagship strong, innovative, and accessible to all. Being a native of Prince George’s County, I look forward to continuing to deepen the University’s connection to the county, and leveraging our institution to promote the economy and social fabric of our county and state.
I take this step because I believe deeply in the power of institutions to lift people up. And few institutions do more for the future of Maryland than our state’s flagship university.
Why I Am Filled With Hope
Change always carries emotion. Leaving the legislature brings gratitude, nostalgia, and pride, but most of all, a sense of hope.
Hope because of what we built together.
Hope because of the leaders we uplifted and the lives we changed.
Hope because the foundations we laid, from justice reform to economic revitalization, from housing to healthcare, from education to transportation, will outlast any one individual.
And hope because I know the people of Prince George’s County, and of District 24 in particular, are resilient, visionary, and unstoppable.
My focus is and will always be on how to find the best way to serve others, as I am called to do in my faith, and as I was raised to do by my family. And service does not end because a role ends. I am still here. I am still committed. I am still working for you.
Thank You, for Everything
Thank you to every resident who called, emailed, marched, testified, challenged me, encouraged me, or simply believed in me. Thank you to the community leaders, advocates, union members, clergy, non-profits, civic associations, teachers, parents, students, and local officials who shaped our work.
Thank you to my colleagues in the General Assembly who trusted me to help lead our Caucus through historic legislative sessions. In particular, I want to thank Speaker Adrienne Jones for encouraging my development and always being open to my voice in leadership decisions, even as I was always the youngest voice in that room. I want to thank Senator Joanne C Benson for supporting me throughout my entire elected career. If I had not been volun-told to run for office by her, I don’t know if I would have ever taken the step to run. I thank God she did, because it's transformed my life and watching her commitment to our district has inspired me to work smarter and longer, everyday. I also want to give a special thank you to my mentor, Congressman Steny Hoyer. Being mentored by him, watching how he handles situations, builds consensus, nurtures staff talent, maintains relationships, and much more has influenced me not just professionally, but personally. My favorite lesson from him was never to live your life looking through your rear-view mirror, because you never know when you will need someone in the future. So be good to people, and they will be good to you.
Thank you to my amazing staff and interns, past and present, who brought passion, innovation, and dedication to serving the people of Maryland. I will particularly shout out my Chiefs of Staff over the years, Ms Faye Highsmith, Jasmine Sneed, Jordan Riddick, and Henry Snurr. The hours were long but I could never have done it without you.
Thank you to my family, my wife Aurin, and our children Miguel and Aurely, who sacrificed so I could serve. They are my priority, my grounding, and my purpose, and I’m looking forward to more time with them as I get acclimated to the new role.
And thank you to the people of District 24, who took a chance on a young man from Springdale, Maryland who wanted nothing more than to serve his community.
My heart is full. My gratitude is endless.
The work continues.
And the best is yet to come .
With deep respect and appreciation,
Jazz M. Lewis
Delegate, District 24 (until January 5, 2026)
Majority Whip, Maryland House of Delegates
Incoming Director of State Government Relations
University of Maryland, College Park

Bethesda, MD 20827
United States

clean_body: [] Dear Friends, Neighbors, and Supporters, I write today with deep gratitude and reflection, and with an excitement about the future that I can feel in my bones. After nearly a decade of serving you in the Maryland House of Delegates, effective January 5, 2026, I will be transitioning out of the legislature to begin a new role in the senior leadership at the University of Maryland, College Park. This was not a decision I made lightly. Serving my home, the people, and communities of Legislative District 24 and Prince George’s County has been the honor of my life. Being a young man whose family wasn’t from Maryland, but chose this community, meant that our neighborhood, recreational teams, our church, and our public schools were the anchor for me and my family. I have so much to be thankful for, and I led with a mindset of trying to be a “hometown hero” for those who never saw themselves in some of the rooms God has graced me with the opportunity to enter. Together, we have built a record of service, legislative achievement, and community investment that will stand the test of time. I am profoundly grateful for every resident who trusted me with their hopes, their challenges, and their belief that public service can still make a difference. As I step into this next chapter, still rooted here in Prince George’s County, still committed to the public good, I want to reflect on what we built together, what this moment means, and why I believe with absolute confidence that the best is yet to come . A Record Built Together When I first entered the General Assembly, I often said my values were shaped by growing up in a working-class family right here in Prince George’s County, a family that taught me to leave people, places, and institutions better than I found them. That commitment guided me in Annapolis, and together we delivered real progress. Transformational Legislation Across the years, we confronted some of the toughest issues facing our state with courage, creativity, and an unwavering belief that Maryland can lead the nation when we choose compassion and common sense. From the early days, we fought for justice. In 2021, we passed the Juvenile Restoration Act , aligning Maryland with constitutional standards and giving people who made bad decisions early in life a second chance at life after demonstrating rehabilitation, delivering a landmark victory for fairness and humanity. We also established Health Equity Resource Communities , securing $22 million annually so that underserved neighborhoods across the state could finally receive the healthcare providers and care they deserve. As the years progressed, our agenda expanded, from workforce protections and transparency reforms to technology governance and children’s online safety. In 2024, we passed major legislation like the Artificial Intelligence Governance Act to set guardrails for the emerging technologies reshaping our daily lives, ensuring that Maryland protects civil rights while embracing innovation. We advanced economic development, public safety reform, consumer protection, and higher-education fairness. In 2023, HB 4 banned legacy and donor preference in college admissions, leveling the playing field for every Maryland student, no matter their family wealth or connections. We made historic investments in our schools and students. Through the Blueprint for Maryland’s Future we continue to make great strides in improving the education our children receive in every jurisdiction of this great state. We were able to make union dues tax-deductible after the Trump Administration took that ability away from our hard-working families at the federal level. By acting, we put more money into the pockets of those who built our state and make our economy run. Union jobs are some of the best that our state has, offering the chance at the American dream and I was honored to continue to stand alongside working Marylanders once again. In 2023, we passed the Public Employee Relations Act , which streamlined how our state government settles disputes with state employees ensuring that problems are settled fairly and quickly and that our state workforce can focus on providing the services that Marylanders depend on every day. We tackled criminal justice reform by passing numerous bills to ensure that equity is at the center of our legal system and that every Marylander is able to entrust that our law enforcement and court system is fair and equitable and that everyone is held accountable fully and fairly. From ensuring that all of our officers have body-worn cameras to making sure we follow best practices, we have made Maryland safer and fairer for all its citizens. We also made our legislative branch more equitable by introducing racial impact analysis so that we can study the impacts that different pieces of legislation will have on Marylanders of color and across all communities. And just last year, we passed the Protect Our Federal Workers Act, which declared that Maryland was not going to stand by while the second Trump Administration illegally fired thousands of civil servants in Maryland and beyond. It offered loans to those impacted workers, allowed our state attorney general to sue the federal government when it fired workers illegally, and ensured we fought back when workers needed us most. From reentry support for citizens leaving incarceration and small-business growth to modernizing our transportation systems and uplifting library workers, nearly every major issue facing Maryland families found voice and momentum through our work together. Leading Through Historic Times When I was appointed House Majority Whip , the youngest to hold the position, I took on the responsibility of ensuring our Caucus could advance bold legislation, from reproductive rights to vocational service-year programs, from cannabis reform to education funding, from anti-poverty tax credits to public safety. Each session, we moved together with discipline and purpose, delivering results for families who needed a government that worked for them. I am grateful to my colleagues who believed in me and to every Marylander who deserved our very best, every single day. A Legacy of Investment in Prince George’s County Service is not only measured in the bills we pass, but in the resources we bring home to strengthen our communities. Here again, WE delivered. Over the years, Prince George’s County secured hundreds of millions, indeed billions, of dollars in state capital investments, touching virtually every corner of the County and District 24. From 2019 onward, we brought home funding for: Central Avenue and Blue Line Corridor revitalization including more than $400 million for infrastructure, placemaking, economic development, and an entertainment district that will anchor the County for a generation. Transit-oriented development at Capitol Heights, New Carrollton, and Largo metro stations. Healthcare expansions , including urgent care, oncology, and hospital modernization across the region at University of Maryland Capital Region Hospital, Doctors Community Hospital, MedStar Southern Maryland, and Adventist HealthCare Fort Washington Medical Center. Parks, playgrounds, and community centers from Hill Road to Highland Gardens, Lake Arbor to Glenn Dale, ensuring our families have safe, vibrant places to gather. * Housing and neighborhood stabilization , including multifamily developments, youth career centers, and shelter investments. Support for Bowie State University, the University of Maryland, College Park, the University of Maryland Global Campus, and Prince George’s Community College , almost every year of my service through new academic and research buildings, innovation hubs such as supporting Ion Q and the quantum initiatives, to student housing and much more. We helped bring home everything from bike lanes and business districts to mental health facilities and funding to incentivize grocery stores locating in the inner beltway. Some appropriations were large, $1.6 billion for our school system in 2024 alone. Others were small but indispensable: the American Legion post renovations, community boxing gyms, arts programs, job training expansions, and more. These were investments in our future . A Commitment to Service That Continues Although my chapter in the legislature is ending, my service to the people of Maryland, and especially to Prince George’s County, continues. At the University of Maryland, I will serve an institution that shaped me into the leader I am today. This campus changed my trajectory, just as it continues to transform the lives of tens of thousands of Marylanders each year. In this role, I will help secure resources, partnerships, and policy support to keep our flagship strong, innovative, and accessible to all. Being a native of Prince George’s County, I look forward to continuing to deepen the University’s connection to the county, and leveraging our institution to promote the economy and social fabric of our county and state. I take this step because I believe deeply in the power of institutions to lift people up. And few institutions do more for the future of Maryland than our state’s flagship university. Why I Am Filled With Hope Change always carries emotion. Leaving the legislature brings gratitude, nostalgia, and pride, but most of all, a sense of hope. Hope because of what we built together. Hope because of the leaders we uplifted and the lives we changed. Hope because the foundations we laid, from justice reform to economic revitalization, from housing to healthcare, from education to transportation, will outlast any one individual. And hope because I know the people of Prince George’s County, and of District 24 in particular, are resilient, visionary, and unstoppable. My focus is and will always be on how to find the best way to serve others, as I am called to do in my faith, and as I was raised to do by my family. And service does not end because a role ends. I am still here. I am still committed. I am still working for you. Thank You, for Everything Thank you to every resident who called, emailed, marched, testified, challenged me, encouraged me, or simply believed in me. Thank you to the community leaders, advocates, union members, clergy, non-profits, civic associations, teachers, parents, students, and local officials who shaped our work. Thank you to my colleagues in the General Assembly who trusted me to help lead our Caucus through historic legislative sessions. In particular, I want to thank Speaker Adrienne Jones for encouraging my development and always being open to my voice in leadership decisions, even as I was always the youngest voice in that room. I want to thank Senator Joanne C Benson for supporting me throughout my entire elected career. If I had not been volun-told to run for office by her, I don’t know if I would have ever taken the step to run. I thank God she did, because it's transformed my life and watching her commitment to our district has inspired me to work smarter and longer, everyday. I also want to give a special thank you to my mentor, Congressman Steny Hoyer. Being mentored by him, watching how he handles situations, builds consensus, nurtures staff talent, maintains relationships, and much more has influenced me not just professionally, but personally. My favorite lesson from him was never to live your life looking through your rear-view mirror, because you never know when you will need someone in the future. So be good to people, and they will be good to you. Thank you to my amazing staff and interns, past and present, who brought passion, innovation, and dedication to serving the people of Maryland. I will particularly shout out my Chiefs of Staff over the years, Ms Faye Highsmith, Jasmine Sneed, Jordan Riddick, and Henry Snurr. The hours were long but I could never have done it without you. Thank you to my family, my wife Aurin, and our children Miguel and Aurely, who sacrificed so I could serve. They are my priority, my grounding, and my purpose, and I’m looking forward to more time with them as I get acclimated to the new role. And thank you to the people of District 24, who took a chance on a young man from Springdale, Maryland who wanted nothing more than to serve his community. My heart is full. My gratitude is endless. The work continues. And the best is yet to come . With deep respect and appreciation, Jazz M. body:
Plus, how you can help support our efforts fighting for fair maps
|
---

Peter,
The fair maps movement is doing a ton of work right now to ensure the actions of our government truly reflect the will of the people. We wanted to give you a quick update on where things stand:
**KEY UPDATES**
* On December 4, the Supreme Court sided with anti-democracy extremists, allowing **Texas** ’s racially gerrymandered map to stand. _The fair maps movement will remain in this fight to ensure that voters in Texas — and nationwide — have equal representation._
* The **Indiana** Senate voted on December 11 to reject one of the most egregiously gerrymandered maps in the country. _This is a testament to the work we’re doing to mobilize voters in defense of fair maps nationwide._
* **Missouri** voters submitted 300,000 citizen signatures to put the state’s congressional gerrymander on the ballot next year.

**ONGOING LITIGATION**
* The National Redistricting Action Fund and its affiliates are involved in cases in **5 states — Alabama, Georgia, Louisiana, Missouri, and Texas — concerning the makeup of up to 10 congressional districts and 7 legislative districts.**
* The National Redistricting Foundation is also involved in **2 cases defending the integrity of census data.**

**It’s your support that allows the fair maps movement to fight for fair representation and mobilize voters to push back when our rights are being threatened.[Donate today to help power the movement working to ensure every American has an equal say in our government.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/121480051/589593782/798345718?refcode=don_aotl_tier1_email_WeeklyDigest_email_20251217-AOTL-Tier1-WeeklyDigest_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251217-AOTL-Tier1-WeeklyDigest_&utm_campaign=WeeklyDigest&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk2IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjJlMDc0MGZiLThjZGItZjAxMS04MTk1LTAwMGQzYTFkNThhYSIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=Af89v5J8US3n8T0oYWdks_Mkiyzu_Y8ZczdCgGdJb5A=)**

It’s your support that allows the fair maps movement to fight for fair representation and mobilize voters to push back when our rights are being threatened.=** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Thanks so much, The Redistricting Action Team Text FAIR to 88228 to receive recurring updates from Redistricting Action by automated text message. Text HELP for help, STOP to end. Msg & Data rates may apply. and | PAID FOR BY THE NATIONAL REDISTRICTING ACTION FUND. Contributions or gifts to The National Redistricting Action Fund are not tax deductible. 712 H St NE, Suite #25, Washington, DC, 20002 This email was sent to:dpwillis67@gmail.com

Spotify. Facebook. Google. Uber. Apple. Amazon.

Fellow American, you've seen these companies share your highlights of 2025.

_Where you've been.
Your top played songs.
Where you've ordered food._

But this is a little different. I would like to hear from you about what Senate Republicans and President Trump accomplished this year since taking back the Senate majority.

Here's a reminder of some of our top achievements ([follow the link to let us know which one you think moved America back on track after four years of Biden/Harris](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=5699604bf06a495498abde138b021303&_e=TOwxoeQ0VB9nLC8NlUdeS2HRDvlaAuVbIfTNo0pEYmp7T6jUsAh_RM07GuA6s72tSErUAKMmCFUmironSRxA2D1L6QqMdjWBfddJeLjdETtqCeKjrbVP_1meQ8mAuag4VmBOfqJbQvwPcxBRLu2VZ35ZP-WaQlzadp4ClYQdr_DyPBj37sHZpxsQeSP3RJ_gZ6IixlW-pMppyqVeAmGBOmTpHaY0MCEOicLHkKazYk3wpCnO31HeGWxpixP1_IOknONId9q3rxVc6IxNrFfd1IZHR3pnzz6S4Mo8-WhAoD-Rt5CSRfP7vc5DExQtrn1Qvl-3LRESAmPmDBAQEY4q-qhmQ4wdMqG_cZ_jQQqyTQWW_1PAGIwytVaLR7vMrTboeLAq7pSBllNTSRGdRypuvv-8eDy0hA_E9wlUBW3wItbqgBU9bvrUVBf17PyVnH-kYOXtZ--fYpUsBUBiEqu2vt1hsQBwhOAXswSnWHla5pekXM3k-oBxzIgj4OenT7i5ESp4AJ4SV8n7uKnDn5ChDiESywoUqiGdn4F1Ubos1zfuz4b5276kyDiJ0mY8_G2NbYYXHvFDP7NYIxdIixgo5YvZbaZjGJnwmhLu2x4PokymTL63rL-4u3nMZ_SEshzA4OFTOtncOa5L9ZJJSIChB1VSGFuQCh4OvZ9SzYmjV7E6zJa-YlP6McpbG-7Jm422gUJKxouPsITOHKbh04LY_9Wwyy5GIhG1BVMpfQjSPTs3Ti0dTzcbOD7RTYONHycK)).

Conservative Wins in 2025:

**[STABILIZING BIDEN-FLATION](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=5699604bf06a495498abde138b021303&_e=TOwxoeQ0VB9nLC8NlUdeS2HRDvlaAuVbIfTNo0pEYmp7T6jUsAh_RM07GuA6s72tSErUAKMmCFUmironSRxA2D1L6QqMdjWBfddJeLjdETtqCeKjrbVP_1meQ8mAuag4VmBOfqJbQvwPcxBRLu2VZ35ZP-WaQlzadp4ClYQdr_DyPBj37sHZpxsQeSP3RJ_gZ6IixlW-pMppyqVeAmGBOmTpHaY0MCEOicLHkKazYk3wpCnO31HeGWxpixP1_IOknONId9q3rxVc6IxNrFfd1EOIhWepsSqMUacZEIpVkTTOhBNkd3CuHHVV2h-n3FKXCSNBT8Zudq__OMe_cKZlmkpiK86VeekK5n362EE5JkiXuVNtKQdte7iC6yQIxLLycTm-CUbtDhJxbBJBlv8d7x9V_3RODUbxxmuLEI4ormCUX1lyBADoVWJuk7RTWV9OCYrV4057DwJmaaLOB8PapsaJcrWGNXrC3PaVzONUyLA2dWb8ySe0Gy25LaJiV2u09QV754g9CouFVpPC4dtA62O2ayaPkxKRqtDZ55VYqOqcUCPfq5tFQqzR2qUcGI4rsBYId7UmKu0OTODowKH0olx65Sd89aS37Fs-b_0MNZ8jYHFa2vztZNbrySbfOt__jLnW-d7ecQdeYOuePpIjRaLFvfmiILYYpMy8-dlBGBtgyTj9ilqtxs8MRR8wBRTuoPnM5kNzMmTKHN72Q-M0b65D6tzXdwOpv49-bYgDRTaefiAYFqn_1xAdWubybatv)** Under President Trump and Senate Republicans, we added nearly 700,000 new jobs for Americans, while seeing an overall stabilization of food prices and actual reductions in gas prices.

**[ONE BIG BEAUTIFUL BILL](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=5699604bf06a495498abde138b021303&_e=TOwxoeQ0VB9nLC8NlUdeS2HRDvlaAuVbIfTNo0pEYmp7T6jUsAh_RM07GuA6s72tSErUAKMmCFUmironSRxA2D1L6QqMdjWBfddJeLjdETtqCeKjrbVP_1meQ8mAuag4VmBOfqJbQvwPcxBRLu2VZ35ZP-WaQlzadp4ClYQdr_DyPBj37sHZpxsQeSP3RJ_gZ6IixlW-pMppyqVeAmGBOmTpHaY0MCEOicLHkKazYk3wpCnO31HeGWxpixP1_IOknONId9q3rxVc6IxNrFfd1BzBHDIYcauQMlUZLswICJzsudLPcun-mRGIisVbIdr-rEg_V5FeBlhl10t2HSDnb3fYPGNFhrfH41JCLEK6w98LOrBW8bC6KOX38_pt4nmPPIURZFA67_E2WLLO6Ok8S_5grAG_8bacANRgX7eGGx4LneTy461AHF9BUVPMn4JpRNQ75alHmuS50wVaDK14bTLHx8pzPoZqYKWulyM_OgKqYvIYelRv_z8CnJ5jODrcx0jl_nMikNuuHqXgaxHbK54wclmLNXyaYetjWGoyhX2dJl4Tj90631KjNaGBXLUjlqR455X0ppnY5h6hOdRvwwjW-oSeLw2u95d8_RYGiGnRNAuLLCasfP8Sy9EoNG6bek1KJrPK0yrSCu6tZETT6e5-f9GmOmvfBuvSMEyTKlXSqu0A5zp0xD8E9G3tGi0evIIrFegrTwv0Z94xk8Czbi5rK_n_ST8I1cOwGF8K331YSAS5PaEgXpAceKQWFT2T)** I was proud to support the largest tax cut in history and deliver on our promise to support President Trump's America First agenda. It secures the border, strengthens our national security, unleashes American energy, reduces wasteful spending, and cuts taxes for working families AND seniors. We are better off because of it.

**[BORDER SECURITY](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=5699604bf06a495498abde138b021303&_e=TOwxoeQ0VB9nLC8NlUdeS2HRDvlaAuVbIfTNo0pEYmp7T6jUsAh_RM07GuA6s72tSErUAKMmCFUmironSRxA2D1L6QqMdjWBfddJeLjdETtqCeKjrbVP_1meQ8mAuag4VmBOfqJbQvwPcxBRLu2VZ35ZP-WaQlzadp4ClYQdr_DyPBj37sHZpxsQeSP3RJ_gZ6IixlW-pMppyqVeAmGBOmTpHaY0MCEOicLHkKazYk3wpCnO31HeGWxpixP1_IOknONId9q3rxVc6IxNrFfd1KUemU4sugyLZ5CXanah7AIdQojSNNKtoV40lCvi5GhvCwz-lCH2e-URRKHKLaNx5mr5TWJFfrDKXiSstEHuDxFX2Ch-NvL1wvidIoyn7CfSOcKDs1VgeEuvshkVyoqNKHPeT9Uz_ckNk8gefqovdRGkoZfddIcuVH-nsFQXmW2X1XdWEj-qxJajUJx6vdqUszRzP6jnaKqjCEa-n32L8wNCVknlh-9S3UwfuM1ZqtUWLemOhvOMU5FjSp3buwdz82o0wJ3h5vAdSpw6vAW19LwB4doL_fmIS85iV8gGjWQtXhUIkxcl_9W2xnzkT_8_e-fUC9SYHKDxoi4ffhUstLmF2YIQsJ4JORbvbZq20Pdhm2QhgU60lKbDiBwiQG_LZg0hcTVY68SuWr5ohxt1IID_MO-oFG3fXDEkSa4OSoyo5a61EDKbPQdOTapkWQOh46SV3GwXL7Gf3SH_MJjvZGZ2gz6YIhYJg13bCcYvGiNs)** We had record-low illegal border crossings in 2025. We kept our promises to keep dangerous drugs out of our country and protect our communities after years of open borders.

**[ENERGY INDEPENDENCE](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=5699604bf06a495498abde138b021303&_e=TOwxoeQ0VB9nLC8NlUdeS2HRDvlaAuVbIfTNo0pEYmp7T6jUsAh_RM07GuA6s72tSErUAKMmCFUmironSRxA2D1L6QqMdjWBfddJeLjdETtqCeKjrbVP_1meQ8mAuag4VmBOfqJbQvwPcxBRLu2VZ35ZP-WaQlzadp4ClYQdr_DyPBj37sHZpxsQeSP3RJ_gZ6IixlW-pMppyqVeAmGBOmTpHaY0MCEOicLHkKazYk3wpCnO31HeGWxpixP1_IOknONId9q3rxVc6IxNrFfd1JO8C0E0UuoAJSaHHa8wl0ChjuOZqTreDSgDwROdehR2QYJqiWMUMaAAE_vhU6r5seOxjQHfldrcetL_2oKtsCEvCdnb_u2sS4B_s97sxMIt2eXU2cJo6f7s-6TCu0Kvl7G2F-QvVRfyi7L1847j5ncJAzOKiw4Pj7YyWW1-hlxMJ-O__wACtdAQF8tEH7MCViQOuTUKf3GP35kPcUl5H7z_7SfwvYbMDyRfE69y3blHJzP3zrA_hMUb32eBly4S2dC1Xi3w8ex-ZCY4lfUboy43T_oTpEojtM3mPGtbgnFCPh26bMEpqrafj6BGODWJbfn7UpynmR2-Gvw04wdE4TKVRPsJudi0YnCt82Xgvt3G_zpuxeFxMRRcqjlRST6TMxX2v4EDRNFWt41ppoCLKe5dcvKZmARQPeOhZ8cK5j1WVuApE9JLBbFitgmUn2ARJdxMoOv2mg4lGjppD4q2jW4qrqKdMXOJUoY740Q-5l7A)** We finally have a Senate and a president who will unleash American energy independence, instead of pushing a radical environmental agenda. American energy ensures that we remain dominant on the world stage. But it doesn't end there.

**[Fellow American, what do you think was our greatest accomplishment in 2025?](http://links.action.supportstevedaines.com/u/click?_t=71faa67ca88f4c4b899697b6a4621901&_m=5699604bf06a495498abde138b021303&_e=TOwxoeQ0VB9nLC8NlUdeS2HRDvlaAuVbIfTNo0pEYmp7T6jUsAh_RM07GuA6s72tSErUAKMmCFUmironSRxA2D1L6QqMdjWBfddJeLjdETtqCeKjrbVP_1meQ8mAuag4VmBOfqJbQvwPcxBRLu2VZ35ZP-WaQlzadp4ClYQdr_DyPBj37sHZpxsQeSP3RJ_gZ6IixlW-pMppyqVeAmGBOmTpHaY0MCEOicLHkKazYk3wpCnO31HeGWxpixP1_IOknONId9q3rxVc6IxNrFfd1IbrG6q5Qi_WVgyU628V3YzeNEi-Sk6a3nApxQV-k1ZPlrKBGOaFwAT6AfTMtV1sOPlXjpOd6nVRhA9gyAl6XcI1t6e02LJqr8HRDPF5toGyQrpjlnOEv2dB-4PcRt-qsJYTItfLIaMWZI_RxwYEPpnOXpDvTjwP51OtIu-NRNuGDLV9iO487DYROTSoZiirAxpH4szbNiBcTn1IaTLJY-uWNV9ThxDYurfuX03uFEjlYX8LjDsHa8Fhi6JbVs0kju2g6zNkEMHCeKNltGTqGJVq6Xz1yooRNwCZ27tjFGGdHu48DGZww0vZ1c6WGxO-8jN9-4Tr4kSf4nBw2G0CIiG7MF2F7xO50oBGQvHvpMl-AlQEE3VrukRWEFaUd8q34BxRJXujB3hMSVzwWcKlLhW6JjRlVSGix2eSeDXRs30H5oVJ-mVp2qkElrtEp2BVhOflATnwmpICrLE8YX0X1M62g4UsM6x5Qn4iOUpOuHVL)**

clean_body: You've seen 2025 "wrapped" from Spotify, Facebook, Google, Uber, Apple, and Amazon. What about what WE accomplished! | Spotify. Facebook. Google. Uber. Apple. Amazon. Fellow American, you've seen these companies share your highlights of 2025. Where you've been. Your top played songs. Where you've ordered food. But this is a little different. I would like to hear from you about what Senate Republicans and President Trump accomplished this year since taking back the Senate majority. Here's a reminder of some of our top achievements (). Conservative Wins in 2025: Under President Trump and Senate Republicans, we added nearly 700,000 new jobs for Americans, while seeing an overall stabilization of food prices and actual reductions in gas prices. I was proud to support the largest tax cut in history and deliver on our promise to support President Trump's America First agenda. It secures the border, strengthens our national security, unleashes American energy, reduces wasteful spending, and cuts taxes for working families AND seniors. We are better off because of it. We had record-low illegal border crossings in 2025. We kept our promises to keep dangerous drugs out of our country and protect our communities after years of open borders. We finally have a Senate and a president who will unleash American energy independence, instead of pushing a radical environmental agenda. American energy ensures that we remain dominant on the world stage. But it doesn't end there. Please make sure to respond before the year is over. Steve Daines Steve Daines is a fifth-generation Montanan, life-long sportsman and father of four serving the people of Montana as their Senator. Steve is committed to always putting Montana first as he works to bring real change to Washington and fights for more jobs, less government and common sense solutions to get our country back on track. Senator Steve Daines | | Paid for by Steve Daines for Montana

name: Beto O'Rourke
subject: In 2025, we chose to fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
I want to tell you the action Powered by People took in 2025.
# [](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/43706?t=1&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
Remember Trump’s inauguration? It wasn't even a year ago. All of the corporate titans and tech billionaires in supplication, pledging their fealty... soon to be followed by the major law firms, the Ivy League universities, the media corporations, even Congress and the Supreme Court... really, anyone with any kind of conventional power was bending the knee to this would-be dictator... it was enough to make you puke... and to tempt us to despair.
But instead, we chose to fight.
**We decided that action would be the antidote to despair, the key to victory, and the one thing in our control that can make a difference.**
This is a long email (about 7 minutes) but I want to tell you the action our Powered by People team took in 2025 to meet the challenge. It made a huge difference, and I'm proud of what we accomplished.
**It started with listening.** Showing up in every part of Texas to bring people together for no-holds-barred town hall meetings. We wanted to hear what was on people’s minds. Yes, their concerns but also their solutions to overcoming the very real challenges we face. People—Democrats, Republicans, Independents, non-voters alike—having conversations eyeball to eyeball. No question off limits, everyone allowed a chance on the mic.
We held town halls in Republican strongholds, places Trump had just won with 70% of the vote. Amarillo, Wichita Falls, Tyler. We had them in suburbs too: Mansfield, Rosenberg (with my friend Tim Walz), Denton, Garland, Humble. And we did the same in bigger cities, from San Antonio and Laredo to Beaumont.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=3&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Town hall in Garland_
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=4&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Town hall in San Antonio_
Everyone in the room learned from everyone in the room. In this age of misinformation and state-sponsored propaganda, it brought us together on the same page of truth, ready to take action. One moment that stood out was when a woman in Humble [talked about](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/65216?t=5&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN) what it means to have her health care taken away.
**Those community meetings at the start of the year led to unprecedented action in the streets.** People coming out in force for the Hands Off march in April and the No Kings protests after that.
Texas showed up, with events in the places you might expect but also in small towns and rural communities. Powered by People organizers were there every step of the way, registering voters, signing up volunteers, and getting folks added to our network to help them turn out in the next election.
We protested Trump’s cuts to foreign aid, his destruction of public education, his “Big Beautiful Bill” that takes the ability to see a doctor away from millions of people while funneling billions to his ultra-rich donors, his attacks on our democracy and our Constitution, the masked, plain-clothes officers he has sweeping our neighbors off the street without a badge or a warrant or any semblance of due process.
And we took it directly to him. When Trump invited himself to give the commencement at the University of Alabama in May, we went to Tuscaloosa and joined thousands of students and community members to offer a powerful, positive alternative to his selfish and destructive authoritarianism.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=6&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Together in Alabama_
But as spring turned to summer, it became clear to all of us at Powered by People that protesting wouldn’t be enough. We couldn’t just be against things. We needed to be for something.
**So we hit the road to share a big, bold, positive, unapologetic vision for our future.** One that tells people exactly how Democrats will deliver when we win power, incorporating what we had heard during our town halls earlier in the year.
We held rallies in Fort Worth (with our friend Bernie) and Amarillo to lay it all out. A future where you can afford to see a doctor, buy a home, send your kids to world-class public schools, and only have to work one job. A future where we have a real democracy; one where we no longer turn our backs on our friends and allies around the world.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=7&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_With Bernie in Fort Worth_
We took this Texas vision to the rest of the country, holding town halls in North Carolina, Pennsylvania, Virginia, Maryland, Wisconsin, Indiana, Missouri, Nebraska, Oklahoma, and Louisiana. We even held a memorable one in Berlin (Germany—not Texas) with Democrats Abroad to make sure that U.S. citizens living outside the country can use their power to vote to help win critical elections. Thousands of people came out to these events, allowing us to make them into big organizing opportunities... registering folks to vote and plugging them in to volunteer shifts in their local communities.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=8&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Town hall in Oklahoma City_
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=9&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Town hall in Charlotte_
While traveling to other states this summer, we also had the opportunity to build our coalition and our movement. We worked directly with partner organizations that share our priority of bringing people together and taking action. That meant joining the summer meetings of the Young Democrats of America, the NAACP, UnidosUS, and Netroots Nation.
All of this work—the travel, the organizing, the coalition building—was put to the test when Donald Trump ordered Greg Abbott and Texas Republicans to give him five more seats in the House of Representatives... a direct attack on Texas and an effort to seize political power before the first votes of 2026 were even cast.
But we weren’t caught flat-footed. In fact, we chose to aggressively fight the President and take our case to the country.
**I couldn’t be more proud of how Powered by People stepped up to lead this redistricting fight. As Republicans began to redraw the maps in order to steal five Democratic seats, we took action.**
We protested at the Texas Capitol and testified inside of it. We hosted a 6,000 person rally in Austin (with our friends Joaquin Castro, Jasmine Crockett, James Talarico, Gina Hinojosa, and Greg Casar). We held organizing events in Houston, Abilene, and El Paso. We brought together thousands more in Fort Worth, reminding Trump and the rest of the country that, while the rich and powerful might be genuflecting in anticipatory obedience to the little dictator, in Texas, **our knees don’t bend.**
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=10&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Rally in Austin_
During TV interviews, podcasts, and out-of-state events, we connected the dots for our fellow Americans about why what happens in Texas matters to them (Trump is trying to steal these seats in order to hang on to power in the House because if he loses his majority, there will finally be a check on his lawlessness, accountability for his crimes and corruption, and real hope for a better future for this country). We got off the back foot and on to the front foot, leading the call for Democratic-controlled states to fight fire with fire by redrawing their maps to cancel out the Texas gerrymander (thank you, California). We would no longer await the punch thrown by these would-be fascists. Instead we would throw ours first and throw it harder.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=11&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Rally in Fort Worth_
But we didn’t just raise awareness, we raised resources too. When the heroic Texas Democrats left the state to break quorum and stop Trump’s power grab, we had their backs. And we continued to have their backs when they returned. Thanks to our grassroots supporters, Powered by People donated over $1 million to the Texas Legislative Black Caucus, the Texas House Democratic Caucus, and the Mexican American Legislative Caucus over the course of the special session.
**Turns out Republicans don’t like when we fight back.** For standing against Trump’s Texas gerrymander, Ken Paxton accused me of bribery, tried to shut down Powered by People entirely, and even asked a judge to imprison me.
Did that stop us?
No. We continued to fight aggressively, taking Paxton to court, and winning in front of an all Abbott-appointed appeals court. I’m sure Paxton knew he could never prove his lies, but I’m also just as sure that his intent was to bury us in legal action and bankrupt us by turning the full power of the State of Texas against us. What he didn’t count on was that we were never, ever going to give in. Though the months-long fight cost us more than $400,000 in legal fees, our supporters stayed with us through the thick of it and helped us emerge stronger than ever.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=12&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=13&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
**So strong that we decided to do what’s never been done before—work with allies across Texas to fill every single seat on the ballot with a Democrat.**
We hit the road to recruit Democratic candidates in every part of the state. We hosted candidate recruitment events in breweries and bars, in church meeting rooms and community centers, on campuses... anywhere we could gather people to remind them what’s at stake and what we can do to meet the moment. And we weren’t just in the districts that are safe for Democrats. We were in Pearland, Waco, College Station, McKinney, and Fort Worth just to name a few.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=14&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Candidate recruitment event in San Antonio_
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=15&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Candidate recruitment event in Waco_
And here’s the result: Democrats filed to run for 39 out of 39 Congressional seats, 16 out of 16 State Senate seats, and 150 out of 150 State House seats. We’ve scoured the state records (which have been digitized back to 1974) and we can’t find another year where Democrats fielded a candidate for every seat.
But we know that it’s not enough just to have candidates on the ballot. They have to have our support too. That’s why Powered by People is organizing on the ground and on college campuses throughout the state. We are registering voters, building new voter relationships and signing up volunteers on campuses like Rice, University of Houston, UT, Austin Community College, UTSA, UTEP, and El Paso Community College. And we know this work works; the young voters who we met and registered and stayed in touch with in 2024 turned out at a rate of 79.5% (versus 42% nationally and 37% in Texas).
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=16&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
_Voter registration event at University of Houston_
**Finally, there was everything that our Powered by People volunteers were doing when I wasn’t physically with them.** When the media wasn’t there. When it wasn’t being documented for Instagram or TikTok. It was the voters being registered every single day. The volunteer shifts happening in every part of the state. The organizing for down ballot races like Senate District 9. And the texts sent, phone calls made, community events attended, action taken.
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=17&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=18&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
[](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=19&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
We had a big year and we’re not slowing down now. We’re only going to turn it up in 2026. That's how we can win a U.S. Senate seat, critical Congressional races, state legislative seats... that's how we take back power, and use it to deliver for the people.
[**If you’re ready to build on this work in 2026, donate $3 today. Your donation will help us register voters, organize across Texas, and win races up and down the ballot.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&t=20&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
**[Donate $3](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251217.JhczGB&refcode=ema20251217-240544&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=39&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)**
Thanks for being in the fight with us.
Beto

[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/pbp-em-2023-footer?refcode=em20251217-240544-footer&t=40&refcode2=240544_444211_LOWmTN&akid=240544%2E444211%2ELOWmTN)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/update-contact-information/)

clean_body: I want to tell you the action Powered by People took in 2025. Remember Trump’s inauguration? It wasn't even a year ago. All of the corporate titans and tech billionaires in supplication, pledging their fealty... soon to be followed by the major law firms, the Ivy League universities, the media corporations, even Congress and the Supreme Court... really, anyone with any kind of conventional power was bending the knee to this would-be dictator... it was enough to make you puke... and to tempt us to despair. But instead, we chose to fight. We decided that action would be the antidote to despair, the key to victory, and the one thing in our control that can make a difference. This is a long email (about 7 minutes) but I want to tell you the action our Powered by People team took in 2025 to meet the challenge. It made a huge difference, and I'm proud of what we accomplished. It started with listening. Showing up in every part of Texas to bring people together for no-holds-barred town hall meetings. We wanted to hear what was on people’s minds. Yes, their concerns but also their solutions to overcoming the very real challenges we face. People—Democrats, Republicans, Independents, non-voters alike—having conversations eyeball to eyeball. No question off limits, everyone allowed a chance on the mic. We held town halls in Republican strongholds, places Trump had just won with 70% of the vote. Amarillo, Wichita Falls, Tyler. We had them in suburbs too: Mansfield, Rosenberg (with my friend Tim Walz), Denton, Garland, Humble. And we did the same in bigger cities, from San Antonio and Laredo to Beaumont. Town hall in Garland Town hall in San Antonio Everyone in the room learned from everyone in the room. In this age of misinformation and state-sponsored propaganda, it brought us together on the same page of truth, ready to take action. One moment that stood out was when a woman in Humble what it means to have her health care taken away. Those community meetings at the start of the year led to unprecedented action in the streets. People coming out in force for the Hands Off march in April and the No Kings protests after that. Texas showed up, with events in the places you might expect but also in small towns and rural communities. Powered by People organizers were there every step of the way, registering voters, signing up volunteers, and getting folks added to our network to help them turn out in the next election. We protested Trump’s cuts to foreign aid, his destruction of public education, his “Big Beautiful Bill” that takes the ability to see a doctor away from millions of people while funneling billions to his ultra-rich donors, his attacks on our democracy and our Constitution, the masked, plain-clothes officers he has sweeping our neighbors off the street without a badge or a warrant or any semblance of due process. And we took it directly to him. When Trump invited himself to give the commencement at the University of Alabama in May, we went to Tuscaloosa and joined thousands of students and community members to offer a powerful, positive alternative to his selfish and destructive authoritarianism. Together in Alabama But as spring turned to summer, it became clear to all of us at Powered by People that protesting wouldn’t be enough. We couldn’t just be against things. We needed to be for something. So we hit the road to share a big, bold, positive, unapologetic vision for our future. One that tells people exactly how Democrats will deliver when we win power, incorporating what we had heard during our town halls earlier in the year. We held rallies in Fort Worth (with our friend Bernie) and Amarillo to lay it all out. A future where you can afford to see a doctor, buy a home, send your kids to world-class public schools, and only have to work one job. A future where we have a real democracy; one where we no longer turn our backs on our friends and allies around the world. With Bernie in Fort Worth We took this Texas vision to the rest of the country, holding town halls in North Carolina, Pennsylvania, Virginia, Maryland, Wisconsin, Indiana, Missouri, Nebraska, Oklahoma, and Louisiana. We even held a memorable one in Berlin (Germany—not Texas) with Democrats Abroad to make sure that U.S. citizens living outside the country can use their power to vote to help win critical elections. Thousands of people came out to these events, allowing us to make them into big organizing opportunities... registering folks to vote and plugging them in to volunteer shifts in their local communities. We worked directly with partner organizations that share our priority of bringing people together and taking action. body:
We’re in danger of falling behind...
| **__A Personal Note from Elise Stefanik_ _**
---
Fellow Republican,
---
Our chances of beating Kathy Hochul depend on hitting _ALL_ of our fundraising benchmarks between now and Election Day.
---
But as it currently stands, we’re in danger of falling behind just days before our most important benchmark yet...
---
**_...our _FIRST_ and _ONLY_ end-of-year deadline!_**
---
Unless we raise another **_$19,055_** before tonight’s mid-month cutoff, we’ll officially enter the final stretch of 2025 behind budget!
---
With the holidays coming up, this would be the worst possible time to fall behind.
---
**To escape this looming crisis, I’m turning to my TOP grassroots supporters like _YOU_ for last-minute help reaching this vital goal. **
---
_As always: If you’ve given all that you can, or can’t afford to contribute right now, please simply disregard what I’m about to ask..._
---
But at this late hour, you’re truly my last hope, Fellow Republican.
---
[**So please, if you can, make a contribution before the mid-month deadline at 11:59 P.M. clean_body: We’re in danger of falling behind... | A Personal Note from Elise Stefanik Fellow Republican, Our chances of beating Kathy Hochul depend on hitting ALL of our fundraising benchmarks between now and Election Day. But as it currently stands, we’re in danger of falling behind just days before our most important benchmark yet... ...our FIRST and ONLY end-of-year deadline! Unless we raise another $19,055 before tonight’s mid-month cutoff, we’ll officially enter the final stretch of 2025 behind budget! With the holidays coming up, this would be the worst possible time to fall behind. To escape this looming crisis, I’m turning to my TOP grassroots supporters like YOU for last-minute help reaching this vital goal. As always: If you’ve given all that you can, or can’t afford to contribute right now, please simply disregard what I’m about to ask... But at this late hour, you’re truly my last hope, Fellow Republican. Thank you, Elise Stefanik Paid for by Elise for Governor PO Box 500 Glens Falls, NY 12801

name: Judicial Watch Corruption Chronicles
subject: Two Sanctuary States Release Nearly 9,000 Criminal Aliens with ICE Detainers Since January
body:
The Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal immigration has
made great strides in a short time, but over a dozen states are making
the effort more difficult by offering illegal aliens—even those
convicted of serious crimes—sanctuary.

[alt_text]

[*****]
December 17, 2025

[article-image]
[https://myjw.pr.judicialwatch.org/link.php?AGENCY=jw&M=27089954&N=78380&L=36675&F=T&drurl=aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuanVkaWNpYWx3YXRjaC5vcmcvc3RhdGVzLXJlbGVhc2UtOTAwMC1jcmltaW5hbC1hbGllbnMvP3V0bV9zb3VyY2U9ZGVwbG95ZXImdXRtX21lZGl1bT1lbWFpbCZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1BTWpjd09EazVOVFElM0QmdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWNvcnJ1cHRpb24rY2hyb25pY2xlcyZ1dG1fdGVybT1tZW1iZXJz&hash=ebd2cc5a4e9ebdbc0d4fbc8113882086c8941965f4750b5a0089ae3233787cba]

The Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal immigration has
made great strides in a short time, but over a dozen states are making
the effort more difficult by offering illegal aliens—even those
convicted of serious crimes—sanctuary. Just two of those states, New
York and Illinois, have released nearly 9,000 criminal aliens from
jails and prisons since January 20, defying Immigration and Customs
Enforcement (ICE) detainers to deport them. Among the offenders
protected by the sanctuary policies are murderers, terrorists, sexual
predators and kidnappers, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
confirms this month. Almost 7,000 criminal aliens flagged by ICE
were released from custody in the state of New York during the roughly
10-month period examined by DHS. Illinois released more than 1,700
criminal aliens from jails and prisons throughout the state rather
than turn them over to federal authorities for removal. The failure to
honor ICE detainers has led to “numerous illegal alien murderers,
rapists, and pedophiles being released” back into sanctuary
communities, Homeland Security officials have established.

clean_body: The Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal immigration has made great strides in a short time, but over a dozen states are making the effort more difficult by offering illegal aliens—even those convicted of serious crimes—sanctuary. [alt_text] [*****] December 17, 2025 [article-image] [] The Trump administration’s crackdown on illegal immigration has made great strides in a short time, but over a dozen states are making the effort more difficult by offering illegal aliens—even those convicted of serious crimes—sanctuary. Just two of those states, New York and Illinois, have released nearly 9,000 criminal aliens from jails and prisons since January 20, defying Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detainers to deport them. Among the offenders protected by the sanctuary policies are murderers, terrorists, sexual predators and kidnappers, the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) confirms this month. Almost 7,000 criminal aliens flagged by ICE were released from custody in the state of New York during the roughly 10-month period examined by DHS. Illinois released more than 1,700 criminal aliens from jails and prisons throughout the state rather than turn them over to federal authorities for removal. The failure to honor ICE detainers has led to “numerous illegal alien murderers, rapists, and pedophiles being released” back into sanctuary communities, Homeland Security officials have established. body:

From CNBC: “RFK Jr.’s vaccine panel weakens recommendation on hepatitis B shot for babies, scrapping universal guidance”

Derek, just a few months ago, RFK Jr. purged the CDC’s vaccine advisory committee – firing career scientists and public health experts and replacing them with political hacks.

Now, he’s coming for long-established vaccination guidelines, pushing debunked conspiracy theories that certain vaccines cause autism. They’re all flat-out false.

But hepatitis B won’t be the end of it. Other vaccines are next.

RFK Jr. has no medical training. No scientific expertise. But he has an agenda, and he’s willing to use lies, conspiracies, and misinformation to push it.

Americans are already paying the price. Measles is back. There’s a growing outbreak in South Carolina. And if this continues, diseases we often no longer have to worry about will come roaring back, too.

This isn’t politics. It’s public safety. He’s gambling with American lives.
